gmw_00000017.name: NGC 3147
gmw_00000017.overview: NGC 3147 is an isolated, face-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:2210.15376, arXiv:2307.01290] that hosts a low-luminosity Seyfert active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0712.0294, arXiv:2209.01807]. Although classified optically as non-barred [arXiv:0712.0294], near-infrared observations reveal a weak stellar bar that drives molecular gas inward toward the nucleus [arXiv:0712.0294, arXiv:0808.1186].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3147 appears X-ray-unabsorbed with a small iron line; although an obscured component can fit, Chandra variability favors direct nuclear viewing and possible BLR absence.

quotes.quote: NGC 3147 Our analysis of the XMM-Newton spectrum of NGC 3147 shows no absorption above the Galactic column, however it features a small (130 eV) iron K line at 6.4 keV. These results are in full agreement with those of Bianchi et al. (2008). used the small EW of the iron K line to argue against the Compton thick nature of this source and came to the conclusion that NGC 3147 has an intrinsic absence of a BLR. However, as we have shown in IRASF01475-0740, this conclusion is not necessarily robust. Fitting the Monte Carlo models to the spectrum once again shows that a very heavily obscured component is consistent with the spectrum.

Other evidence suggests, however, that this may not be the correct interpretation in this case. The 2ks Chandra observation of NGC 3147 shows that the measured flux of the nucleus is 2 times that measured by XMM-Newton indicating that it is variable in nature and hence it must be being observed directly, rather than in scattered light. Furthermore, the Chandra imaging reveals no evidence for extra-nuclear X-ray sources that could be producing the unabsorbed X-ray profile or the variability, and spectral extraction from the nuclear region in the Chandra image does not reveal a significantly harder X-ray spectrum then the larger XMM-Newton beam (Table 4).

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3147 as an isolated Seyfert 2 and low-rate true type 2 AGN candidate with point-like radio emission and a Compton-thin torus fit.

quotes.quote: NGC 3147* NGC 3147* is an isolated Seyfert 2 galaxy 1997Ho of Hubble morphological type SA(rs)bc. It was suggested as a true type 2 Seyfert candidate 2012bianchi , but later confirmed as an AGN accreting at low rate 2019bianchi . In the radio, it shows a point-like structure 2001ulvestad,2006krips .

At X-rays, 2017bianchi using NuSTAR data, shows that spectrum of NGC 3147* can be simply modelled by a power-law with a standard 1.7 and an iron emission line. These spectral properties, together with significant variability on time-scales as short as weeks, strongly support a line-of-sight free of absorption for this source. They suggested that NuSTAR data adds further evidence in favor of an X-ray spectrum completely unaffected by absorption, confirming NGC 3147 as one of the best cases of true Type 2 Seyfert galaxies, intrinsically characterized by the absence of a BLR.

We perform a simultaneous fit with this configuration of the data with PEXMON and found R _ f =3.99 _ 0.97 ^ 6.41 . We also fitted the data using borus02 model and found that the data is consistent with a Compton thin torus with density (N_ H,refl ) =23.27 _ 22.98 ^ 23.94 covering less than 89 of the sky.

sources.summary: The paper maps CO in NGC 3147, identifies a near-infrared stellar bar missed optically, and finds torques driving central molecular gas inward toward the low-luminosity Seyfert nucleus.

quotes.quote: We present CO(1-0) and CO(2-1) maps of the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 3147 of the NUclei of GAlaxies (NUGA) sample at 1.8 '' 1.6 '' and 1.4 '' 1.2 '' resolution respectively. Identifying the presence of a bar in NGC 3147 we also compute the gravity torques exerted from the stellar bar on the gas.

For the galaxy NGC 3147, after merging the interferometer data with the short spacing observations done with the IRAM-30m, we find that a nuclear peak and two molecular rings or pseudo-rings (at 10 ^ 2 kpc and 20 ^ 4 kpc, respectively) dominate the CO(1-0) map, while in the CO(2-1) we detect a peak of emission in the nucleus and a single and central ring at 10 ^ (Fig. fig1 ). Fig. fig2 shows that the molecular gas distribution has partial counterparts at wavelengths tracing the star formation, e.g. infrared emission (SPITZER) and ultraviolet one (GALEX). Using a NIR image obtained at the Canada-France-Hawaii Telescope, we identify the presence of a bar in NGC 3147 classified as non-barred galaxy in the optical. Computing the gravity torques from this stellar bar, we find that the gas in the main central CO pseudo-ring is subject to a dominant negative torque and loses angular momentum.
We conclude that some molecular gas is presently inflowing to the center, and probably feeding the low-luminosity Seyfert 2 observed in NGC 3147 see casasola07 .

sources.summary: The paper measures the distance to barred spiral Seyfert galaxy NGC 3147 using three Type Ia supernovae, adopting a SALT3 distance modulus consistent with BayeSN and Cepheid results.

quotes.quote: NGC 3147 is a barred spiral galaxy in the Draco constellation, at (2000.0) = 10^ h 16^ m 50^ s , (2000.0) = +73 ^ 24', being in the focus of interest due to its low-luminosity Type II Seyfert active galactic nucleus Panessa02 . Its heliocentric redshift is z=0.00934 Epinat08 . The historical distance estimations show a wide range between 27.7 Mpc from Tully-Fisher relation, Bottinelli84 and 55.2 Mpc based on the Type Ia SN 1972A, Parodi2000 , but the latest pre-SN 2021hpr results narrowed down to 39.3 Mpc SN 1997bq, Tully13 and 43.7 Mpc SN 2008fv, Biscardi12 .

The proximity and face-on orientation of NGC 3147 makes it a prominent host for discovering transient events. In the past half-century, this galaxy hosted six SNe, four of which were classified as Type Ia (the other two, SN 2006gi and SN 2021do, were Type Ib/ events).

We found that the results scatter between = 32.97 and 33.35 mag with a mean value of = 33.12 0.16 mag. This estimation for the distance of NGC 3147 is in good agreement with previous SN-based studies (see Table tab:distance_summary and Figure fig:dist_moduli ), and also marginally consistent with the most recent Cepheid-based distance _ Ceph =33.01 0.165 mag, Riess22 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3147 as a Seyfert 2 AGN host monitored by Swift, with 27 XRT/UVOT epochs and expected X-ray variability from its active nucleus but insignificant UV variability.

quotes.quote: NGC 3147 The weekly monitoring of NGC 3147, a Seyfert 2 galaxy hosting an AGN Ptak1996 , ranged from the Christmas day 2013 to the second week of July 2014. Good data have been collected for 27 epochs by both the XRT and the UVOT (see Table observations_NGC3147 ). We have detected one transient event (a known dwarf nova, located outside the target galaxy) in our UVOT dataset, at coordinates RA = 10:15:39.8, Dec = +73:26:05.0.

In the XRT data we found two variable sources. The first, as expected, is the active nucleus of the NGC 3147 (Fig. NGC3147_X_center ), NGC3147-I1. Its variability in the UV band is however not significant.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3147 as a high-mass Cepheid-calibrator host with three Type Ia supernova siblings whose consistent distances support a common host distance and Hubble-constant estimate.

quotes.quote: We present Young Supernova Experiment Pan-STARRS-1 grizy photometry of SN 2021hpr, the third spectroscopically confirmed SN Ia in the high-stellar-mass Cepheid-calibrator galaxy NGC 3147. We analyse NGC 3147's trio of SN Ia siblings: SNe 1997bq, 2008fv and 2021hpr, using a new version of the BayeSN model of SN Ia spectral-energy distributions, retrained simultaneously using optical-NIR BgVrizYJH (0.35--1.8 m) data. The distance estimates to each sibling are consistent, with a sample standard deviation 0.01 mag, much smaller than the total intrinsic scatter in the training sample: _0 0.09 mag.

Fitting the trio for a common dust law shape yields R_V=2.69 0.52 . Our work motivates future hierarchical modelling of more siblings, to tightly constrain their intrinsic scatter, and better understand SN-host correlations. Finally, we estimate the Hubble constant, using a Cepheid distance to NGC 3147, the siblings trio, and 109 Hubble flow ( 0.01 < z_ CMB < 0.08 ) SNe Ia; marginalising over the siblings' and population's intrinsic scatters, and the peculiar velocity dispersion, yields H_0=77.9 6.5 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3147 as a Seyfert 2 whose Chandra image is nucleus dominated, variable relative to earlier observations, and shows little X-ray absorption.

quotes.quote: NGC 3147 (S2) . --- This object was observed with in 1993 September and the observed flux was 1.6 10^ 12 in the 2--10 keV band (Ptak et al. 1996, 1999; Terashima et al. 2002b). Our image is dominated by the nucleus and shows that the off-nuclear source contribution within the beam is negligible. Therefore, a comparison between the observed flux ( 3.6 10^ 12 ), which is 2.3 times larger than that of , implies time variability providing additional evidence for the presence of an AGN.

However, the luminosity ratios / and / L_ O III 5007 suggest small obscuration (Terashima et al. 2002b). The observed variability supports the interpretation that the X-ray emission is not scattered emission from a heavily obscured nucleus. This galaxy is an example of a Seyfert 2 with only little absorption in the X-ray band.

sources.summary: The paper reports diffuse 347-GHz emission around NGC 3147 and identifies it as a distant, high-power LLAGN with a steep spectrum but no detectable jet.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, in the JCMT measurements at 347 GHz, we found significant elongated features in NGC 4258 (Doi et al. in preparation), NGC 4579, and NGC 5866, and a very diffuse structure in NGC 3147 (Fig. figure2 ). These features are consistent with the morphology of CO emissions that had been imaged with millimetric interferometers (NGC 4258, Helfer_etal.2003 ; NGC 4579, Sofue_etal.2003 ), with dust lane along the major axis of the host galaxy (NGC 5866, e.g., Malhotra_etal.2000 ), or with morphology of a low-frequency radio image (NGC 3147, Hummel_etal.1985 ). It is, therefore, plausible that most of the 347-GHz emission from these sources originates in stellar processes of star-forming regions, probably dust emission, associated with their host galaxies.

NGC 266 and NGC 3147 show steep spectra, but have no detectable jet in spite of their high core powers (Table table4 ). Since the two LLAGNs are the most two distant LLAGNs in our sample, it could be somewhat hard to reveal their putative small-scale jet structures. Their high radio powers might be also responsible for the dominance of such small-scale jets.

sources.summary: The paper fits three Swift spectra of NGC 3147 with a simple variable power law, finding little absorption and a photon index that decreases with luminosity.

quotes.quote: NGC 3147 Three flux-binned spectra of NGC 3147 were produced from the Swift data. We find the best-fitting model to the Swift data to be a simple power law for which was allowed to vary ( ^2_R = 0.73 ), Previous modelling of the spectrum of NGC 3147 has also shown a lack of any strong absorption e.g. Ptak1996 . As the Swift data are already slightly over-fitted by this model ( ^2_R < 1 ), we assume that more complex models are incorrect, despite producing lower ^2 values. We find similar values of the photon index to those found by Ptak1996 ( = 1.35-1.58 ). This model show a a reduction in with increasing intrinsic luminosity of the system.

sources.summary: The paper argues that NGC 3147 is not a true type 2 AGN: narrow-slit spectroscopy reveals an extremely broad H line consistent with a mildly relativistic thin accretion disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 3147 has been considered the best case of a true type 2 AGN: an unobscured AGN, based on the unabsorbed compact X-ray continuum, which lacks a broad line region (BLR). However, the very low luminosity of NGC 3147 implies a compact BLR, which produces very broad lines, hard to detect against the dominant background host galaxy. Narrow ( 0.1 0.1 ) slit \ spectroscopy allowed us to exclude most of the host galaxy light, and revealed an H line with an extremely broad base (FWZI 27 000 km s ^ 1 ). The line profile shows a steep cutoff blue wing and an extended red wing, which match the signature of a mildly relativistic thin accretion disk line profile. It is indeed well fit with a nearly face on thin disk, at i 23 , with an inner radius at 77 15 _g , which matches the prediction of 62^ +18 _ 14 _g from the R_ BLR L^ 1/2 relation.

sources.summary: The paper detects NGC 3147 in VLBA snapshot observations as an unresolved nucleus with high brightness temperature, and describes it as a true type 2 AGN with unabsorbed X-ray primary emission.

quotes.quote: For the additional snapshot observation sample, the nuclei for both the detections (NGC 3147 and NGC 3516) appear unresolved, but with follow up higher sensitivity deep observations, the core for NGC 2782 and NGC 4388 appear slightly elongated.

We find brightness temperatures of 10^ 7.6 K and 10^ 6.3 K from our two VLBA snapshot detections (NGC 3147 and NGC 3516), indicating either compact jet-associated or corona-associated synchrotron radiation as their likely dominant radio emission mechanism.

NGC 3147 (obsid: 60101032002): This AGN has been classified as a true type 2 AGN, i.e., a source with an optical spectrum without any broad line and at the same time without any significant obscuration. The \ spectrum confirms that the X-ray primary emission is unabsorbed and hence the genuine lack of a BLR, which is thought to be a consequence of the very low accretion rate Bianchi_2019MNRAS.488L...1B . Since the X-ray scaling method cannot be applied in this accretion regime, the small value derived (5.5 2.1) 10^5 M_ should not be considered reliable.

sources.summary: The paper confirms NGC 3147 as a low-luminosity, low-Eddington AGN with variable double-peaked disc-line emission and a subarcsecond optical-to-X-ray SED lacking a UV bump.

quotes.quote: A previous narrow-slit ( 0.1 arcsec) Hubble Space Telescope observation unveiled a broad relativistic H profile in NGC 3147, a low-luminosity ( L_ bol 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 ), low-Eddington ratio ( L_ bol /L_ Edd 10^ 4 ) active galactic nucleus (AGN), formerly believed to be a candidate true type 2 AGN intrinsically lacking the broad-line region. The new observations presented here confirm the double-peaked profile of the H line, which further shows variability both in flux and in the inner radius of the emitting disc with respect to the previous epoch. Similar disc line profiles are also found in prominent ultraviolet (UV) lines, in particular Ly and C iv . The new data also allow us to build a simultaneous subarcsec optical-to-X-ray spectral energy distribution of NGC 3147, which is characterized by the absence of a thermal UV bump, and an emission peak in the X-rays.

sources.summary: The paper maps molecular gas in NGC 3147, finding central CO emission, a ring and outer spiral, regular kinematics, a weak NIR bar, and modest inflow toward its Seyfert nucleus.

quotes.quote: We present ^ 12 CO(1-0) and ^ 12 CO(2-1) observations of the SA(rs)bc Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 3147, obtained with the IRAM interferometer at 1 9 1 6 and 1 6 1 4 resolutions, respectively.

Using a near-infrared (NIR) image obtained with adaptive optics at the Canada-France-Hawaii Telescope (CFHT), we identify a weak bar in NGC 3147, which is classified as non-barred galaxy in the optical.
The amount of inflowing gas in NGC 3147, quantified from the gravity torques computation, is modest but could still be sufficient to feed the Seyfert 2 nucleus at a rate of about 0.5 M _ yr ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC3147 as a well-established unabsorbed Seyfert-2 example, excluding it from stacked spectra because existing evidence indicates little or no absorption.

quotes.quote: In Fig. oiii there are 12 additional Seyfert-2 galaxies (NGC1058, NGC3147, NGCC3941, NGC3976, NGC4168, NGC4378, NGC4472, NGC4477, NGC4501,NGC4565,NGC4698 and NGC4725) with N_H less than 10 ^ 22 cm ^ 2 but an average F_ 2-10 keV /F_ [ OIII ] value. This behaviour is not unknown (e.g. Pappa et al 2001, Gliozzi, Sambruna Foschini 2007). In particular NGC3147 is a well established example, through simultaneous optical and X-ray observations, of a spectroscopically classified Seyfert-2 galaxy with very little or no absorption (Bianchi et al. 2008). NGC4698 and NGC4565 have also been discussed to be good candidates, (see Georgantopoulos Zezas 2003, Panessa Bassani 2002).

NGC3147, NGC4565 and NGC4698 are not considered in the mean spectrum since there is already evidence that they do not present any absorption. We also exclude the five contaminated sources leaving the cases of NGC3976, NGC4725, NGC4378 and NGC4477 to be considered.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 3147's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 3147 is a face-on spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 30.1 4.5 Mpc amanullah_spectra_2010 with a Sy 2 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 , which is the best known candidate of a ``true'' (unabsorbed) type II Seyfert (e.g., bianchi_ngc_2008,matt_suzaku_2012 ). Note that the detailed multiwavelength study by found large amounts of inflowing gas in the central arcsecond ( 150 pc) region of NGC 3147. The nucleus appears point-like at radio wavelengths even with VLBI ulvestad_origin_2001 .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3147 as a candidate true type 2 Seyfert whose GNIRS spectrum has weak emission lines and many stellar absorption features.

quotes.quote: NGC 3147 (S2) NGC 3147 is a promising candidate for a ``true'' type 2 Seyfert nucleus, in which the broad line region is genuinely lacking rather than simply obscured; Bianchi08,Matt12 . The optical spectrum of the source is well fit by a stellar population model with age=8 Gyr Bruzual93 . The GNIRS spectrum (Fig.

gmw_00000018.name: 3C 390.3
gmw_00000018.overview: 3C 390.3 is a broad-line radio galaxy [arXiv:0908.0716, arXiv:astro-ph/9912477] hosted by an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1710.02194]. Its active nucleus exhibits double-peaked Balmer emission lines [arXiv:1510.02162, arXiv:1406.5898] originating from an accretion disk [arXiv:1107.0455, arXiv:1803.10860] and displays strong multi-wavelength variability [arXiv:1803.10860, arXiv:1712.00309].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes 3C 390.3 as a broad-line radio galaxy whose X-ray variability lacks strong short-timescale changes, shows a tentative PSD break, and resembles radio-quiet AGN behavior.

quotes.quote: We investigate the short-term variability properties and the power spectral density (PSD) of the Broad-Line Radio Galaxy (BLRG) 3C 390.3 using observations made by , , and \ on several occasions between October 2004 and December 2006.

On timescales of the order of few hours, probed by uninterrupted \ light curves, the flux of \ is consistent with being constant in all energy bands. On longer timescales, probed by the 2-day \ and \ observations, the flux variability becomes significant. The latter observation confirms that the spectral variability behavior of \ is consistent with the spectral evolution observed in (radio-quiet) Seyfert galaxies: the spectrum softens as the source brightens. The correlated variability between soft and hard X-rays, observed during the \ exposure and between the 2 \ pointings, taken 1 week apart, argues against scenarios characterized by the presence of two distinct variable components in the 0.5--10 keV X-ray band.

A detailed PSD analysis carried out over five decades in frequency suggests the presence of a break at T_ br =43^ +34 _ 25 days at a 92 confidence level. This is the second tentative detection of a PSD break in a radio-loud, non-jet dominated AGN, after the BLRG 3C 120, and appears to be in general agreement with the relation between T_ br , M_ BH , and L_ bol , followed by Seyfert galaxies. Our results indicate that the X-ray variability properties of \ are broadly consistent with those of radio-quiet AGN, suggesting that the X-ray emission mechanism in \ is similar to that of nearby Seyfert galaxies without any significant contribution from a jet component.

sources.summary: The paper models 3C390.3's broad double-peaked Balmer lines with an elliptical accretion-disk BLR, reproduces H light-curve behavior, and argues the emission originates in the accretion disk around the central black hole.

quotes.quote: In this manuscript, the structure of broad emission line regions (BLRs) of well-mapping double-peaked emitter (AGN with broad double-peaked low-ionization emission lines) 3C390.3 is studied. Besides the best fitted results for double-peaked broad optical balmer lines of 3C390.3 by theoretical disk model, we try to find another way to further confirm the origination of double-peaked line from accretion disk. Based on the long-period observed spectra in optical band around 1995 collected from AGN WATCH project, the theoretical disk parameters of disk-like BLRs supposed by elliptical accretion disk model (Eracleous et al. 1995) have been well determined. Through the theoretical disk-like BLRs, characters of observed light-curves of broad double-peaked H of 3C390.3 can be well reproduced based on the reverberation mapping technique. Thus the accretion disk model is preferred as one better model for BLRs of 3C390.3.

Furthermore, we can find that different disk parameters should lead to some different results about size of BLRs of 3C390.3 from the one measured through observational data, which indicates the measured disk parameters are significantly valid for 3C390.3. After that, the precession of theoretical elliptical disk-like BLRs being considered, we can find that the expected line profile in 2000 by theoretical model is consistent with the observed line profile by HST around 2000. Based on the results, we can further believe that the origination of broad double-peaked balmer emission lines of 3C390.3 are from accretion disk around central black hole.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes 3C 390.3 as a double-peaked type 1 AGN, comparing ZDCF and SPEAR lags and treating its H result as an outlier affected by sampling.

quotes.quote: We used the Z-transformed Discrete Correlation Function (ZDCF) and the Stochastic Process Estimation for AGN Reverberation (SPEAR) methods for the time series analysis of the continuum and the H and H line fluxes of a sample of well known type 1 active galactic nuclei (AGNs): Arp 102B, 3C 390.3, NGC 5548, and NGC 4051, where the first two objects are showing double-peaked emission line profiles. The aim of this work is to compare the time lag measurements from these two methods, and check if there is a connection with other emission line properties. We found that the obtained time lags from H are larger than those derived from the H analysis for Arp 102B, 3C 390.3 and NGC 5548. This may indicate that the H line originates at larger radii in these objects.

Since the diversity in the shape and width of the line profiles implies the diversity in the shape of transfer function and thus significant variations in the strength of the line response to continuum variations see R95 , we selected our sample to have two types of objects: AGNs with double-peaked broad line profiles (Arp 102B and 3C 390.3) and typical type 1 AGNs with single-peaked profiles (NGC 5548 and NGC 4051). The double-peaked broad lines of Arp 102B and 3C390.3 were successfully modeled with the circular relativistic Keplerian accretion disk around a SMBH C89,CH89,Halp90,E97,E09 . However, their broad emission line profiles vary in a complex way, thus more complex BLR models are needed Gez07, Pop11 . The other two objects, NGC 5548 and NGC 4051 are typical broad single-peaked line AGNs. Over the 6-year monitoring campaign since 1996, NGC 5548 sometimes crosses the boundary between a Sy1 type and a Sy1.8 type AGN Sh04 , and NGC 4051 is relatively nearby object that was part of the extensive reverberation mapping campaign see P04,D06 .

In order to test the statistical relationship between the ZDCF and SPEAR results, we calculated the Pearson correlation coefficient and the p-value between different sets of time lags, which confirmed that results obtained from H of 3C 390.3 are an outlier. Such behavour of H line of this object could have roots in bad median and average sampling period and very broad the ACF of the continuum and the line itself. The secondary (spurious) time lags are obtained for H lines of Arp 102B and 3C 390.3. These spurious lags could arise due to the fact that the H line and continuum of both objects have large sampling period. In the case of 3C 390.3 average and median sampling periods are larger than 100 days and in the case of Arp 102B average sampling period is 100 days.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes optical variability in the 3C 390.3 nucleus, finding narrow-line trends, reverberation-mapping limits after trend removal, continuum-line power laws, Balmer-decrement behavior, and a low Eddington ratio.

quotes.quote: We summarize results of the analysis of the optical variability of the continuum and emission-line fluxes in the 3C 390.3 nucleus during 1992--2014. The 5007 flux increases monotonically by 30 per cent in 2003--2014. The narrow Balmer lines show similar monotonic increase, while the variability patterns of the 6300 narrow line are completely different from that of .

We show that the reverberation mapping can only be applied to the entire period of observations of the 3C 390.3 nucleus after removing a long-term trend. This trend has been expressed by a slowly varying scale factor (t) in the power-law relationship between the line and continuum fluxes: F_ line (t) F_ cont ^a . We find the power-law index a equals to 0.77 and 0.54 for the \ and \ lines, respectively. The observed relationship between the Balmer decrement and the optical continuum flux is as follows: F( )/F( ) F_ cont ^ 0.20 and F( )/F( ) F_ cont ^ 0.18 . The 3C 390.3 nucleus is an `outsider' in the relationship between optical luminosity and black hole mass.

sources.summary: The paper monitors AGN 3C 390.3, describing it as a broad-line radio galaxy with strong variability, extended radio lobes, a jet, and H emission consistent with an accretion disk.

quotes.quote: We have monitored the AGN 3C 390.3 between 1995 and 2000. A historical B-Band light curve dating back to 1966, shows a large increase in brightness during 1970 -- 1971, followed by a gradual decrease down to a minimum in 1982. During the 1995--2000 lapse the broad H emission and the continuum flux varied by a factor of 3.

Most of the objects, included in the multiwavelength monitoring AGN Watch, are radio-quiet Sy1 galaxies and only one, 3C 390.3 (z=0.0561), is a well known broad line radiogalaxy. It is also a powerful double lobed FRII radio-galaxy with a relatively strong compact core. The two extended lobes, at a position angle of 144 ^ , each one with a hot spot, are separated by about 223 (Leahy Perley ). A faint well-collimated thin jet at P.A. = 37 ^ , connecting the core to the northern lobe, has been observed by Leahy Perley ( ).
Our study favours the formation of the broad H line of 3C 390.3 in an accretion disk.

sources.summary: The paper links 3C 390.3's variable optical continuum to its compact radio jet, finding correlated jet-component, optical, UV, and X-ray behavior and placing broad-line emission about 0.4 pc from the central engine.

quotes.quote: We present observational evidence for the link between variability of the radio emission of the compact jet, optical and X-ray continua emission and ejections of new jet components in the radio galaxy 3C 390.3. The time delays between the light curves of the individual jet components and the light curve of the optical continuum are estimated by using minimization methods and the discret correlation function. We find that the variations of the optical continuum are correlated with radio emission from a stationary feature in the jet. This correlation indicates that the source of variable non-thermal continuum radiation is located in the innermost part of the relativistic jet. We suggest that the continuum emission from the jet and counterjet ionizes material in a subrelativistic outflow surrounding the jet, which results in a formation of two conical regions with broad emission lines (in addition to the conventional broad line region around the central nucleus) at a distance 0.4 parsecs from the central engine.

This can also be deduced from the facts that, in 3C 390.3, the changes of the optical flux at 5100 \ follow the variations in the UV and soft X-ray emission, with time delays of about 5 5 days dietrich and the overall spectrum in the optical to soft X-ray range is represented by a single power-law with a spectral index =0.89 wamsteker . These observations indicate that a large fraction of the variable continuum emission is non-thermal and it is produced in a region of 5 light days (0.004 pc) in size. The link between the optical continuum and the component S1 in the radio jet is further supported by the correlation between the continuum light curve and the characteristic epochs t_ D and t_ S1 of the moving components C4--C7.

sources.summary: 3C 390.3 is presented as a powerful, lobe-dominated radio galaxy with unusual optical morphology and superluminal motion; its X-ray spectra show moderate reflection and nearly constant Fe-line flux amid continuum changes.

quotes.quote: 3C 390.3 The optical morphology of this powerful radio galaxy is unusual; there are no spiral arms, but the luminosity profile does not follow the ^ 1/4 law in the outer parts of the image (Smith Heckman 1989). In the radio frequencies, the object is lobe-dominated and displays superluminal motion (Wamsteker Clavel 1989 and references therein). The galaxy has been observed by , , , and OSSE.

We find a moderate Compton reflection component corresponding to /2 0.3 in both observations (Table 7), which is similar to the result of NP94 for the 1988 observation. The presence of reflection is significant in the (longer) 1988 observation at the 99.99 per cent confidence level.

From Table 7, we see that the flux in the Fe line in all observations presented here is compatible with being constant, 3 10^ 5 cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , whereas the Fe-K ionizing flux ( 7 keV) varies by a factor of 2 , as illustrated in Figure 6a. The correlation coefficient between and the 7--10 keV flux is =0.16 , which corresponds to the probability that there is indeed no correlation between those quantities of >45 per cent. This argues against the bulk of the line being due Compton reflection from an inner region of an accretion disk, in which case a proportionality of the line to the flux would be expected.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes 3C 390.3 as a radio-loud active galaxy whose double-peaked Balmer lines trace a complex, mostly disk-like broad-line region with possible jet and perturbation effects.

quotes.quote: One of the well known AGN with broad double-peaked emission lines in its spectrum is the radio-loud active galaxy 3C 390.3. Although, the double-peaked line profiles can be explained by different hypothesis see e.g. vz91 , as e.g. super-massive binary black holes gas96 , outflowing bi-conical gas streams zh96 ), it seems that in this case a disk emission is present in the BLR hereinafter Paper I sh10 . There is a possibility that a jet emission can affect the optical emission in 3C 390.3 tig10 , and some perturbations in disk could be present jov10 and they can also affect the double-peaked line profiles.

The aim of this paper is to investigate the changes in the the BLR structure of 3C 390.3 that cause the line profile variations. To perform this investigation we analyzed the peak separation variations, variations in line segments and variation in Balmer decrement. Using a relatively simple disk model, we try to explain qualitatively the changes in disk structure that can cause the line parameter variations.
The geometry of the BLR of 3C390.3 seems to be very complex, and inflows/outflows might be present, but it is evident that the broad line region with disk-like geometry has dominant emission.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes joint Suzaku/NuSTAR and Swift data for the broad-line radio galaxy 3C 390.3, measuring coronal Comptonization parameters, weak reflection, a soft X-ray excess, and ionized iron emission.

quotes.quote: We present the results from a joint Suzaku / NuSTAR broad-band spectral analysis of 3C 390.3. The high quality data enables us to clearly separate the primary continuum from the reprocessed components allowing us to detect a high energy spectral cut-off ( E_ cut =117_ 14 ^ +18 keV), and to place constraints on the Comptonization parameters of the primary continuum for the first time. The hard over soft compactness is 69 _ 24 ^ +124 and the optical depth 4.1 _ 3.6 ^ +0.5 , this leads to an electron temperature of 30_ 8 ^ +32 keV. Expanding our study of the Comptonization spectrum to the optical/UV by studying the simultaneous Swift UVOT data, we find indications that the compactness of the corona allows only a small fraction of the total UV/optical flux to be Comptonized.

Our analysis of the reprocessed emission show that 3C 390.3 only has a small amount of reflection ( R 0.3 ), and of that the vast majority is from distant neutral matter. However we also discover a soft X-ray excess in the source, which can be described by a weak ionized reflection component from the inner parts of the accretion disk. In addition to the backscattered emission, we also detect the highly ionized iron emission lines Fe XXV and Fe XXVI.

sources.summary: The paper studies 3C 390.3 as a double-peaked broad-line AGN, finding intrinsic Baldwin-effect behavior and noting broad Balmer profiles, radio loudness, and relatively high spectral variability.

quotes.quote: We investigate the intrinsic Baldwin effect (Beff) of the broad H and H emission lines for six Type 1 active galactic nuclei (AGNs) with different broad line characteristics: two Seyfert 1 ( NGC 4151 and NGC 5548 ), two AGNs with double-peaked broad line profiles ( 3C 390.3 and Arp 102B ), one narrow line Seyfert 1 ( Ark 564 ), and one high-luminosity quasar with highly red asymmetric broad line profiles ( E1821+643 ). We find that a significant intrinsic Beff was present in all Type 1 AGNs in our sample. Moreover, we do not see strong differences in intrinsic Beff slopes in different types of AGNs, which probably have different physical properties such as inclination, broad line region (BLR) geometry, or accretion rate.

Double-peaked broad line AGNs 3C 390.3 and Arp 102B , whose broad Balmer lines show two distinguished peaks with very large overall width (FWHM 10000 \ kms^ 1 ). This type of objects are called double-peaked line (DPL) AGNs. These lines are supposed to be emitted from an accretion disk ch89 .

3C 390.3 , which is a radio loud AGN, seems to have higher variability in the spectra sh10b than Arp 102B sh13 ; Narrow line Seyfert 1 (NLSy1) AGN Ark 564 , which has a strong Fe II emission and somewhat narrower broad lines (FWHM 2000 \ kms^ 1 ) with a prominent narrow component.

sources.summary: The paper models 3C 390.3 as an unobscured broad-line radio galaxy whose hard X-ray spectrum constrains the torus column density, covering factor, and inclination.

quotes.quote: 3C 390.3 The unobscured 3C 390.3 is our simplest example, since the lack of line-of-sight absorption in the \ band allows us to set \ to zero. The top two panels in fig:sweep show that a good fit can be found for any assumed , but that there is a clear minimum in ^2 around the best fit at =1.1 10^ 24 . For the best fit, ^2=561.0 for 612 degrees of freedom (d.o.f.). This model is shown in the top left panel of fig:eeuf ; the flat residuals suggest that all features in the data are accounted for by this spectral solution. With \ fixed at the best-fit value, we find =0.32_ 0.07 ^ +0.05 .

The inclination angle is constrained to >0.3 , so that our line of sight does not intercept any of the reprocessing material, consistent with the type 1 optical classification of 3C 390.3. fig:nh_cf_plane shows the probability density distribution in the two-dimensional plane spanned by \ and , obtained from a fit in which \ is left free to vary. In this case, the covering factor constraint is slightly broader: =0.3_ 0.1 ^ +0.2 .

sources.summary: The paper links 3C 390.3 jet ejections to optical, radio, and X-ray variability, identifying stationary and moving VLBA jet features and X-ray dips associated with component ejections.

quotes.quote: To examine this coupling, we combine the jet ejection epochs in a radio-loud galaxy 3C 390.3 ( z =0.00561) and archived monitoring data in radio, optical and X-ray ( ROSAT and RXTE ) bands. The dense X-ray data allows zooming into the regions of X-ray dips to study the X-ray emission characteristics at the epoch of a jet ejection event. Ten epochs of Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) imaging at 15 GHz (obtained between 1994 to 2002 as a part of the 2 cm VLBA survey ) revealed the structure of the jet, the kinematics of jet components and the evolution of their emission on angular scales of 3 milliarcseconds (1 mas=1.09 pc) . Three stationary features (D, S1 and S2, see Fig.  fig1 ) and five moving components (C2-C6) with apparent speeds from 0.8 to 1.5 were identified.

The disk-jet coupling in 3C 390.3 is further evidenced from the hard X-ray monitoring data. In Fig.  fig5 , the ejection time of the component C8 is compared with various characteristics of the X-ray light-curve at 2-20 keV . The jet ejection epoch coincides with the dips in the intensity of the X-ray flux and of the flux variability (left upper panel), the hardening of the spectrum (similar to 3C 120) and a large variance of the hardness (left lower panel).

sources.summary: The paper examines 3C 390.3's northern and southern hotspot complexes, finding X-ray jet and hotspot components, a resolved double X-ray structure at knot B, and weak southern hotspot emission.

quotes.quote: The northern compact hotspot of 3C 390 is a well-known X-ray source p97,hll98,hhwb04 . Diffuse X-ray emission from the large hotspot region of the S lobe was discussed by hc05 . A detailed examination of the Chandra data shows that several components of the N hotspot complex are X-ray sources (Fig. \ 390.3hsn ).

The radio jet, which is detectable throughout the N lobe lp95 brightens strongly in the area shown by our image, and there is faint but clear X-ray emission associated with this section of the jet. The strongest X-ray `source' is the known X-ray counterpart of the primary hotspot lp95 , knot B, here denoted NX1. The centroid of this region is only slightly ( 0 4 0 1 , 0.4 kpc) offset to the S of the peak of the radio emission, but the resolution of Chandra shows that the X-ray feature is actually resolved into two components, separated by 1.5 arcsec (1.6 kpc) and placed symmetrically on either side of the radio peak.

sources.summary: The paper links 3C 390.3 optical continuum and double-peaked H variability to its parsec-scale jet, especially stationary component S1, using long-term VLBI and spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: The first DP emission line profile was discovered in the H profile of the broad-line radio galaxy 3C 390.3, whose parsec-scale radio jet has been monitored at 15 GHz since 1995 with the Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA). The compact jet can be modelled by circular Gaussian components (see Figure 1).

Arshakian et al. (2006) found a significant correlation with the flux density at 15 GHz of the stationary component S1 in the parsec-scale jet and the optical continuum emission in 3C 3903. This strongly implies that the jet emission from component S1 is the main source of the optical continuum driving the DP emission line variability.

We found a correlation between the S1 jet component and the H optical emission using 11 years of VLBI and H broad-emission line monitoring, supporting the idea that the optical nuclear emission in 3C 390.3 has a non-thermal origin. Figure 2 shows the suggested scheme that explains the basic properties of the optical emission (continuum and broad H ) driven by the inner jet.

sources.summary: The paper finds a positive broad H line-width/flux correlation in 3C390.3, explains it with a disk-like BLR, and still supports virialization.

quotes.quote: In this manuscript, we carefully check the correlation between the line width (second moment) and the line flux of the double-peaked broad H of the well-known mapped AGN 3C390.3, in order to show some further distinctions between double-peaked emitters and normal broad line AGN. Based on the Virialization assumption M_ BH R_ BLR V^2(BLR) and the empirical relation R_ BLR L^ 0.5 , one strong negative correlation between the line width and the line flux of the double-peaked broad lines should be expected for 3C390.3, such as the negative correlation confirmed for the mapped broad line object NGC5548, R_ BLR V^2(BLR) L^ 0.5 ^2 = constant . But, based on the public spectra around 1995 from the AGNWATCH project for 3C390.3, one reliable positive correlation is found between the line width and the line flux of the double-peaked broad H .

In the context of the proposed theoretical accretion disk model for double-peaked emitters, the unexpected positive correlation can be naturally explained, due to different time delays for inner parts and outer parts of disk-like BLR of 3C390.3. Moreover, the Virialization assumption is checked and found to be still available for 3C390.3. However, time-varying size of the BLR of 3C390.3 can not be expected by the empirical relation R_ BLR L^ 0.5 .

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 390.3 as a radio-loud Population B source with complex line profiles, weak Al III, and a low-density broad-line-region solution.

quotes.quote: At the Population B end of the 4DE1 sequence most sources show weak optical Fe ii emission as well as very weak or undetectable Al iii 1860 i.e., Al iii 1860 / C iii ] 1909 0. Many of these sources are radio loud. 3C 390.3 shows complex line profiles including a prominent blue hump in the Balmer lines and HILs. The blue hump (which is unlikely to be the broad component because of the large shift) shows Al iii 1860/ C iii ] 1909 0.05 while only an upper limit can be assigned to optical Fe ii emission.

In this case there is no high-density solution. The measured emission line ratios converge toward a low-density solution that also accounts for the observed Al iii 1860 / C iii ] 1909 ratio: (n_ H U) 8.55 0.14 \ at 1 .

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 390.3 as a broad-line radio-loud type 1 AGN whose continuum and emission-line light curves show significant multi-year periodicities with complex correlation polarity.

quotes.quote: The characteristics of these objects encompasses the entire range of type 1 AGN activity (see Table  tab:data ): from Seyfert 1 objects NGC 4151 and NGC 5548 as prototypes of spectral type variation (from Seyfert 1.0 to 1.8), a very broad line and radio loud object 3C 390.3, then Arp 102B that is considered as a LINER and archetypical disc accretion emitter due to extremely broad, double peaked Balmer lines, and finally radio quiet but optically powerful near-by quasar E1821+643.

3C 390.3. The 2D correlation maps in Fig.  fig:per3c show the signature of the 6.3 and 7.1 yr period to be present in the continuum and H line. The method yields 0.7 for the correlation coefficients of both periods with a significance p<0.00001 . Somewhat weaker periodicity (correlation coefficient 0.5, but still significant) was found at 9.5 years.

2D (auto)correlation maps of Arp 102B have simple linear shape, but for 3C 390.3 a manifold of substructures is superimposed. Namely, our analysis has shown the fluctuations in the interactions between the oscillations in the continuum and the emission lines of 3C 390.3 due to substantially different levels of correlation polarity.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes 1992-2014 variability of 3C 390.3, interpreting broad-line lags, double-peaked profiles, narrow-line variability, and possible BLR infall in its active nucleus.

quotes.quote: Results of the analysis of the variability of the \ and \ broad emission-line profiles and the 4686 \ emission-line fluxes in the 3C 390.3 nucleus during 1992--2014 are present. Probably, a radial infall motion can be present in the broad-line region of 3C 390.3 in addition to the Keplerian rotation. The nucleus of the radio galaxy 3C 390.3 is a prototype to so-called double-peaked emitters, objects with strongly double-peaked broad-line profiles (red and blue bumps or shoulders).

The present study is a continuation of the previous studies of the 3C 390.3 nucleus carried out in the Crimean Astrophysical Observatory (CrAO) since 1992: Ser02,Ser11 and Sergeev, Nazarov Borman (2017) hereafter Ser17 . However, in the case of 3C 390.3 the underlying difficulty is that there are evidences for the narrow-line variability cl87,Zhe95,Ser17 . A discrepancy between spectral and photometric light curves of 3C 390.3 Ser17 has been attributed to this variability.

sources.summary: The paper finds 3C 390.3 has soft-excess residuals, extended soft X-ray emission around the nucleus, no extra absorption, and unconstrained high-energy cut-off.

quotes.quote: Inspection of figure pl2 (left panel) suggests that the model employed in table 3 does not provide a good description of the data of 3C 390.3, where the residuals have an unusual concave shape, which may be due to the presence of a low energy component (soft excess) still affecting the spectrum around 2-3 keV. It is interesting to note that so far no soft excess has been reported for this source except for an old claim never confirmed afterwards b38 ; however a Chandra image of 3C 390.3 reveals extended soft X-ray emission around the nucleus so the soft excess photons we detect could be related to this component ( b61 and figure 7 in b39 ). Due to the evidence found, in the following we adopt the model wa _g *wa*(bb+po+zga) (table 3A and figure 21247_3c390_bb right panel) as our basic representation of 3C 390.3.

In all but 2 AGN (B3 0309+411 and 3C 390.3), an intrinsic absorption component is strongly required by the data, at more than 90 confidence level for IGR J13109-5552 and S5 2116+81 and at more than 99 confidence level for the remaining objects. As far as the other sources are concerned, we could only set a lower limit for their cut-off energies at around 60-80 keV, apart from 3C 390.3, where the high energy cut-off could not be constrained at all.

sources.summary: The paper links 3C 390.3's optical continuum variability to compact radio-jet components, arguing that jet and counterjet emission ionizes outflowing gas and produces double-peaked broad-line regions.

quotes.quote: Variations of the optical continuum emission in the radio galaxy 3C 390.3 are compared to the properties of radio emission from the compact, sub-parsec-scale jet in this object. We showed that very long-term variations of optical continuum emission ( 10 years) is correlated with the radio emission from the base of the jet located above the disk, while the optical long-term variations (1-2 years) follows the radio flares from the stationary component in the jet with time delay of about 1 year. This stationary feature is most likely to be a standing shock formed in the continuous relativistic flow seen at a distance of 0.4 parsecs from the base of the jet.

To account for the correlations observed we propose a model of the nuclear region of 3C 390.3 in which the beamed continuum emission from the jet and counterjet ionizes material in a subrelativistic outflow surrounding the jet. This results in the formation of two conical regions with double-peaked broad emission lines (in addition to the conventional broad line region around the central nucleus) at a distance 0.6 parsecs from the central engine.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes radio, optical, UV, and X-ray monitoring of 3C 390.3, identifying parsec-scale jet components and linking radio-knot passages through S1 with optical flares.

quotes.quote: The principal results of this work comes from analysis of combined radio VLBI (15 GHz), optical/UV and X-ray data of the radio-loud galaxy 3C 390.3, and they can be summarized as follows: Structure of the parsec-scale jet: from ten epochs of VLBI observations we identified three stationary components (D, S1 and S2) and nine moving components (C4-C12) of the jet on scales of a few parsecs. Apparent speeds of moving components are estimated to be in the range from 0.7 to 1.6 . We found a new correlation between the local maxima in the optical continuum light curve and the epochs at which the moving components of the jet pass the stationary radio feature S1. Nine radio events follow the peaks of optical flares with the mean time delay of 1.2 0.4 months. Optical flares brighten when radio knots move between stationary components D and S1.

sources.summary: Suzaku spectra of 3C 390.3 are included among BLRGs with significant >7 keV absorption lines, interpreted as blue-shifted Fe ultra-fast outflows from accretion disk winds.

quotes.quote: In Tombesi et al. (2010b) we performed a systematic 4--10 keV spectral analysis of long Suzaku observations of five BLRGs, i.e. 3C 111, 3C 120, 3C 390.3, 3C 382 and 3C 445. We detected, for the first time at X-rays in radio-loud AGNs, several absorption lines at energies greater than 7 keV in the first three sources. The lines are detected with high significance ( > 99 ) according to both the F-test and extensive Monte Carlo simulations (see Fig. 1). Their likely interpretation as blue-shifted Fe XXV--XXVI K-shell resonances implies an origin from highly ionized gas outflowing with mildly relativistic velocities, in the range v 0.04--0.15c. A fit with specific photo-ionization models gives ionization parameters in the range log 4--5.6 erg s ^ 1 cm and column densities of N_H 10^ 22 - 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 .

sources.summary: The paper includes 3C 390.3 among the modeled AGN, finding comparatively narrow ranges in emission-line width and radius and a different broad-line region structure from the other objects.

quotes.quote: The galaxy 3C 390.3 was monitored in a ground-based optical monitoring campaign from 1994 October through 1995 October (Dietrich et al. dietrich98 ). In parallel an extensive monitoring campaign was carried out with the IUE satellite from 1994 December 31 to 1996 March 5 (O'Brien et al. obrien98 ). For the values of the line widths in 3C 390.3 (Table 1, Fig. 3) we used the unblended halfs of the emission lines (Peterson et al. peterson04 ).

The widths of their lines differ by a factor of 2.5 to 6. In contrast to the other galaxies, the line widths of the emission lines in 3C 390.3 (Table 1, Fig. 3) differ by a factor of 1.18 only. The radii of the line-emitting regions in 3C 390.3 (Table 1) also span a comparatively limited range.

sources.summary: The paper presents 24 epochs of spectropolarimetry of the radio-loud active galaxy 3C 390.3, measuring continuum and Balmer-line polarization lags that imply jet and outflow components.

quotes.quote: Here we present the spectropolarimetric observations of the radio loud active galaxy 3C 390.3 in the period 2009-2014 (24 epochs). The galaxy has been observed with the 6-meter telescope of SAO RAS using the SCORPIO spectropolarimeter. We explore the variability and lags in the polarized light of the continuum and broad H line.

We found that the lags between unpolarized and polarized continuum at 5100 are 10 -- 40 days; between the unpolarized continuum and the broad H line 60 -- 80 days; between the unpolarized continuum and broad H flux 140 -- 190 days; between the unpolarized continuum and polarized H is 90 -- 160 days (see Table tab4 ). In all three methods we obtained that the lag between the unpolarized and polarized continuum is significantly smaller than lags between the continuum and broad lines, that indicates that the variable polarized continuum is originating in a region which is significantly smaller than the BLR.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 390.3 as a double-peaked type 1 AGN whose Balmer profiles vary strongly, especially in the blue peak, plausibly from accretion-disk perturbations.

quotes.quote: The objects have been selected so that representatives of type 1 AGN with different BEL profiles are in the sample: Arp 102B and 3C 390.3 have BELs with double-peaked line profiles, Ark 564 is a Narrow-Line Seyfert 1 (NLSy1), and NGC 4151 and NGC 5548 are well-studied Seyfert 1.5 with strongly variable BEL profiles, with bumps and asymmetries present in the line profiles (see Figure 1).

In order to show how the line profiles vary in two DPL objects, we plot the mean and rms profile for the H line of 3C 390.3 (Figure 5, upper panel) and Arp 102B (Figure 5, bottom panel). From the shape and intensity of the rms profiles, it is obvious that in case of 3C 390.3 the line profiles vary significantly, while in Arp 102B the line profiles remain almost unchanged.

It is obvious that for some objects, the BLR geometry is changing during decades of observation, e.g. in case of 3C 390.3 the rms profile of H shows strong variability especially in the blue peak Po11 . One possible scenario to model this variability is with the orbiting bright spots in accretion disk Jo10 .

sources.summary: The paper finds 3C 390.3 monitoring inconsistent with binary broad-line motion, requiring excessive mass and 28 pc separation contradicted by reverberation mapping.

quotes.quote: Those three objects, Arp 102B, 3C 390.3, and 3C 332, are included in our present collection but their time series are now 2, 1.5, and 1.3 times longer, respectively.
However, later rejected this interpretation, as they found that the motion in 3C 390.3 stopped after 1988 (which is also evident from our Figure vcurve ).

In the case of 3C 390.3, we can use constraints on the inclination angle of the radio jet, assumed to be perpendicular to the orbital plane, to refine the constraint on the mass of a hypothesized SMBH binary: Eracleous1996 estimated that i > 19^ based on the observed superluminal motion in the radio jet. Applying this constraint raises the minimum mass of the binary to (M_ min /M_ )=14.6 , which is rather implausible. Moreover, the binary separation in 3C 390.3 implied by the constraints is 28 pc.

sources.summary: The paper evaluates 3C 390.3 as a double-peaked emitter/SBHB candidate, discussing earlier orbital interpretations, later rejection, Gaussian-like jitter behavior, and high inferred minimum masses.

quotes.quote: noted that the failure to detect such a change could be explained by large BH masses, and proposed that 3C 390.3 is also a SBHB in which both BLRs are visible. The observed drift of the displaced peaks in the spectrum of 3C 390.3 between 1968 and 1988 was consistent with this hypothesis, and a sinusoidal model fit to the data suggested a period of 300 yr and a total mass of 7.7 10^9 . However, the sinusoidal trend did not continue past 1990, and rejected the SBHB interpretation of 3C 390.3, together with two other double-peaked emitters, 3C 332 and Arp 102B.
We find that the jitter distribution for the well-sampled radial velocity curves of 3C 390.3 and Arp 102B is well represented by a Gaussian.

The minimum masses resulting from this analysis are close to 10^ 11 for two objects (Arp 102B, 3C 390.3), in the range (0.4--4) 10^ 9 for nine objects, and in the range (0.8--3) 10^ 8 for the remaining three objects.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 390.3 as a local active galaxy in its bare-AGN sample and analyzes XMM-Newton/Swift X-ray spectra, including continuum, soft-excess, and spectral-fitting behavior.

quotes.quote: 3C 390.3 is a radio-loud Seyfert 1 nearby (z=0.056) AGN Afanasiev2015 . However, from timing analysis, this source is classified as a radio-quiet Seyfert and it was found that there is no noticeable contribution from the jet to the X-ray emission Sambruna2009 . In the X-ray regime, detection of soft-excess below 2 keV and narrow Fe lines are reported from XMM-Newton observations of the source Sambruna2009 . Later, from the modelling of Fe lines and ionized reflection, it is shown that the source is ambiguous in broad-line radio galaxies Tombesi2013 . The mass of the central black hole is estimated as 2.0 10^9 M _ Sergeev2011 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses 3C 390.3 as a Population 2 AGN with double-peaked lines, a relatively large BLR inclination, and an FRII radio-source orientation consistent with no superluminal motion.

quotes.quote: Of the seven Population 2 objects in the Onken sample, estimates are available for only two AGNs, 3C 390.3 and NGC 4151. The former is one of the relatively rare strongly double-peaked emitters; the double peaks are widely regarded as the signature of an inclined disk in Keplerian rotation. Eracleous et al. \ (1996), based on several indicators, conclude that the inclination of the BLR in this system must be in the range 19 < i < 42 ^ , and based on the profile fitting alone, Eracleous Halpern (1994) find that i = 24-30^ . At the very least, this relatively large inclination is consistent with our finding that 3C 390.3 is not a member of Population 1. On the other hand, it is quite natural to find a relatively large inclination for a powerful FRII radio source like 3C 390.3 that does not show superluminal motion.

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 390.3 as a well-studied radio galaxy monitored in soft X-ray, UV, and optical bands, with substantial host contribution and a V-band lag behind RXTE flux.

quotes.quote: 3C 390.3 is our best-studied radio galaxy. Radio galaxies lie at the opposite end of the eigenvector 1 from NLS1s. Fig. 3 shows the results of our simultaneous soft X-ray, UV, and optical monitoring. The ROSAT 0.1 -- 2 keV fluxes are from Leighly97 , the IUE 1370 UV fluxes are based on OBrien98 , and V-band fluxes have been derived with scalings and corrections from Dietrich98 . 3C 390.3 has a substantial (and as yet undetermined) host galaxy contribution.

On the other hand, in subsequent monitoring of 3C 390.3 we find that our V-band flux appears to lag the RXTE 2--10 keV flux by 4.5 days (see Fig. 4 of GaskellKlimek03 ), and the amplitude of the short-term optical variability exceeds the amplitude of the X-ray variability.

sources.summary: The paper fits 1995-1999 H alpha profiles of 3C 390.3 with accretion-disk bright spots, interpreting two line outbursts as decaying perturbations.

quotes.quote: We applied this model to the observed H line profiles of 3C 390.3 (observed in the period 1995-1999), and estimated the parameters of both, accretion disk and perturbing region. Our results show that two large amplitude outbursts of the H line observed in 3C 390.3 could be explained by successive occurrences of two bright spots on approaching side of the disk. These bright spots are either moving, originating in the inner regions of the disk and spiralling outwards by crossing small distances during the period of several years, or stationary. In both cases, their widths increase with time, indicating that they most likely decay.

sources.summary: The paper discusses 3C 390.3 as a broad-line AGN with long-term blue-peak velocity changes and profile-variability behavior that off-axis illumination can explain.

quotes.quote: Orbital motion of regions of excess Balmer line emission was suggested by zheng+91 as the cause of line-profile variability in 3C 390.3. gaskell96a showed that blue peak in 3C 390.3 showed an almost linear decrease in blueshift over a period of 20 years, but this did not continue eracleous+97 .

In their studies of profile variability of Arp 102B, NGC 4151, and 3C 390.3 sergeev+00,sergeev+01,sergeev+02 found three curious things in all three objects that have hitherto not been satisfactorily explained: H showed strong rms variations in different velocity bins while the total line flux changed only a little. The integrated line flux has a much better correlation with continuum flux than any individual velocity bin does. Relatively narrow velocity regions of line profiles can at times show a strikingly weaker correlation with the continuum (or even anti-correlations.) All three of these effects can readily be explained by the off-axis-variability model.

sources.summary: The review presents 3C 390.3 as an early supermassive-binary candidate whose line-peak drift fit the hypothesis, but reverberation mapping favored disk emission instead.

quotes.quote: One of the first candidate for the SMB system was 3C390.3 g83,g85 . Moreover, an observed systematic drift of the blue-shifted, broad H peak in the spectrum of 3C 390.3 between 1968 and 1988 was consistent with the SMB hypothesis g96b . But reverberation mapping of 3C 390.3, however, showed that on a light-crossing timescale the red and blue peaks varied near simultaneously see e.g. die98,sh01,sh10 and the other effects seen in the line profiles indicated disc origin see er97,sh01,eh03,sh10,pop11 . This strongly rules out binary black holes as the cause of the displaced peaks in this object.

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 390.3 as a radio-loud AGN with a single-trough Fe K absorber, confirms earlier absorption at similar confidence, but rates its outflow marginal.

quotes.quote: Of the 10 sources in which the Fe K absorber is manifested by a single trough only 4 show evidence for degenerate solutions (3C 390.3, 4C +74.26, ESO 103-G035 and NGC 4151).

state that 3/5 of the BLRGs in their sample, namely 3C 111, 3C 120 and 3C 390.3, show evidence for highly-ionised absorption lines in their spectra; with that found in 3C 111 also being confirmed in more recent subsequent observations ( tombesi:2011b ) We are able to confirm the absorption lines detected in both 3C 111 and 3C 390.3 at a similar confidence level.

The four objects where our outflow detections are not corroborated are: 3C 390.3, 4C+74.26, NGC 5506 and SWIFT J2127.4+5654. The outflows that we detect in three of these objects (3C 390.3, 4C+74.26 and SWIFT J2127.4+5654) are only marginal detections from Monte Carlo simulations, i.e., 95 P_ MC < 99 , which suggests that the lines may not be visually apparent in the raw data without a statistically driven analysis.

sources.summary: The paper models 3C390.3 as a double-peaked AGN, finding disk-ring time lags correlated with radii and deriving a black hole mass consistent with literature values.

quotes.quote: In the letter, under the widely accepted theoretical accretion disk model for the double-peaked emitter 3C390.3, the extended disk-like BLR can be well split into ten rings, and then the time lags between the lines from the rings and the continuum emission are estimated, based on the observed spectra around 1995. We can find one much strong correlation between the determined time lags (in unit of light-day) and the flux weighted radii (in unit of R_G ) of the rings, which is well consistent with the expected results through the theoretical accretion disk model. Moreover, through the strong correlation, the black hole masses of 3C390.3 are independently estimated as 10^9 M_ , the same as the reported black hole masses in the literature. The consistencies provide further evidence to strongly support the accretion disk origination of the double-peaked broad balmer lines of 3C390.3.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 5273 with 3C 390.3, citing its broadband cut-off, photon index, electron temperature, and similar coronal parameters despite different radio properties.

quotes.quote: Using joint / broadband spectral analysis of the broad-line radio galaxy 3C 390.3, lo15 detected a high energy cut-off at 117 ^ +18 _ 14 keV when the source accretes at 1 of Eddington luminosity. They obtained the photon power-law index of 1.71 ^ +0.1 _ 0.1 and the coronal electron temperature of 30 ^ +32 _ 8 keV. These parameters that describe coronal properties of 3C 390.3 are qualitatively similar to that obtained from the energy spectral analysis of NGC 5273 presented here. However, it may be noted that the core of NGC 5273 is not detected in radio with the European VLBI Network (EVN) at 1.6 GHz and 5 GHz above the threshold of few hundreds of Jy pa10 .

sources.summary: The paper reports marginal PSF mismatch, a sufficient single-bulge fit, an acceptable two-component alternative, and literature descriptions as an elliptical host.

quotes.quote: 3C 390.3 (Fig. 11.5) --- \ \ There is a marginal PSF mismatch for this object. While a single bulge (17.09 mag) is sufficient for representing the host, the fit with two components ( m_ bul =17.45 mag) works reasonable well, also. The host is well-fit with a \ profile with n 3.1 in the \ IR image (Floyd et al. 2008). Bennert et al. (2010) also described the host as an elliptical galaxy (but see Bentz et al. 2009).

sources.summary: The review discusses spectropolarimetric monitoring of 3C 390.3, reporting BLR and polarized-continuum region sizes and linking its H-line polarization to Keplerian BLR motion.

quotes.quote: The spectropolarimetric monitoring was conducted at BTA telescope for Sy 1.5 Mrk 6 (Afanasiev et al. 2014) and Sy 1 3C 390.3 (Afanasiev et al. 2015).

A similar result was obtained for 3C 390.3: the size of the BLR in H _ was 120 lt days (for H _ was 2 times less, 60 lt days), and the size of the region where polarized continuum forms – 10 lt days. Thus, the region of the polarized continuum, approximately 10 times smaller than BLR, and it is consistent with the idea of the polarized radiation coming from the accretion disk.

Thus, observing clouds rotating at Keplerian orbits, the obtained dependence (v_ i /) vs. ( ( _i)) must be linear, which was discovered firstly by modeling, then by observations in the broad H _ line in Mrk 6 (Afanasiev et al. 2014) and in 3C 390.3 (Afanasiev et al. 2015), and for for 35 Sy 1 galaxies (Afanasiev et al. 2019).

sources.summary: The paper highlights 3C 390.3 as a high-redshift sample object whose Ca triplet lines avoided sky emission but required severe atmospheric-absorption correction that was successfully applied.

quotes.quote: In the case of 3C 390.3, the atmospheric absorption correction was quite critical since all of the \ lines lie in a region of strong atmospheric absorption. In part due to the broad \ features in this object, we were able remove the telluric lines successfully.

3C 390.3 -- Due to its high redshift, the \ lines fall in a region relatively free of night sky emission lines eliminating the need for nod-and-shuffle sky subtraction. However, the atmospheric absorption is severe; careful division by atmospheric template was remarkably successful.

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 390.3 as an exceptional radio-loud Seyfert with double-peaked Balmer lines, modeled using two broad Gaussian components whose combined flux defines the broad component.

quotes.quote: The modelling described above works well in most objects except in a few cases, for example the objects 3C 390.3 and Mark 509. The object 3C 390.3 is a radio-loud (RL) Seyfert galaxy that shows double-peaked structure in their Balmer emission lines (e.g. O'Brien et al. 1998; Eracleous Halpern 2003; Strateva et al. 2003). To measure the broad H emission of 3C 390.3, we employ two separated broad Gaussian components to reproduce the observed double-peaked profile. In 3C 390.3, the flux of H broad component includes the contributions of both broad Gaussian profiles.

sources.summary: The broad-line radio galaxy 3C 390.3 is highly variable in HRI observations and is treated as point-like except for a previously discussed X-ray hotspot.

quotes.quote: 3C 390.3 Leighly \ (1997) discuss 90 separate HRI observations of this broad-line radio galaxy in the context of a variability study; the source is shown to be highly variable on time-scales of weeks. We adopt their average value as our count rate. As discussed by Harris, Leighly Leahy (1998a), the radial profile of the stacked HRI observations is broader than would be expected from the nominal HRI PRF, but its width is within the range observed to be a result of aspect uncertainty, and so we treat the source as point-like [ with the exception of the X-ray hot spot discussed by Prieto (1997) and Harris ] .

sources.summary: The review says 3C 390.3 profile variability rules out a binary BLR interpretation and instead supports BLR gas orbiting in an inclined disk.

quotes.quote: Firstly, although for a while it looked like the expected binary orbital motion was being seen in long-term profile variations in 3C 390.3 gaskell96 , further observations showed that the radial velocity changes were completely inconsistent with a binary black hole eracleous+97 but were instead consistent with orbital motion of concentrations of BLR gas orbiting in a disk. The second fatal blow was that velocity-resolved reverberation mapping of 3C 390.3 strongly ruled out the binary BLR hypothesis because the redshifted and blueshifted peaks varied simultaneously on a light-crossing timescale obrien+98,dietrich+98 . This demonstrated conclusively that the double-peaked profiles arose from an inclined disk, as had been widely proposed (see references and discussion in gaskell+snedden99 ).

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 390.3 as a strong double-peaked AGN with reverberation data, arguing its simultaneous line-component response favors a disk-emitter origin over binary broad-line regions.

quotes.quote: In particular 3C 390.3 is a strong double-peak object with good reverberation mapping data Dietrich_etal_1998 . The time-ordered data of this source show that the blueshifted and redshifted components respond to the continuum variations almost simultaneously. Thus, it is more likely that the double-peaked emission originates from a disk rather than from two corotating BLRs in a binary system.

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 390.3 as a luminous AGN missed by its edge method, with XMM-Newton observations showing higher-energy, highly ionized absorption.

quotes.quote: The remaining four (IC 4329A, NGC 5548, 3C 382, and 3C 390.3) are categorized as having outflows in 1997MNRAS.286..513R and non-outflow sources in our study.

Finally, ionized absorption is detected at higher energies than probed in our study (6.6 keV) in XMM-Newton observations of 3C 390.3, indicating an even higher ionization parameter of = 3.43 erg cm s ^ 1 2009ApJ...700.1473S .
The most luminous sources in our sample, those with L_ 14-195 keV 44.5 , are, from most to least luminous, 4C +74.26, MR 2251--178, 3C 390.3, 3C 382, 1H 0419--577, and Mrk 926.

sources.summary: The paper discusses 3C 390.3 as a double-peaked quasar once considered compatible with a binary broad-line-region model, but whose later line variability contradicted that interpretation.

quotes.quote: He also suggested that quasars with double-peaked emission lines, such as 3C 390.3, could represent SBHB in which both BLRs are visible. Indeed, an observed systematic drift of the blue-shifted, broad H peak in the spectrum of 3C 390.3 between 1968 and 1988 was consistent with this hypothesis gaskell96 . However, this behavior is a rather uncommon feature of double-peaked emission lines e.g., gezari07,lewis10 and, in fact, the trend observed in 3C 390.3 did not continue past 1990 eracleous97, shapovalova01 .

sources.summary: The atlas gives 3C 390.3's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: 3C 390.3 is a flat-spectrum FR II radio source identified with the elliptical galaxy VII Zw 838 martel_hubble_1999 at a redshift of z= 0.0561 ( D 259 Mpc). It contains a variable AGN with optical Sy 1.5 classification veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 and blazar characteristics that9 is also a member of the nine-month BAT AGN sample. 3C 390.3 features supergalactic-scale biconical radio lobes with a faint one-sided jet extending to the north-eastern lobe (PA 145 ; e.g., leahy_jets_1995 ).

sources.summary: The review identifies 3C 390.3 as a radio-loud double-peaked-line AGN with strong variability and BLR geometry following an accretion disk.

quotes.quote: Here we present some interesting findings of the long-term (more than a decade) optical monitoring campaign of a sample of seven type 1 AGN of different spectral types: Seyfert 1 galaxies (NGC 5548, NGC 4151, NGC 7469), Narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy - NLSy 1 (Ark 564), double-peaked line radio loud (3C 390.3) and radio quiet (Arp 102B) galaxy, and a luminous quasar (E1821+643), a supermassive binary black hole candidate.

If we compare the normalized light-curves shown in Figure fig:1 , the strongest variable objects are NGC 5548, NGC 4151, and 3C 390.3 (see also in Table 1 the variability parameter F _ var which is 40 ), followed by Arp 102B and NGC 7469 (F _ var 20 ).

For example, in case of 3C 390.3 it is clear that the BLR is following the accretion disk geometry Po11 , whereas in case of the other double-peak line emitter Arp 102B this is maybe not the case Po14 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C 390.3 as a broad-line radio galaxy with an atypically strong nucleus that extends the radio-optical correlation and overlaps the X-ray selected BL Lac region.

quotes.quote: The two broad-line objects in the sample, 3C 382 and 3C 390.3, show atypically strong nuclei, as would be expected from their ground-based classification as N-galaxies.

The correlation is strong over several orders of magnitude of luminosity, extending further than that of Chiaberge \ (1999) primarily because of the inclusion of the luminous broad-line objects 3C 382 and 3C 390.3.

In fact, Fig. \ alpha shows that they occupy a region which overlaps with the RBLs, and is clearly distinguishable from the region occupied by X-ray selected BL Lacs (XBLs); the only two objects to overlap with the XBLs are the broad-line radio galaxies 3C 382 and 3C 390.3.

gmw_00000019.name: M82
gmw_00000019.overview: M82 is a nearby, highly inclined starburst galaxy [arXiv:2201.02881] with an irregular morphology [arXiv:0906.0757] and a central bar [arXiv:2302.14800]. Driven by tidal interactions with M81 [arXiv:1002.0456, arXiv:1903.03767], it hosts a multiphase galactic wind [arXiv:1507.01951, arXiv:2401.16648] and exhibits intense star formation [arXiv:1107.4988, arXiv:2401.16648].

sources.summary: The paper detects molecular emission in M82, placing it in the eastern molecular peak and central region, and interprets its chemistry as an evolved starburst with enhanced cosmic-ray flux.

quotes.quote: At a distance of 4 Mpc, M82 is the prototype evolved starburst, with a large \ mass, where many molecules were seen for the first time outside our Galaxy, including , a tracer of strong irradiation of molecular gas fuente:co+ . The molecular emission can be roughly divided into two prominent lobes of molecular emission, separated by 30 '' , which is interpreted as a 400 pc rotating molecular ring. There is also molecular gas in the nucleus, which is best seen in high- J CO lines or in high-density tracer emission, such as HCO (e.g., garcia-burillo:m82 ).

In the case of M82, the emission peak at 300 \ suggests an origin in the eastern molecular peak at about 12 '' (240 pc) from the nucleus, which is where the CO emission peaks tilanus:m82 and where \ and \ are also located fuente:co+,martin:meth . The profile almost extends to 200 , which suggests that significant \ is also present in the central region of the galaxy, as found before for \ mauersberger:n2h+ . In fact, a single Gaussian profile does not give a good fit to the M82 spectrum. Figure  f:spec shows a decomposition of the profile into two Gaussians, where the narrow component represents \ in the eastern molecular peak of M82 and the broad component either the more widely-distributed molecular gas or the contribution from the nucleus. This decomposition of the line profile may not be unique, but serves to illustrate the existence of multiple \ components in the nucleus of M82.

sources.summary: The paper discusses M82's nuclear molecular gas, arguing its detected line components trace dense gas structures and that its ionization likely comes from starburst ultraviolet light and cosmic rays.

quotes.quote: Using the new 16-pixel HARP imaging spectrometer on the James Clerk Maxwell Telescope, line emission at a rest frequency of 364 GHz was detected towards the prototypical starburst galaxy M82 and the prototypical ultraluminous merger Arp 220. The derived \ abundances and / \ ratios imply very high ionization rates for the dense molecular gas in the nuclei of these two galaxies. Chemical models meijerink:grid indicate that the origin of this high ionization is irradiation by X-rays in the case of Arp 220, whereas for M82, a combination of ultraviolet light and cosmic rays is needed. The high ionization rates of these galactic nuclei make magnetic fields more effective in retarding their gravitational collapse and the formation of stars. The origin of the irradiation in M82 is the evolved starburst schreiber:m82 , whereas for Arp 220, an AGN may be needed, as claimed before by downes:arp220 .

The velocities and widths of the two components of the \ line profile observed toward M82 may be used to constrain the location of the gas. Observations of CO emission lines toward the M82 nucleus show a flattened structure of size 50 20 '' (1000 400 pc) with emission peaks on its north-eastern and south-western ends. The velocity field is well described by a monotonic gradient along the major axis, so that the structure is probably a rotating ring or torus.

sources.summary: The review presents M82 as a nearby starburst galaxy with galactic superwinds, irregular morphology, and interaction-triggered activity tied to M81 and NGC3077.

quotes.quote: Located at only a distance of 3.63 Mpc (image scale 17.6 pc arcsec ^ 1 ; Freedman et al. 1994), M82 is one of the most observed objects. The nuclear starburst and the associated galactic superwinds along with the filamentary distribution of dust, not only give it a visually appealing morphology, but also make it an interesting object of study in the entire range of electromagnetic spectrum. It is one of the brightest infrared objects in the sky. Historically, M 82 was given a morphological class of Irr II by Holm50 , I0 by deVa59 and ``Amorphous'' by Sand79 . It was referred to as an exploding galaxy by San64 due to the discovery of high velocity H filaments that were thought to be ejected from the nucleus.

The discovery of a bridge of intergalactic gas connecting M82 to its neighbor M 81 and subsequent N-body simulations suggest that the starburst in M82 is triggered by an encounter between M82 and NGC3077, both members of the M 81 group Gott77, Yun93 . Though there exist innumerable studies on M82, a vast majority of them discuss the nuclear starburst or a phenomenon related to the starburst activity. However, interest in M82 as a galaxy is growing in recent years.

sources.summary: The paper uses M 82 as a nearby starburst benchmark, describing its central CO SED, low- and high-excitation components, and outflow-driven whole-galaxy CO behavior to justify a single-component LTE model.
