This is particularly suggested by the detection of methanol in M 82 with abundances well above that expected by pure gas-phase chemistry, which is an evidence of the presence of a significant amount of molecular material shielded from the pervading UV radiation and similar to that found in other starbursts galaxies such as NGC 253 or Maffei 2 Martin06a .

Within this sequence, NGC 253 and M 82 would represent the extreme evolutionary states. Thus NGC 253 appears to be in an early stage with huge amounts of dense molecular gas feeding the ongoing burst of star formation where the dominant chemical fingerprints we observe are those of the shocks affecting this material. On the other side, M 82, with a similar star forming rate, has consumed most of its molecular gas reservoir and its ISM shows the traces of the photodissociation produced by the young hot stars formed during star burst period.

sources.summary: VERITAS observed M 82 as a prototype starburst galaxy and detected point-like very-high-energy gamma-ray emission from its dense star-forming environment.

quotes.quote: The importance of finding new types of extragalactic VHE emitters motivated the VERITAS observations of the M 82 starburst galaxy. M 82 is a prototype starburst galaxy where the interaction of a group of galaxies produces a very active starburst region. In this region, a high cosmic ray density, as inferred from radio synchrotron emission , is believed to result from the high star formation and supernova (SNR) rate.

M 82 also contains a high mean gas density of 150 particles/cm ^2 . The VERITAS M 82 data set, taken in dark time between 2007 and 2009, constitute a very deep exposure of 137 h. The excess counts map (see Figure 2) is consistent with a point source at the position of M 82.

sources.summary: The paper observes M 82's nuclear starburst ISM with CCH and CO isotopologues, deriving a ^12C/^13C lower limit of 138 and an oxygen isotopic ratio above 350.

quotes.quote: El Golf 40, Piso 18, Las Condes, Santiago, Chile To derive carbon isotopic ratios from optically thin tracers in the central regions of the starburst galaxies M 82 and NGC 253. We present high sensitivity observations of CCH and two of its ^ 13 C isotopologues, C ^ 13 CH and ^ 13 CCH, as well as the optically thin emission from C ^ 18 O and ^ 13 C ^ 18 O. We assume the column density ratio between isotopologues is representative of the ^ 12 C/ ^ 13 C isotopic ratio. From CCH, lower limits to the ^ 12 C/ ^ 13 C isotopic ratio of 138 in M 82, and 81 in NGC 253, are derived.

Lower limits to the ^ 12 C/ ^ 13 C ratios from CO isotopologues support these. ^ 13 C ^ 18 O is tentatively detected in NGC 253, which is the first reported detection in the extragalactic ISM. Based on these limits, we infer ratios of ^ 16 O/ ^ 18 O >350 and >300 in M 82 and NGC 253, respectively, and ^ 32 S/ ^ 34 S >16 in NGC 253.

sources.summary: The review describes M82's nucleus as a star-forming system where Herschel spectra show unexpectedly strong water-ion absorption, high relative abundance, and chemistry implying much atomic hydrogen or enhanced destruction.

quotes.quote: The nucleus of the galaxy M82 is well known for its high rate of star formation. To measure the abundance of \ in M82, weiss:m82 have obtained spectra of the \ 1_ 10 1_ 01 , 1_ 11 0_ 00 and 2_ 02 1_ 11 transitions with the high-resolution HIFI spectrometer onboard Herschel. Unexpectedly, the spectrum near 1113 GHz shows absorption in the \ 1_ 11 0_ 00 line at 1115 GHz, which is stronger than the neighbouring \ line.

The authors infer column densities of 4 14 \ for \ and 3 14 \ for . This high relative abundance of \ is surprising as \ reacts quickly with \ to form . The observations thus indicate that a significant fraction of hydrogen is in atomic form, unless another process is reducing the \ abundance.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes M82 as a prototypical X-ray-bright starburst, separating charge-exchange and thermal contributions in He-like triplets and tying the results to its nuclear outflow.

quotes.quote: In this letter we analyze the K triplets of He-like ions of , , and \ of M82 observed by Reflection Grating Spectrometers RGS, den01 . M82 is one of the nearby brightest galaxies in X-ray and regarged as a prototypical starburst. A bipolar outflow originated from the nuclear starburst extends beyond 5 ' (about 5 kpc) from the disk e.g., Ste03 .

We analyze the triplets of , , and \ observed in the reflection grating spectra of the starburst galaxy M82. The flux contribution of the CXE is 90 , 50 , and 30 to the , , and \ triplet, respectively. Averaged over all the three triplets, the contribution of the CXE is 50 of the total observed triplet flux.

sources.summary: The paper models Messier 82 as a nearby irregular starburst, using continuum data to estimate free-free and nonthermal star-formation rates and to constrain anomalous microwave emission.

quotes.quote: Messier 82 Messier 82 ( 09^ h 55^ m 52.7^ s , +69^ 40^ m 46^ s J2000.0), also known as NGC 3034 and G141.40+40.56, is an irregular starburst galaxy at a distance of 3.3 0.5 Mpc 1988Freedman . Its optical dimensions are 11.2 4.3 arcmin 1991deVaucouleurs , and at 857 GHz it is unresolved by with a beam size of 5.3 4.2 arcmin.

The SFR within M82 can be calculated from the non-thermal synchrotron radiation by assuming an initial mass function (IMF) and supernova rate; similarly, it can also be estimated from the free-free emission by assuming stellar models. Using the approximate formulae given by 1992Condon , we find a synchrotron SFR of 2.6 based on the 1.4 GHz flux density, and a free-free SFR of 3.0 based on the best-fit free-free EM.

Based on the 100 m flux density, we would expect to see 0.36 Jy of AME at 30 GHz. Between 23 and 143 GHz inclusive, the weighted mean of the residuals is M82:residuals Jy, or less than M82:residuals:3sig Jy at 3 , indicating that there appears to be significantly less AME within M82 compared to our own Galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes M82 as a nearby true starburst galaxy detected above GeV energies, with flat gamma-ray spectra implying hadronic cosmic-ray interactions near acceleration regions.

quotes.quote: The nearest starburst galaxies M82 and NGC253 were detected above GeV energies, tracing the interaction of hadronic cosmic rays with the gas in the galaxy.

While observations of our own Galaxy indicate that propagation steepens the cosmic ray spectrum before interactions take place, the two starburst galaxies with observed gamma-ray spectra, M82 and NGC253, reveal a quite flat spectral behavior of around E^ 2.3 . Thus, it is expected that a large fraction of the cosmic ray interactions happen in the vicinity of the sources.

From Fermi- and IACT-observations, the gamma-ray flux from M82, NGC253, the Milky Way and the star-forming region 30 Doradus in the LMC are known fermi_starbursts2010 . The measurements give a unique opportunity to draw conclusions about the primary cosmic ray flux. First of all, the gamma-ray spectra for M82 and NGC253 appear to be very flat, i.e. \ E^ 2.3 and flatter.

sources.summary: The paper describes M 82 as a nearby prolific star-forming galaxy with long radio monitoring, many compact central radio sources, and a newly studied nuclear radio transient.

quotes.quote: M 82 is prolific star-forming galaxy at a distance of 3.6 Mpc 1994ApJ...427..628F . Its proximity therefore makes it an ideal place to study star formation. To this end, M 82 has been well monitored by various observatories at several wavelengths for decades e.g. 1980ApJ...235..392T, 1981ApJ...246..751K, 1988Natur.334...43B,1994MNRAS.266..455M, 1997A&A...320..378S, 2001A&A...365..571W, 2008MNRAS.391.1384F . In particular, radio observations have revealed approximately 60 compact radio sources in the central region of M 82 2002MNRAS.334..912M .

For the last 15 to 20 years M 82 has been observed at radio frequencies at intervals of six months to one year. In that time, only two other transient sources of unknown nature have been detected in the galaxy 1985Sci...227...28K, 1994MNRAS.266..455M .

sources.summary: The paper presents M 82 as a nearby interacting starburst galaxy where intense central star formation, high supernova rates, and cosmic-ray acceleration motivate VHE observations.

quotes.quote: M 82 a prototype small starburst galaxy, located approximately 3.7 Mpc from Earth, in the direction of the Ursa Major constellation. M 82 is gravitationally interacting with its nearby companion M 81. This interaction has deformed M 82 in such a way that an active starburst region in its center with a diameter of 1000 light years has been developed . Throughout this compact region stars are being formed at a rate approximately 10 times faster than in entire ``normal" galaxies like the Milky Way. Hence the supernovae rate is 0.1 to 0.3 per year .
The high star formation rate in M 82 implies the presence of numerous shock waves in supernova remnants and around massive young stars.

sources.summary: The paper studies M82 as a nearby edge-on starburst, likely an SBc barred spiral, using Herschel spectroscopy to probe its molecular gas and outflow-related emission.

quotes.quote: M82 is a nearly edge-on galaxy, notable for its spectacular bipolar outflow and high IR luminosity 5.6 10^ 10 , Sanders:2003 . Though its high inclination angle of 77 ^ makes it difficult to determine, M82 is likely a SBc barred spiral galaxy with two trailing arms Mayya:2005 . Its redshift-independent distance is about 3.4 0.2 Mpc Dalcanton:2009 , and after correcting the commonly cited redshift (0.000677, deVaucouleurs:1999 ) with WMAP-7 parameters to the 3K CMB reference frame, we find a redshift of 0.000939.

Due to its proximity, M82 is an exceptionally well-studied starburst galaxy. High star formation rates 9.8 \ , likely enhanced by interactions with M81, Yun:1993 and a large gas reservoir produce bright molecular and atomic emission lines.
As one of the brightest extragalactic submillimeter sources, M82 has been studied extensively with Herschel .

sources.summary: The paper describes M 82 as a bright evolved starburst molecular emitter whose line survey has fewer features than NGC 253 and enhanced gas-phase, UV-linked species.

quotes.quote: Their comparison of the molecular abundances of just seven species in the starburst galaxy prototypes, M 82 and NGC 253, where only three species were detected in both galaxies, showed that significant differences could be identified. Such abundance differences were attributed to the different heating mechanisms driving the chemistry in their central regions.

Although expected from previous observations, the detection rate in M 82 was significantly lower, with 72 spectral features from 18 molecular species. The combination of both line surveys resulted in the most complete comparison between the molecular composition of two extragalactic sources. Indeed, the observed chemical differences are claimed to be directly linked to the different state of evolution of their nuclear starbursts, and therefore to the different leading heating mechanisms affecting the ISM within their central few hundred parsecs. While a number of species, such as CH _3 OH, HNCO, NH _3 , SiO, NS , HOCO , and CH_2NH ,claimed to be formed in dust grains and injected into gas phase via shocks, were found to be enhanced in NGC 253, other molecules, such as CO ^+ , HCO, HOC ^+ , C_3H_2 , CH _3 CCH, and NH_2CN , mostly formed in the gas phase and likely enhanced by intense UV fields, are significantly more abundant towards M 82.

sources.summary: The paper uses M 82 as a nearby starburst example with specified magnetic-field, gas, and radiation densities, finding bremsstrahlung dominates 1 GHz electron cooling while ionization and winds also matter.

quotes.quote: The gamma-ray observations of M 82 and NGC 253 suggest that more CR proton power is advected away in their nuclear starburst winds than is lost to pion production, although the efficiency of pionic losses is still much greater than in the Milky Way Lacki11-Obs .

We consider M 82 as an example (the other nearby starburst NGC 253 has a similar environment), which has a magnetic field strength of 200 \ (from detailed models of the radio spectrum; Domingo05 ; Persic08 ; deCeaDelPozo09-M82,Rephaeli10 ), a mean gas density of 300 \ ^ 3 ( Weiss01 , assuming a scale height of 50 pc), and a radiation energy density of 1000 \ \ ^ 3 ( Sanders03 , again assuming a starburst radius of 250 pc). Of course, none of these values are constant throughout the region, and it is not known how inhomogeneity affects CRs in starbursts, but using the average values seems to work for models fitting current radio and gamma-ray data deCeaDelPozo09-Obs .

For our fiducial values of the physical parameters in M 82, we see that bremsstrahlung is the most important cooling process for 1 GHz emitting e^ , though ionization and winds are also important. However, the final e^ lifetime is t_ cool 39 \ , shorter than even the bremsstrahlung losses. We can quantify the importance of other losses relative to bremsstrahlung with the ratio X = t_ brems t_ e . For our fiducial M 82 values, X = 2.6 at 1 GHz; other losses are even less important.

sources.summary: The paper applies its SPIRE FTS semi-extended correction to M82, modeling the core with an exponential profile and comparing corrected CO line fluxes against photometry-map-based corrections.

quotes.quote: M82 M82 is a nearly edge-on starburst galaxy at 3.4 Mpc distance from the Milky-way  dalcanton-apjs-2009 . The FTS observed M82 in fully sampled mode on the 2010-11-08 (OD 543; ObsID 1342208388). Details of results from the fully sampled map of M82 can be found in kamenezky-apj-2012 . Here, we apply the correction to the spectrum of M82 taken with the central FTS detectors, SLWC3 and SSWD4, at J2000 coordinates, ( , )=(9 h 55 m 52.6 s , +69 d 40 m 48.02 s ) and compare it to the beam size corrected data taken at the same coordinates with the central detectors  kamenezky-apj-2012 .

Assuming the intensity distribution at the core of M82 can be described as a 2-D circular exponential profile, B() = exp(-/r_ e ) ( W \ m^ 2 \ sr^ 1 \ Hz^ 1 ) we correct the spectrum of SLWC3 and SSWD4 by matching the fluxes at 959< <989  GHz with varying scale radius ( 8''<r_ e <14'' ). The best-fit r_ e determined by minimizing ^ 2 , defined in Equation  size_chisq , is 11'' , which corresponds to _ D 15'' in FWHM.

sources.summary: The paper models M82 as a prototypical starburst core, comparing its conditions with NGC 253 and Arp 220 and predicting strong magnetic fields from supernova-driven turbulence.

quotes.quote: Throughout this work, I compare my results to four prototypical starburst regions: the Galactic Center Central Molecular Zone (GCCMZ), the starburst cores of NGC 253 and M82, and the starburst nuclei of Arp 220. The conditions in these regions span those of z = 0 starburst regions. In Table table:BasicProperties , I list the basic properties that I use in computations for these regions. These regions are relatively well studied. Models of the nonthermal emission from each has constrained the magnetic field strength and CR energy densities, and all but Arp 220 have gamma-ray detections. All have star-formation surface densities above the ``Heckman threshold" of 0.1 \ \ ^ 1 \ ^ 2 .f., Heckman90 .
I predict magnetic field strengths of 70 \ in the Galactic Center starburst, 300 \ in M82 and NGC 253, and 2 \ in Arp 220's nuclei.

sources.summary: The paper describes M82 as a nearby archetypal starburst galaxy shaped by interaction with M81, hosting a bar and recent nuclear starburst where the studied clusters reside.

quotes.quote: M82 is the archetype nearby 3.6 Mpc, 1'' = 17.5 pc; freedman94 starburst galaxy oconnell78, oconnell95 . The current ( 10 Myr) starburst activity is concentrated in a 500 pc ( 30 '' ) region centred on the nucleus. From Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ) imaging, the starburst is known to consist of a number of prominent, high surface-brightness clumps, first identified and labelled by oconnell78 .
M82 is a dynamically complex system, perturbed significantly by its gravitational encounter with M81 some 2 10^8 yrs ago yun_ho_lo93 .
M82 hosts a 1 kpc long stellar bar, known from near-infrared and H \ studies larkin94, achtermann95, wills00 . This may also have formed as a consequence of the M81-M82 interaction e.g. barnes96 .

sources.summary: The paper describes M82 as a local starburst with multiphase outflows, argues current kpc-scale dusty gas is not explained by shell-like radiation pressure, and notes a brighter past starburst.

quotes.quote: The Local Starburst M82: M82 has an extensively studied outflow, with evidence for 200-600 km s ^ 1 line-emitting gas and dust on kpc scales, a molecular outflow on small scales, and hot nuclear X-ray emission (e.g., shopbell,walter,strickland_heckman ). Using the dust-scattered UV emission from the central starburst hoopes , calculated the Eddington ratio for dusty gas on 0.5-5 kpc scales. Using the large-scale rotation curve from , found that although significantly more UV escapes the starburst along the minor axis than along our line of sight, the Eddington ratio is still much less than unity on large scales.

These results imply that a shell-like radiation pressure-driven outflow cannot account for the dusty gas currently seen on kpc scales. Moreover, this result highlights the fact that the additional large-scale acceleration that produces the high velocities discussed here may not be generic, but may only occur in special circumstances or geometries. One mitigating factor is that find that the bolometric luminosity of the M82 starburst was a factor of 4 larger 6 Myr ago, indicating that the system may have potentially approached the single-scattering Eddington limit on scales within the starburst _ SS 1 for M(<300 pc )=10^9 M _ , M_ sh =10^8 M _ , R=300 pc and L=2 10^ 11 L _ (see eq. gammass ).

sources.summary: The paper uses SN 2014J polarimetry to probe M82, finding that interstellar dust in the starburst galaxy causes unusual polarization and differs from Galactic dust.

quotes.quote: We presented optical and near-infrared multi-band linear polarimetry of the highly reddened Type Ia SN 2014J appeared in M82. SN 2014J exhibits large polarization at shorter wavelengths, e.g., 4.8 in B band, and the polarization decreases rapidly at longer wavelengths, with the position angle of the polarization remaining at approximately 40^ over the observed wavelength range. These polarimetric properties suggest that the observed polarization is likely to be caused predominantly by the interstellar dust within M82.

Further analysis shows that the polarization peaks at a wavelengths much shorter than those obtained for the Galactic dust. The wavelength dependence of the polarization can be better described by an inverse power law rather than by Serkowski law for Galactic interstellar polarization. These suggests that the nature of the dust in M82 may be different from that in our Galaxy, with polarizing dust grains having a mean radius of <0.1 \ m .

sources.summary: The paper maps M82 dense molecular gas, connecting HCN and HCO+ emission to its molecular disk, central starburst, and starburst-driven outflow, with lower limits on dense outflowing gas.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: centered on =09^ h 55^ m 53.00^ s , =+69 40 47 (J2000), the radio center of this galaxy

quotes.quote: Kepley National Radio Astronomy Observatory, 520 Edgemont Road, Charlottesville, VA 22903-2475, USA We mapped 3 mm continuum and line emission from the starburst galaxy M82 using the Combined Array for Research in Millimeter-wave Astronomy.

The molecular gas in M82 had been previously found to be distributed in a molecular disk, coincident with the central starburst, and a galactic scale outflow which originates in the central starburst. With the new short spacings-corrected maps we derive some of the properties of the dense molecular gas in the base of the outflow. From the HCN and \ J =(1-0) line emission, and under the assumptions of the gas being optically thin and in local thermodynamic equilibrium, we place lower limits to the amount of dense molecular gas in the base of the outflow.

The kinematics and spatial distribution of the dense gas outside the central starburst suggests that it is being expelled through chimneys. Assuming a constant outflow velocity, the derived outflow rate of dense molecular gas is 0.3 , which would lower the starburst lifetime by 5 .

sources.summary: The paper presents subarcsecond LOFAR imaging of M82, resolving compact low-frequency sources and diffuse outflow-related emission in its nuclear starburst region.

quotes.quote: The international LOFAR telescope was used to observe M82 at 110-126 MHz and 146-162 MHz. Images were obtained using standard techniques from very long baseline interferometry. images were obtained at each frequency range: one only using international baselines, and one only using the longest Dutch (remote) baselines. The 154 MHz image obtained using international baselines is a new imaging record in terms of combined image resolution (0.3 '' ) and sensitivity ( =0.15 mJy/beam) at low frequencies ( <327 MHz). We detected 16 objects at 154 MHz, six of these also at 118 MHz.
The images obtained using remote baselines show diffuse emission, associated with the outflow in M82, with reduced brightness in the region of the edge-on star-forming disk.

sources.summary: The paper uses SN 2014J spectra to describe M82 as a nearby dusty irregular galaxy with disk-side geometry and distinct molecular and atomic interstellar gas components.

quotes.quote: SN Ia 2014J was discovered in the dusty irregular galaxy M82 in mid-January 2014. With a discovery magnitude of R=10.5 and a distance of just 3.5 Mpc, SN 2014J was accessible to a wider variety of telescopes and instruments than most SNe. Initial observations found that SN 2014J exhibited high-velocity spectral features ( v 16,000 km s ^ 1 ) and significant extinction ( A_V=2.5 1.3 mag; Goobar et al. 2014). This makes SN 2014J a useful case study for potential CSM and as a probe of the ISM of M82.

They note that the bulk of the H 1 emission (from radio data) along the line of sight to SN 2014J is at a velocity of 70 km s ^ 1 with respect to the rest frame of M82, and optical spectra presented by Westmoquette et al. (2009) shows that the approaching side of the disk of M82 is southwest of its center. This means that SN 2014J is on the approaching side of the disk. Welty et al. and Ritchey et al. model the absorption features as multiple components with a velocity resolution of 9.5 km s ^ 1 , and find that compositional diversity between dust clouds in M82 can be broadly categorized in two groups: molecular gas that may be significantly shielded or experiencing a weaker radiation field, and atomic gas with either less shielding or a stronger ambient radiation field. The molecular gas has velocities from 70 to 157 km s ^ 1 with respect to the rest frame of M82 (consistent with the bulk of H 1 ), while the atomic gas is >-70 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper uses M82 as a local starburst benchmark for radiatively cooling winds, citing its compact star-forming region, mass loading, hard X-ray constraints, and possible complications from interaction-driven halo gas.

quotes.quote: The choice of parameters for scaling the relations above (and below) is motivated in part by M82, which has R 250 pc and M _ 5-10 M _ yr ^ 1 (e.g., rieke,forster2001,forster2003 ). In addition, from X-ray observations M82 has been inferred to have 1 and 0.2-0.5 strickland_heckman . These values of M _ and R are also roughly appropriate for the individual massive star-forming clumps seen in high redshift galaxies genzel_clump .

The story of M82 may be more complicated because the wind has likely overrun halo gas from the tidal interaction with M81, or because it simply does not fit with the model of a radiatively cooling wind discussed here. Work by indicates weak cooling on large scales. In addition, found that 1 and 0.2-0.5 in M82 based on the diffuse hard X-ray emission observed in its core.

sources.summary: The paper finds young stellar arcs and an extended disturbed RGB halo around M82, with its outer halo bluer and more metal-poor than the inner halo.

quotes.quote: Our survey also reveals for the first time the very extended ( 2 ) halos of RGB stars around M81, M82 and NGC 3077, as well as faint tidal streams that link these systems. The halos of M82 and NGC 3077 exhibit highly disturbed morphologies, presumably a consequence of the recent gravitational encounter and their ongoing disruption. While the halos of M81, NGC 3077 and the inner halo of M82 have the similar colors, the outer halo of M82 is significantly bluer indicating it is more metal-poor.

In M82, a prominent stellar feature can be seen at ( , ) (0.1,0.6) , identified as an arc by Sun2005ApJ and Davidge2008ApJ . MSs are also distributed well beyond the radius up to the projected distance of about 16 kpc from M82. The halo of M82 has a color gradient from the inner greener area to the outer bluer region; the greener part is also extended toward the northeast and along the direction of the young stellar arc.

sources.summary: The review presents M 82 as a northern starburst galaxy detected by VERITAS, emphasizing its faint gamma-ray flux and the background rejection needed for detection.

quotes.quote: Starburst galaxies also form part of the extragalactic observing program for VERITAS. The emission here is thought to be due to interactions of a very dense cosmic ray population with interstellar gas and radiation. Only two such galaxies have been detected by IACTs; M 82 in the north M82 (Fig. M87 ) and NGC 253 in the south NGC253 .

The VERITAS detection of M 82 highlights the exceptional background rejection capabilities of IACTs: during 137 hours of observations, over 95 million events were recorded. Following the gamma-ray selection cuts, 99.9997 of the cosmic ray background events were removed, leaving an excess of less than one gamma-ray photon per hour. The corresponding flux is below 1 of the steady flux from the Crab Nebula.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Swift/XRT monitoring of M82 and argues that the reported 62-day X-ray periodicity likely comes from unresolved periodic sources southeast of M82 X-1.

quotes.quote: M82 X-1 is the brightest ultraluminous X-ray source in starburst galaxy M82 and is one of the best intermediate mass black hole candidates. Previous studies based on the Rossi X-ray Timing Explorer /Proportional Counter Array ( /PCA) reported a regular X-ray flux modulation of M82 with a period of 62 days, and attributed this periodic modulation to M82 X-1. However, this modulation is not necessarily from M82 X-1 because /PCA has a very poor spatial resolution of 1^ .

In this work, we analyzed 1000 days of monitoring data of M82 X-1 from the Swift /X-ray telescope (XRT), which has a much better spatial resolution than /PCA. The periodicity distribution map of M82 reveals that the 62-day periodicity is most likely not from M82 X-1, but from the summed contributions of several periodic X-ray sources 4 southeast of M82 X-1. However, /XRT is not able to resolve those periodic sources and locate the precise origin of the periodicity of M82.

sources.summary: The paper models M82 radio and gamma-ray spectra, finding high magnetic field strengths, higher acceleration efficiency, high cosmic-ray energy density, and departure from equipartition.

quotes.quote: Starburst Galaxies: M82, NGC 253, Arp 220 Using the updated version of the YEGZ models, we have re-worked our analysis of the radio and ray spectra for M82 and NGC 253 and have also applied the models to the radio spectrum for the nuclei of Arp 220. Our previous results for M82 and NGC 253 are consistent with the newer models, where the best-fitting magnetic field strengths range from 200--250 G (NGC 253) and 250--350 G (M82) YoastHull13,YoastHull14 .

Though the observed ray spectra for M82 and NGC 253 are quite similar, we find that a slightly larger acceleration efficiency ( = 0.2 ; or equivalently a somewhat higher supernova rate) is required for M82 and a slightly smaller acceleration efficiency ( = 0.05 ) is necessary to fit NGC 253. Thus, for M82 U_ CR ranges from 420 to 620 eV cm ^ 3 and from 260 to 350 eV cm ^ 3 for NGC 253 (see Table 2, Figure 2).

We also estimated the CR energy density for M82, as a check of the method, and calculated an energy density of 550 eV cm ^ 3 , which is within the range of our results from the YEGZ models. Intense Star Formation and Equipartition? Taking the ratio of U_ B to U_ CR from our models, M82 diverges from equipartition by a factor of 2.5 to 7 and NGC 253 by a factor of 2.9 to 5.9 (see Figure 2, Table 2).

sources.summary: The paper uses M82 as an observed starburst-wind benchmark, citing its magnetic-field models, limited wind fuel supply, high mass loading, rapid depletion, and fast outflows.

quotes.quote: Let's make a generous estimate of this CR energy assuming the magnetic field strength in a galaxy at a radius of 200 pc is 300 G. This galactic magnetic field value is consistent with models of M82 developed by . Assuming the radial magnetic field component drops off as 1/^ 2 , while the azimuthal component will drop off as 1/ , then at 200 kpc, the azimuthal component should dominate the radial component.

M82, as an example, contains about 10^ 8 M_ of stellar mass and has a wind mass-loading factor of order 10 M_ /yr in the central starburst region ( , ), meaning that a wind with this strength could only last for approximately 0.1 Gyrs until it exhausts the internal mass supply of M82 ( ). New high resolution images of M82 suggest an even higher outflow rate that could deplete the galaxy of molecular gas within 8 Myrs ( . Overall, this suggests that wind lifetimes are more realistically on the order of 100 Myrs, much shorter than the lifetimes of galaxies, which are on the order of Gyrs.

sources.summary: The paper uses M82 as the observational template for starburst-wind simulations, citing its hot plasma, gas-rich disk, starburst-region size, current feedback estimates, and clustered star formation.

quotes.quote: The Chevalier85 model has been used to successfully explain numerous observations of starburst galaxies, particularly the hot plasma at the center of the nearby nuclear starburst galaxy, M82 Strickland09 . Despite its utility as a model for the hot phase of a galactic outflow, the Chevalier85 model does not address potentially large energy losses due to radiative cooling, limiting its applicability once the hot wind has reached a radius at which these physical processes become dynamically important.

Our initial conditions are chosen to create a disk galaxy loosely modeled after the gas-rich nearby starburst, M82. The galaxy contains M_ gas = 2.5 10^9 M _ of gas Greco12 , distributed in an isothermal disk ( T_ gas = 10^4 K ), with an exponential surface density profile defined by the scale radius, R_ gas = 1.6 kpc . The pressure gradient in the radial direction is balanced by the gas rotation. We artificially truncate the gas disk at R = 4.5 kpc in order to reduce potential boundary effects from the simulation box. The disk is in vertical hydrostatic equilibrium with a static gravitational potential that consists of a Miyamoto-Nagai disk Miyamoto75 plus a spherically-symmetric NFW dark matter halo Navarro96 . The disk potential is set by the stellar mass, M_ stars = 10^ 10 M _ Greco12 , scale radius, R_ stars = 0.8 kpc Mayya09 , and scale height, z_ stars = 0.

We choose 300 pc because that radius was demonstrated via 2D hydrodynamic simulations to produce a similar wind solution to a disk injection region with radius R = 750 pc Strickland09 , a good fit to the size of the starburst region in M82. The mass and energy are distributed equally at each time step over the volume of all the cells within the spherical volume V = 4 R^3/3 . For the first half of each simulation, we choose our energy and mass injection rates, E and M , such that we expect to see a cooling radius within 10 kpc . In particular, from 5 to 40 Myr , we assume a star formation rate M _ SFR = 20 M_ yr ^ 1 , a mass-loading factor = 0.6 , and energy-loading factor = 0.9 . The mass-loading rate therefore assumes that the hot wind incorporates approximately 2-3 times the expected return from supernovae and stellar winds alone, which for a continuous star formation model are expected to yield an M 0.25 M _ SFR Leitherer99 . The energy-loading rate assumes that 90 of the energy from supernovae is thermalized in the hot gas driving the wind, where we have assumed there is one 10^ 51 erg supernova per 100 M_ of stars formed. Thus, at early times, our mass injection rate is M = 12 M_ yr ^ 1 , and our energy injection rate is E = 5.4 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 . Using these parameters in Equation eqn:r_cool gives a cooling radius of r_ cool = 2.77 kpc , assuming a spherical outflow with opening angle = 4 and a mean molecular weight = 0.6 , as appropriate for fully-ionized solar-metallicity gas. After 40 Myr , we ramp down the feedback, reducing the assumed star formation rate to M _ SFR = 5 M_ yr ^ 1 , the mass-loading to = 0.3 , and we assume all of the supernova energy is thermalized, = 1.0 . Thus, our mass and energy injection rates at late times are given by M = 1.5 M_ yr ^ 1 and E = 1.5 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 . With these parameters, the advection time for the hot wind to reach a radius of 10 kpc is t_ adv 5.5 Myr , which is much shorter than the cooling time, t_ cool 93 Myr , so we do not expect to see a cooling radius in the hot wind during the low mass-loading state. These two states are designed to roughly mimic a picture in which some event (perhaps a merger) brings in a large quantity of gas to the galaxy, and the high surface density drives a nuclear starburst. At early times, when there is still plenty of gas in the center, the mass-loading rate is higher, whereas at later times, much of the gas has been consumed by star formation or already driven out in a wind, so the mass-loading is lower. Our particular choices for , , and SFR at late times are based on the observed estimates for M82 at the present day Strickland09 . However, this is just one possible picture - our goal in this paper is not to elucidate the small-scale processes that set and , but to test the effects on the the large-scale hot wind that results from a given combination of and . The central feedback model described above is valuable for comparison to the 1D analytic models, and may even be a reasonable approximation of very concentrated nuclear starburst systems. However, star formation is generally expected to proceed in a highly clustered manner. In M82, star clusters associated with the most recent burst of star formation are observed within the central kiloparsec of the disk Mayya08 .

sources.summary: The paper maps M82 and finds weak HCN relative to infrared emission, low HCN/HCO+ ratios, possible subsolar-metallicity effects, and lower gas density than NGC 253.

quotes.quote: Keck Observatory, 65-1120 Mamalahoa Hwy, Kamuela, HI 96743, USA Ying-He Zhao Yunnan Observatories, Chinese Academy of Sciences, Kunming 650011, China Ming Zhu National Astronomical Observatories, Chinese Academy of Sciences, Beijing 100012, China We present \ and \ maps of six nearby star-forming galaxies, NGC 253, NGC 1068, IC 342, M82, M83, and NGC 6946, obtained with the James Clerk Maxwell Telescope as part of the MALATANG survey. All galaxies were mapped in the central 2 2 region at 14 (FWHM) resolution (corresponding to linear scales of 0.2-1.0 kpc).

We discuss possible explanations for these trends in the next subsection. htbp f6a.eps f6b.eps Compared with other galaxies in our sample, M82 appear weakened in HCN(4-3) relative to IR with ratio of IR/HCN(4-3) mostly above 1 scatter (see Fig. fig:scatter (left)), while the IR/HCO ^+ (4-3) ratio shown in Fig. fig:scatter (right) is well within the 1 scatter. A plausible explanation for the decrease of L'_ HCN(4-3) /L'_ HCO^+(4-3) could be a low HCN abundance in M82. braine17 observed various molecular lines in low-metallicity Local Group galaxies and found that both HCN and HNC lines are weak with respect to the IR emission, while HCO ^+ follows the trends observed in galaxies with solar metallicity. They attributed the weakness of the nitrogen bearing molecules to the low nitrogen abundance in these galaxies, based on the observed trend in the HCN/HCO ^+ ratio with metallicity. The weak \ emission observed in M82 may be a similar effect, as there is some evidence of sub-solar metallicity for this galaxy e.g., origlia04,nagao11 . Another possible explanation is related to the relatively low gas density observed in M82.

sources.summary: The paper describes M82 as a starburst interacting with M81, notable for long-running radio monitoring that found multiple unidentified transients and an unusual transient event rate.

quotes.quote: An excellent example of the targeting technique is demonstrated by the discovery of three unidentified radio transients in the starburst galaxy M82 star formation rate (SFR) of around 3.6 M_ yr ^ 1 ; see and references therein grimm03 , which were discovered via frequent and ongoing radio monitoring that was running from the 1980s until 2010 kronberg85,muxlow94,mcdonald02,fenech08,muxlow10,gendre13 . These transients were detected between 1981 and 2009, indicating a minimum occurrence rate of 1 every 10 years muxlow10 . One of these transients, 43.78+59.3 as named by muxlow10 , switched on and rose to its maximum radio luminosity within 8 days, increasing in flux by a factor of 5, where it remained stable in flux for at least 19 months gendre13 .

Several classifications were suggested by muxlow10 , including a faint SN or a second SMBH from a galaxy that merged with M82 sometime in its past. However, the most likely classification is a stellar mass accreting system muxlow10,joseph11 . M82 is an unusual galaxy due to its recent interaction history so may have an atypical transient event rate for example, a study of the compact radio sources within the central kpc of M82 identified 30 SN remnants and 16 H II regions; mcdonald02 .

sources.summary: The paper models the nuclear starburst of M82, predicting a young-cluster mass function that reproduces observations down to the completeness limit.

quotes.quote: As an example of a starburst environment in an external galaxy with a well-sampled mass function, we show in the right panel of Figure fig:CMF_CMZ the observed young CMF in the central starburst region of M82, along with the predictions of our model. To obtain the predictions in the nuclear region, we use the median inferred molecular gas column density = 500 2 from Kamenetzky12 , an angular velocity of = 0.23 ^ 1 , and a gas velocity dispersion of _ ISM = 60 . The angular velocity is calculated for the 450 central region using the M82 mass model from Martini18 . The ISM velocity dispersion was calculated in the same region using the map of CO velocity dispersion in Fig. 5 of Leroy15 . We show the CMF obtained by Mayya08 for clusters in the nuclear region with ages 8 .
The CMF prediction reproduces the observed mass function of young clusters in M82 down to the completeness limit of the observations, M 2 10^4 , which lies above the theoretical minimum cluster mass.

sources.summary: The paper uses M82 as the host of the studied disk super star clusters, describing its nearby distance, rich cluster population, disk-wide burst, halted star formation, and likely interaction trigger.

quotes.quote: M82 is an excellent candidate to carry out such a study, as it is relatively nearby 3.63 Mpc Freedman , and has a rich population of clusters in its nucleus and disk Mayyacat . Spectroscopic ages have been obtained for around 40 of the disk SSCs. The derived ages occupy a relatively narrow range between 50--300 Myr Konst2009 . Mayya2006 suggested, based on the analysis of the photometric, dynamical, and chemical properties, that the entire galaxy participated in a disk-wide burst of duration of a few hundred million years.

The disk stopped forming stars around 50 Myr ago, which is well supported by the absence of red supergiants in its disk Davidge2008 . The disk-wide burst, and the formation of disk clusters, were most likely triggered by the interaction of M82 with its neighbours M 81 and NGC 3077 Yun1999 .

sources.summary: The paper uses M82 as a nearby starburst application case, deriving its UV field from a DustPedia SED and finding predominantly ionized PAHs, stronger excitation than NGC 253, and average PAH size 86.

quotes.quote: M82 and NGC 253 are the brightest prototypes of nearby starburst galaxies located at similar distances ( 3.5 Mpc; Dalcanton2009 ; Jacobs2009 ), with comparable IR luminosities but different chemical compositions Martin2009b . For M82, we calculated the average UV photon energy from its modeled spectral energy distribution (SED). The M82 SED decomposition and fitting was performed with the SED-fitting code cigale Boquien2019 and was obtained from the DustPedia Davies2017 archive http://dustpedia.astro.noa.gr/ http://dustpedia.astro.noa.gr/ .

In terms of charge state, both M82 and NGC 253 are located in between the N25C75 and pure cationic tracks, indicating highly ionized environments where PAHs exist predominantly in their cationic state (Fig. fig:multigrid ). Specifically, NGC 253 resides closer to the N25C75 track while M82 is located closer to the track of pure cationic PAHs, suggesting less intense excitation mechanisms for NGC 253 compared to M82.
With respect to the PAH size distribution, M82 has an average PAH size of 86 11 and for NGC 253 the average PAH size is estimated to be 123 25 .

sources.summary: The paper observes M82 in 850 micron polarized light and interprets its magnetic field as poloidal in the superwind and spiral-arm-aligned or toroidal in the galaxy plane.

quotes.quote: We observed the starburst galaxy M82 in 850 m polarised light with the POL-2 polarimeter on the James Clerk Maxwell Telescope (JCMT). We interpret our observed polarisation geometry as tracing a two-component magnetic field: a poloidal component aligned with the galactic `superwind', extending to a height 350 pc above and below the central bar; and a spiral-arm-aligned, or possibly toroidal, component in the plane of the galaxy, which dominates the 850 m polarised light distribution at galactocentric radii 2 kpc. Comparison of our results with recent HAWC+ measurements of the field in the dust entrained by the M82 superwind suggests that the superwind breaks out from the central starburst at 350 pc above the plane of the galaxy.

M82 is a nearby (3.5 Mpc; jacobs2009 ) edge-on starburst galaxy, interacting with its neighbour M81. It has a bipolar `superwind' emanating from its central region e.g. shopbell1998 . M82 is classed as irregular, but nonetheless has a disc with a well-identified inclination angle of 76.9^ clark2018 , and a 1-kpc-long bar telesco1991 from which weak m=1 logarithmic spiral arms emanate mayya2005 .

sources.summary: The paper uses M82 as its reference starburst galaxy, adopting M82-like core, halo, star-formation, and supernova parameters and comparing simulated outflows to X-ray observations.

quotes.quote: These parameters are appropriate for star-forming galaxies resembling the nearby starburst galaxy M82.

The radius of the starburst region is set to be r_ sb =250 \ pc , a value similar to that estimated for the M82 starburst core see deGrijs2001 . The magnetic field strength of the starburst region is set to B_0=50 \ G in the fiducial case. This strength is comparable to estimates of M82 Klein1988 .

Comparison with observations Simulations in this work have adopted parameters appropriate for M82-like galaxies. We therefore consider M82 as a reference for comparison with observations. M82 was observed in X-rays on many occasions e.g.

sources.summary: The paper treats M82 as a nearby galaxy with Planck halo temperature asymmetry, estimating halo rotation of 100-345 km s^1 and noting complex outer dynamics.

quotes.quote: Planck data has shown the existence of a temperature asymmetry towards the halo of several nearby galaxies, such as M31, NGC 5128, M33, M81 and M82. However, the cause of this asymmetry is an open problem. A possibility to explain the observed effect relies on the presence of ``cold gas clouds'' populating the galactic halos, which may be the answer to the so-called missing baryon problem.

For the M81 galaxy v_ rot turns out to be in the range 250-450 km s^ 1 while for M82 it is between 100 km s^ 1 and 345 km s^ 1 . In particular, in the case of M82, the halo rotation velocity (which, in our model, is assumed to be fixed and equal to that observed for the galactic disk, that is certainly a simplified assumption) seems to change sign in the outer galactic region, outside about 30-60 kpc from the galactic center. Actually, the signs of a complex behavior of the M82 dynamics were already present in the literature and interpreted as possibly resulting from the interaction of M82 with the M81 and/or NGC 3077 galaxies in the past .

sources.summary: The paper uses M82 as the realistic background and benchmark for nProFit tests, describing it as a nearby prototype starburst with high crowding, strong gradients, many clusters, and high inclination.

quotes.quote: We used an image in F555W filter extracted from the HST Legacy Survey, provided by the Hubble Heritage Team, of the prototype starburst galaxy M82, which is a complex study case, due to its high crowding, high inclination angle and background gradient. In Fig fig:mock_sample , we show a simulated image containing 105 clusters along with the zoom on sub-images around 5 of the clusters in the mock sample (the background of the mock sample has considerable gradient).

We used an image from the HST Legacy Survey made publicly available by the Hubble Heritage Team Mutchler2007 corresponding to the prototype starburst galaxy M82, located at a distance of 3.63 Mpc Freedman . Such a galaxy represents an interest study case, ideal to determine the extents of the code, considering that it has a strong background gradient, a large number of clusters along its disk and nucleus (around 600) Mayyacat and a high inclination degree 77 ^ Mayya2005 . In addition to the realistic background conditions, the mock sample spans a wide range of central surface brightness values (spanning around 4.5 mag/arcsec ^2 , from 18.5 mag/arcsec ^2 to 14 mag/arcsec ^2 , in 5 bins), initially set to test the accuracy of the code and its dependence on the surface brightness profiles of the clusters. In Fig. fig:mock_sample , we show the mock sample constituted by 105 clusters with the M82 galaxy image in the F555W as the background image. We assumed a mass-to-light ratio of 1 (resembling that of a population of 2.2 Gyr in the F555W filter) and a zero-point magnitude of 25.779 mag. The initial structural parameters, were simulated, considering realistic values (following the values reported in Cuevas2020,Cuevas2020b ), resembling clusters in M82.

sources.summary: The paper fits M82's gamma-ray SED with cosmic-ray transport models, adds VERITAS data, and finds the diffusion-dominated model unable to match high-energy emission above 1 TeV.

quotes.quote: For M82 and NGC 253 we also use the data provided by VERITAS 2009Natur.462..770V and H.E.S.S. 2018A&A...617A..73H , respectively. For each galaxy, as performed by Ref. Ambrosone:2021aaw , we determine the most-likely values for the two free parameters of the two models: the star formation rate M _* and the spectral index of injected protons and electrons.
On the other hand, for M82 and NGC 253 model B is disfavoured at 2 level.

In order to highlight this behaviour, in Fig. fig:m82 we show the SED for M82 predicted by the two models in their best-fit scenario. This result is highly significant since, differently from , we find that model B is not able to accommodate high-energy data for this source. This suggests that the diffusive model B cannot provide a full explanation of SFGs and SBGs emission above 1 TeV.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes M82 as a nearby starburst where FIR/sub-mm polarization traces outflow magnetic fields; its polarized SED matches the outflow and 850 data are recovered.

quotes.quote: We present a comprehensive study of the B-fields in three nearby ( 3.5 - 17.2 Mpc) starbursts (M82, NGC 253, and NGC 2146) at 5 pc-- 1.5 kpc resolutions using publicly available 53 - 890 imaging polarimetric observations with SOFIA/HAWC+, JCMT/POL-2, and ALMA. We find that the polarized spectral energy distributions (SEDs) of the full galaxies are dominated by the polarized SEDs of the outflows with dust temperatures of T_ d,outflow ^ PI 45 K and emissive index of _ outflow ^ PI 2.3 .

Note that the best fit to the polarized SED of M 82 also reproduces the 850 polarized measurement of the outflow, although the 850 was not used for the fitting procedure. This result indicates that the polarized emission arising from the 850 observations is within the beam of the observations, i.e., the polarized flux at 850 of M 82 from the JMCT/POL-2 observations is not lost. This is not the case when using the total flux SEDs (Section subsec:RES_I ). We find that the modified blackbody function describing the polarized SED of the full galaxy is similar to that from the outflow region for M 82.

sources.summary: The paper models M82 as a GeV- and TeV-detected starburst galaxy, adopting its supernova-rate range and finding broad PWN parameter space for its observed TeV flux.

quotes.quote: M82 M82, at a distance of d=3.6 0.3 Mpc , is also detected in GeV and TeV ray by Fermi LAT and VERITAS, respectively 2010ApJ...709L.152A,2009Natur.462..770V . The inferred SFR is 10 M_ yr ^ 1 , considerably higher than that in NGC 253 and 2013MNRAS.429L..70O estimate a type-II SN rate of 0.06 yr ^ 1 . estimated that the SN explosion rate varies from 0.08 to 0.3 yr ^ 1 in M82. Similar to NGC 253, we also consider _e v_ SN of M82 as a combined free parameter from 0.01 to 0.1 yr ^ 1 .

The calculated SED has very similar properties to those of NGC 253, as expected due to the similar target radiation field, magnetic fields, and average particle densities in the SB regions (see Figure fig:M82 ). The parameter space of PWNe to account for the TeV flux of M82 is given in Figure fig:eta_M82 . The available region in the parameter space for M82 is broader compared to that for NGC 253, because VERITAS observation gives a larger statistical error for the TeV flux.

sources.summary: The paper studies JWST/NIRCam 3.3 micron PAH emission in M 82, finding filamentary wind substructure and PAH suppression where X-ray emission is bright.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage In this paper we study the filamentary substructure of 3.3 m PAH emission from JWST/NIRCam observations in the base of the M 82 star-burst driven wind. Finally, we show that the outflow PAH emission is suppressed in regions of the M 82 wind that are bright in X-ray emission. M 82 is arguably the best-studied galactic wind in the literature.

M 82 is very near to edge-on (inclination 80^ ), which has allowed for the two nebulae extending outward from the stellar midplane of the galaxy to be easily observed Lynds1963 . In this work, we will focus on new observations from the James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) of the polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon (PAH) emission at the center of M 82. The 3.3 m PAH emission ejected from the starburst of M 82 is not a smooth structure, it is rather organized in plumes, and those plumes are composed of smaller scale clouds (some with identifiable cometary shapes).

sources.summary: The paper reports XMM/RXTE observations of M82, detecting a 54 mHz QPO from a central ULX likely M82 X-1 and using Chandra imaging to assess source confusion.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery with the EPIC CCD cameras onboard XMM-Newton of a 54 mHz quasiperiodic oscillation (QPO) in the > 2 keV X-ray flux from an ultraluminous X-ray source (ULX) in the starburst galaxy M82. This is the first detection of a QPO in the X-ray flux from an extra-Galactic ULX, and confirms that the source is a compact object. Based on the QPO strength and previous Chandra observations it appears likely that the QPO is associated with the most luminous object in the central region of M82, CXOM82 J095550.2+694047, however, XMM imaging alone is not sufficient to unambiguously confirm this.

The central region of M82 has been resolved by Chandra (see Kaaret et al. 2001; Matsumoto et al. 2001). The PN and MOS images are dominated by a bright point source whose location is consistent with M82 X-1. At XMM's spatial resolution, however, the field is crowded by several fainter sources resolved by Chandra.

sources.summary: The paper observes methanol emission in M 82, finding high- and low-excitation molecular components whose abundances imply dust-grain injection and dense warm cores shielded from UV radiation.

quotes.quote: Observations of three transitions of CH _3 OH towards the center and two positions around the nucleus of M 82 are presented. Two different components are found, one with high excitation ( n( H_2) 10^6 cm^ 3 , T_ rot 20 K) and a the other with low excitation ( n( H_2) 10^4 cm^ 3 , T_ rot 5 K). The high observed methanol abundance of a few 10^ 9 can only be explained if injection of methanol from dust grains is taken into account. While the overall [ CH _3 OH ] / [ NH _3 ] ratio is much larger than observed towards other starbursts, the dense high excitation component shows a similar value to that found in NGC 253 and Maffei 2. Our observations suggest the molecular material in M 82 to be formed by dense warm cores, shielded from the UV radiation and similar to the molecular clouds in other starbursts, surrounded by a less dense photodissociated halo.
The dense warm cores are likely the location of recent and future star formation within M 82.

sources.summary: The paper studies M82's central starburst with VLBI, estimating its radio-derived star-formation rate and arguing that its complex molecular and ionized ISM affects supernova-remnant expansion.

quotes.quote: Received 2006 March 21; in original form 2006 February 16 firstpage - lastpage 2006 We present the results of a second epoch of 18 cm global Very Long-Baseline Interferometry (VLBI) observations, taken on 23 February 2001, of the central kiloparsec of the nearby starburst galaxy Messier 82. These observations further investigate the structural and flux evolution of the most compact radio sources in the central region of M82.
The total flux density of M82 at 1.4 GHz is 8.5 Jy and using a distance of 3.2 Mpc, then the SFR(M 5 M _ ) for M82 can be estimated using the relation given by Cram et al., to be 2.5 M _ yr ^ 1 .

Furthermore, studies by Wei , Walter Neininger (1999) weiss99 have shown that the molecular gas in the starforming regions of M82 has a kinetic temperature of 150 K and number densities of 10^3 cm ^ 3 which corresponds to a pressure two orders of magnitude less than that assumed by Chevalier Fransson. Clearly the ISM of M82 is complex, and to assign a single pressure to the starburst region may be unrealistic. Hence it is likely that most of the SNR in M82 are embedded in regions with pressures significantly lower than 10^7 cm ^ 3 K, especially since pre-supernovae stellar winds from the massive progenitor stars of these remnants are likely to have driven away much of the material within the vicinity of these young supernova remnants prior to the supernova phase beginning.

sources.summary: The paper presents M82 as a nearby starburst galaxy hosting an extreme ULX, a 62-day X-ray periodicity, a flare, and a second luminous X-ray source.

quotes.quote: In 240 days of X-ray monitoring of M82, we have discovered an X-ray periodicity at 62.0 2.5 days with a peak to peak amplitude corresponding to an isotropic luminosity of 2.4 10^ 40 erg s^ 1 in M82 and an X-ray flare reaching a peak luminosity of 9.8 10^ 40 erg s^ 1 . The periodicity and flare likely originate from the ultraluminous X-ray source (ULX) in M82 which has been identified as a possible intermediate mass black hole. This source is a second ultraluminous X-ray source in M82 and may give rise to the QPOs detected from the central region of M82.

One of the most extreme ULXs is located in the nearby starburst galaxy M82 ptak99 . Chandra observations showed that the brightest source in the galaxy, CXOU J095550.2+694047 = X41.4+60 kaaret01,matsumoto01 , is offset from the nucleus, not coincident with any radio supernova remnant, and highly variable on time scales of weeks. The offset of the source from the dynamical center of the galaxy places an upper bound on its mass of 10^ 5 10^ 6 M_ , depending on its age, due to dynamical friction kaaret01 .

sources.summary: The paper treats M 82 as an extreme starburst outlier in the X-ray/UV relation, attributing its intrinsic deviation to predominantly extraplanar UV emission likely produced by disk starlight scattered from halo dust.

quotes.quote: The strongly discrepant data point visible in the L_ X / L_ UV diagram represents measurements for one of the most extreme starburst galaxies, M 82. In order for M 82 to follow our correlation, a much higher (lower) UV (X-ray) luminosity of about 1 dex is required. However, independent observations carried out in the UV CW82,Rif95,Ho05 and at X-ray energies Fab92,St04a yield very consistent results which imply that this ``deviation'' is an intrinsic feature of this outstanding galaxy.

More than 60 of the UV emission of M 82 measured at about 1530 \ is extraplanar in origin Ho05 . These authors claim that extraplanar star formation or a combination of photoionization and shock ionization either appear unlikely or cannot account for the halo emission in the UV. Instead stellar continuum radiation from the disk plane scattered at dust particles in the halo shall be the main mechanism causing the UV emission.

sources.summary: The paper reports an expanding molecular superbubble in M 82, centered on SNR 41.9+58, that has broken out of the disk and contributes to the galaxy outflow.

quotes.quote: M 82 is the best studied nearby starburst galaxy ( D = 3.25 Mpc ). The central few hundred parsecs of this galaxy are heavily obscured by dust and gas which hides the central starburst region against direct observations at optical wavelengths. Evidence for strong star--forming activity in the central region comes from radio (e.g. Kronberg \ ) and infrared observations (e.g. Telesco \ ) and also from the prominent bipolar outflow visible in \ (e.g. Bland Tully , McKeith \ , Shopbell Bland--Hawthorn ) and in X-rays (e.g. Bregman \ ). The massive star formation (SF) is believed to be fuelled by the large amount of molecular gas which is present in the centre of M 82.

The pv--diagrams therefore show that the expanding molecular shell has already broken out of the disk and now only shows the signature of an expanding molecular ring. It should be noted that the remaining molecular gas in M 82 shows clear solid--body rotation.

sources.summary: The paper uses M82 as the nearest well-studied starburst benchmark, emphasizing its compact radio-emitting central region and supernova remnants as motivation for milliarcsecond SKA resolution.

quotes.quote: The nearest and best studied starburst galaxy is undoubtedly M82. Located only 3 Mpc away, its radio emission is concentrated within the central few kpc of the galaxy and is dominated by radio emission from both recent and relic supernova remnants (SNRs). Fig. fig1 shows a superb, wide-field EVN 18 cm image of M82 produced by Pedlar et al. (1999) .

If M82 is typical of higher redshift starbursts (and recent VLBI observations by Smith et al. 1999 of the more vigorous and distant starburst, Arp 220, suggest that it may well be), we can expect to detect with SKA individual SNR in starburst galaxies out to cosmological distances - at least in terms of sensitivity. But this is only part of the story. If the SNR are distributed on scales similar to that observed in both Arp 220 and M82, then at z = 1.5 the bulk of the radio emission will occupy a region of sky no greater than 60 milliarcseconds across.

sources.summary: The paper uses M82 as a CO-line calibration object, citing a model combining central and outer emission, its low mass, first-transition brightness, and supersolar metallicity.

quotes.quote: In the case of M82, weiss05a present an accurate model of the CO emission from this galaxy, which takes both the central and the outer regions into account. The combined emission is a reliable estimate of what would be observed if M82 was shifted to cosmological distances, therefore we will use it to calibrate the R ratio in our model. The relatively low mass of M82 M 10^ 10 M_ , mayya06 is typical of the objects that contribute to the bulk of the correlation term estimated here ( M=10^9-10^ 11 M_ for redshift z=3-5 ).

M82 and the Antennae, for example, are extremely bright in the first two transitions, compared with the other objects. M82, which is the object used to calibrate most of the results presented here, has a metallicity 1.5 times the solar value galliano08 .

sources.summary: The paper uses M82 as the observational basis for galactic-wind model parameters, noting its starburst status, observed mass, possible wind-energy scaling, and symmetric poloidal core magnetic field.

quotes.quote: Table param_table shows some observational constraints on the parameters as obtained from one of the best observed, starbursting galaxies, M82.

M82's observed mass of 6 10^ 9 M _ 1998PASJ...50..227S is smaller than the average mass of the seeded halos (table mseed_stats ). To take this into account, we assume that the mass of the ISM M_ ISM in the model is equal to that of the halo mass in the simulation, and not from M82. There is further mass dependence in the mass outflow rate M _ . Therefore the value inferred from M82 might be underestimated and the obtained wind energy represents a lower limit for large halos.

In M82, 1994AA...282..724R observe a symmetric poloidal magnetic field structure in the core, while in edge-on galaxies combinations of symmetric (S0) and antisymmetric (A0) field structures are observed (R.Beck, priv.com.).

sources.summary: M 82 is treated as an evolved starburst prototype whose chemistry indicates strong photodissociation, and the observations target its northeast molecular complex rather than the center.

quotes.quote: M 82 and NGC 253 are the strongest and richest extragalactic molecular sources. These are the archetypes of starburst galaxies housing the two brightest extragalactic IRAS sources Soifer89 . M 82, as opposed to the young starburst in NGC 253, is claimed to be the prototype of evolved starburst. This is supported by its observed chemistry characterized by low abundance of complex molecules such as SiO, CH _3 OH Mauers93,Martin06b and large abundance of molecular ions like CO ^+ , HOC ^+ , and H _3 O ^+ which are expected to be enhanced in PDRs Fuente05,Fuente06,vdtak08 .

In this work we did not observe the central position towards M 82, but a position in the north-east molecular complex (hereafter M 82 ^* ) where most of the photodissociation regions are located as observed in the HCO emission high-resolution maps Burillo02 .

sources.summary: The paper studies M 82 as a nearby nuclear starburst galaxy, using formaldehyde and methanol detections to infer warm, moderately dense molecular gas in its lobes.

quotes.quote: In this contribution, we present the results of our recent study on the physical properties of the molecular gas in the prototypical nearby starburst galaxy M 82, demonstrating the diagnostic power of selected para-formaldehyde lines for external galaxies [ 11 ] .

As the most prominent nearby galaxy in the northern hemisphere with a nuclear starburst, M 82 has been the target of many investigations, including numerous molecular line studies [ e.g. \ 3,18 ] . Figure 1 shows an optical image of the highly inclined galaxy and its prominent galactic wind outlined in the H emission (red).

We detected several para- \ lines at 146 GHz and at 218 GHz in both molecular lobes of M 82, including \ lines of the K_a =2 ladder. Our non-LTE line ratio analysis suggests the presence of warm ( 200 K), moderately dense ( 7 10^3 cm ^ 3 ) molecular gas near the nuclear starburst, which is in very good agreement with the properties of the high-excitation molecular gas component found in recent comprehensive studies of multiple CO lines.

sources.summary: The paper uses M82 as a starburst-galaxy SED example, decomposing stellar, PAH, dust, free-free, and synchrotron components to illustrate dust-model fitting.

quotes.quote: The galaxy used for this illustrative purpose is the starburst M82. The dust model used in the calculations is the BARE-GR-S model of , consisting of PAHs, and bare silicate and graphite grains with solar abundances constraints. Figure 7a shows the SED of M82, and its various emission components: stellar emission at optical, and near-IR wavelengths; the PAH spectrum at mid IR wavelengths; dust emission from mid- to far-IR wavelengths; and free-free and synchrotron emission at radio wavelengths. Fig 7b depicts a fit to the dust spectrum using an ISRF characterized by a power-law distribution of radiation field intensities. PAH abundance determined by this method will underestimate the real abundance of PAHs in the galaxy compared to the method outlined below.

sources.summary: The paper models M82 as a nearby starburst, underpredicting its radio emission in the fiducial model while finding gamma-ray predictions comparable to Fermi and VHE detections.

quotes.quote: As an example of the power and limitations of our approach, we show the predicted synchrotron radio spectra of the starbursts in M82, NGC 253, and Arp 220 of our fiducial model in Figure fig:StarburstRadio . We calculate the radio emission using the Schmidt law, and assuming a disk geometry with the radius of the starburst from Thompson06 and scale height h = 100 \ . Our fiducial model underpredicts the radio emission of M82 by a factor of 2 and overpredicts the radio emission of Arp 220 by a factor of 4 .

Interestingly, our predictions for M82 and NGC 253 are comparable to the Fermi and VHE detections Acciari09,Acero09,Abdo10 . However, this agreement is caused by a fortuitous cancellation of factors. In Table table:GammaRayFluxes , we show that the predicted SN rates for M82 and NGC 253 using the Schmidt law are very small. If we combine the IR luminosities of Sanders03 with equations eqn:StarEmiss and eqn:CRpNorm , we find SN rates of 0.065 ^ 1 for M82 and 0.039 ^ 1 for NGC 253.

sources.summary: The paper uses M 82 as the prototypical extragalactic PDR, contrasting its UV-dominated molecular chemistry and low dense-core content with NGC 253.

quotes.quote: This is the case for the prototypical SB galaxy M 82, where the large observed abundances of molecular species such as HCO, HOC ^+ , CO ^+ , and H _3 O ^+ are claimed to be probes of the high ionization rates in large PDRs formed as a consequence of its extended evolved nuclear starburst Burillo02,Fuente06,VdTak08 . The molecular abundance ratios we derive for these molecules are very similar to those found in M 82. Such high abundances ratios of HOC ^+ , HCO, and CO ^+ relative to HCO ^+ have been claimed to be the evidence of M 82 being mostly dominated by photodissociation.

Therefore, M 82 is still outstanding not only as a PDR dominated galaxy, but by the underabundance of complex molecules such as CH _3 OH, HNCO or SiO Mauers93,Martin06a,Martin06b , evidence for the lack of large amounts of dense molecular material which would potentially fuel its nuclear starburst as compared to other starburst galaxies like NGC 253 Martin09 . Our data in combination with the HNCO abundances Martin09 indicate that the molecular clouds in M 82 are different from those in NGC253.

sources.summary: The paper presents M82 as a prototypical starburst whose central OH masers trace molecular gas conditions, dynamics, and velocity structure in the starburst region.

quotes.quote: The nearby starburst M82 is the prototypical starburst and one of the most intensively studied. It is known to contain large amounts of gas (e.g. H i , wills00 ; CO, shen95 ), have strong outflows (e.g. winds traced by H , shopbell98 ), numerous supernova remnants and H ii regions (e.g. muxlow94,mcdonald02,fenech08 ) and a history of strong star formation due to a past encounter with M81, although there is evidence that the rate of star formation has decreased significantly in the last 5 Myr ( beir08 ). Various types of maser have been detected in the central starburst of M82 over the last few decades, as would be expected for a galaxy with a significant molecular gas content and strong continuum emission, and these features can be used to investigate the molecular gas conditions and dynamics within the galaxy. Main line OH masers were first detected in M82 by and, while they are significantly stronger than maser emission seen in the Milky Way, they are fainter than the megamasers seen at the other extreme in AGN. Previous studies of the OH masers in M82 ( argo07 ) have found 10 individual masing regions, none of which are spatially resolved with the 1 4 beam of the VLA.

sources.summary: The paper describes M82 as a peculiar M81-group galaxy embedded in H I tidal streams and involved in a modeled interaction with M81 about 200 Myr ago.

quotes.quote: To do so, we have obtained deep and wide-field optical imaging data of the nearby early-type spiral M81 (NGC 3031), resolving stars well below the tip of the red giant branch of metal-poor stellar populations. The M81 group of galaxies is one of the nearest groups to our own. It contains one large spiral, two peculiar galaxies (M82 and NGC 3077), two small spirals galaxies (NGC 2976 and IC 2574), as well as a large number of dwarf galaxies e.g. karachentsev85, karachentsev01, chiboucas08 . The core galaxies of the group are strongly interacting. Atomic hydrogen observations have revealed the presence of a large number of tidal streams with large, dynamically complex atomic hydrogen clouds embedding M81, M82, NGC 3077, and NGC 2976 vdh79,appleton81,yun94,boyce01 .

A similar conclusion has been derived for the young stellar systems in the tidal bridge connecting M81 and M82 demello08a , and is consistent with the gas-phase metallicity of an H ii region in Holmberg IX as measured by makarova02 .
Detailed modelling of the dynamics of the M81 group suggests that the three largest galaxies in the system had an interaction 250 Myr ago for M81 and NGC 3077, and 200 Myr ago for M82 and M81 yun99 .

sources.summary: The paper presents M82 as a nearby prototypical starburst whose GeV and TeV detections constrain cosmic rays and imply efficient pionic losses.

quotes.quote: M82 and NGC 253 are nearby ( D 2.5 - 4.0 \ ), prototypical starburst galaxies, each having an intense star-forming region of about 200 \ radius in the center of a more quiescent disk galaxy.

Although ray emission from M82 and NGC 253 has been sought for several years with no success (at GeV, Cillis05 ; and at TeV, Aharonian05,Itoh07 ), the launch of Fermi and the advent of powerful VHE ray telescopes has led to recent detections of both starbursts at GeV energies (with Fermi ; Abdo10a ) and in VHE rays (M82 with VERITAS, Acciari09 ; NGC 253 with HESS, Acero09 ). These GeV and TeV detections constrain the cosmic ray (CR) population in these dense star-forming environments. In this paper, we discuss the implications of the ray detections of M82 and NGC 253.
We find that a fraction 0.4 and 0.2 of luminosity in CR protons is lost to pion production in M82 and NGC 253, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper uses Herschel-SPIRE spectra of M82 to measure its central CO ladder and infer a very warm molecular component likely heated by turbulent dissipation.

quotes.quote: We present the observations of the starburst galaxy M82 taken with the Herschel SPIRE Fourier Transform Spectrometer. The spectrum (194--671 m) shows a prominent CO rotational ladder from J = 4--3 to 13--12 emitted by the central region of M82 . The fundamental properties of the gas are well constrained by the high J lines observed for the first time. Radiative transfer modeling of these high-S/N ^ 12 CO and ^ 13 CO lines strongly indicates a very warm molecular gas component at 500 K and pressure of 3 10^ 6 K cm ^ 3 , in good agreement with the H _ 2 rotational lines measurements from Spitzer and ISO . We suggest that this warm gas is heated by dissipation of turbulence in the interstellar medium (ISM) rather than X-rays or UV flux from the straburst.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes compact supernova remnants in the starburst galaxy M82, using MERLIN data and Monte Carlo tests to fit its radio surface-brightness to diameter relation.

quotes.quote: In this paper we use the new observations of compact SNRs in M82 by F08. From F08, we extract data set that consists of 31 SNRs and fit D regression for both orthogonal and vertical offsets. Additionally, Monte Carlo simulations are performed to illustrate the selection effect of survey sensitivity on the fitted D slopes for M82 sample (the simulation algorithm is explained in more details in Section 5).

The central kpc of M82 was mapped at 5 GHZ using MERLIN Multi-Element Radio Linked Interferometer Network . The largest detectable angular size with this array at 5 GHz is 1.2 arc seconds (18.5 pc at the distance of M82).

sources.summary: M 82 is presented as the most evolved starburst in the sample, with PDR-dominated chemistry, low fragile-molecule abundances, and dense gas shaped by UV radiation.

quotes.quote: The large overall abundance of HCO in M 82 has been interpreted as evidence for a chemistry mainly dominated by photon-dominated regions (PDRs) within its central 650 pc ( Burillo02 ), which does not mean that other processes like shocks or X-ray heating do not take place in its nucleus Baan08 . The dissociating radiation explains the low abundance in M 82 of the complex and fragile species cited above ( Mauers03 ; Martin06 ). Other molecules like CS are not under-abundant in M 82 relative to NGC 253, which can also be explained in terms of PDRs chemistry, since a high abundance of this molecule is predicted for slightly shielded regions ( Drdla89,Sternberg95 ). This evidence, as well as the detection of other PDR enhanced species such as HOC ^+ and CO ^+ has led to think of M 82 as the prototype of a giant extragalactic PDR ( Nguyen89,Mao00,Burillo02,Martin09 ).

Finally, M 82, the more evolved starburst galaxy, shows a Region II of intermediate densities of 10 ^5 cm ^ 3 , probably surrounded by a large envelope dominated by photodissociating radiation, and a Region III of denser gas, comparable in size to the cores of NGC 253 and IC 342.

sources.summary: The paper argues that M82’s gamma-ray emission can include a major pulsar-wind-nebula contribution, comparing its observed luminosity and supernova-rate efficiency with NGC253.

quotes.quote: Recently, gamma-ray emission at TeV energies has been detected from the starburst galaxies NGC253 (Acero et al., 2009) [ 1 ] and M82 (Acciari et al., 2009 [ 2 ] . It has been claimed that pion production due to cosmic rays accelerated in supernova remnants interacting with the interstellar gas is responsible for the observed gamma rays. Here, we show that the gamma-ray pulsar wind nebulae left behind by the supernovae contribute to the TeV luminosity in a major way.

With the mean distances from the NED 3.8 Mpc for M82 and 3.1 Mpc for NGC253 the observed luminosities are L_ , M82 ( GeV )=2.2 10^ 40 \ erg \ s^ 1 and L_ , NGC253 ( GeV )=5.6 10^ 39 \ erg \ s^ 1 , implying an efficiency of 0.01 for R_ M82 =0.25  year ^ 1 and R_ NGC253 =0.07  year ^ 1 (see caption of Fig.2 for references). The values are lower than the values of 0.1 in the Ginzburg-Syrovatskii scenario for the origin of cosmic rays in the Milky Way galaxy [ 3 ] , perhaps due to adiabatic losses in the overpressured starburst region or the hotter interstellar medium compared to the Milky Way.

sources.summary: The paper studies hydrocarbon grains in M 82's superwind, detecting PAH and aliphatic features in halo regions and finding aliphatic-to-aromatic ratios rise with distance from the center.

quotes.quote: We investigate the properties of hydrocarbon grains in the galactic superwind of M 82. With AKARI, we performed near-infrared (2.5 -- 4.5 m ) spectroscopic observations of 34 regions in M 82 including its northern and southern halos. Many of the spectra show strong emission at 3.3 m due to polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs) and relatively weak features at 3.4 -- 3.6 m due to aliphatic hydrocarbons. In particular, we clearly detect the PAH 3.3 m emission and the 3.4 -- 3.6 m features in halo regions, which are located at a distance of 2 kpc away from the galactic center. We find that the ratios of the 3.4 -- 3.6 m features to the 3.3 m feature intensity significantly increase with distance from the galactic center, while the ratios of the 3.3 m feature to the AKARI 7 m band intensity do not.

sources.summary: The paper studies M82's starburst superwind, finding spatially correlated soft X-ray and line emission, a forbidden-line-dominated triplet, and possible charge exchange, recombining plasma, or resonance scattering explanations.

quotes.quote: We present a spatial analysis of the soft X-ray and \ emissions from the outflow of the starburst galaxy M82. We find that the two emissions are tightly correlated on various scales. The \ triplet of M82, as resolved by X-ray grating observations of , is dominated by the forbidden line, inconsistent with the thermal prediction. The \ triplet also shows some spatial variations. We discuss three possible explanations for the observed \ triplet, including the charge exchange at interfaces between the hot outflow and neutral cool gas, a collisional non-equilibrium-ionization recombining plasma, and resonance scattering.

sources.summary: The paper treats M82 as a prototypical gamma-ray-detected starburst, linking its hard spectrum and enhanced gamma-ray flux to rapid massive star formation.

quotes.quote: Two of the nearest starburst galaxies, M82 and NGC 253, have been detected in high-energy gamma rays by both space-based lat_2010_starburst and imaging air-Cherenkov telescopes acciari_2009_m82,acero_2009_ngc_253 . Although all galaxies are expected to produce CR-induced emission at some level, large numbers of SNRs together with dense interstellar gas (average number densities 500 cm ^ 3 ) and intense radiation fields led several authors to anticipate the central starbursts of M82 and NGC 253 as detectable gamma-ray sources e.g. paglione_1996_ngc_253,blom_1999_starburst,domingo-santamaria_2005_ngc_253,persic_2008_m82,de_cea_del_pozo_2009_m82,rephaeli_2010_ngc_253 . Indeed, the observed gamma-ray fluxes from M82 and NGC 253 imply enhanced CR energy densitities within galaxies undergoing rapid massive star-formation.

For M82 and NGC 253, the extrapolated power-law fits from the LAT energy range naturally connect with spectral measurements obtained by imaging air-Cherenkov telescopes at higher energies acciari_2009_m82,acero_2009_ngc_253,ohm_2011_ngc_253 .
