The 217 GHz luminosity is mostly the thermal glow of dust heated by starlight in the starburst (and Seyfert) regions Williams10,Peel11 .
This is demonstrated by the positive curvature in their microwave spectra, in contrast to the negative curvature expected of a blackbox spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper summarizes M82 molecular-outflow morphology and notes that its dynamics and masses are not captured by the simplified spherical-shell model.

quotes.quote: The molecular outflow observed in M82 has a complex morphology. The part of it outlined by CO emission is at a larger radii than the part seen with HCN and HCO ^+ lines. The CO (J=1-0) observations show diffuse molecular gas in a nearly spherical region of radius 0.75 kpc, with a total molecular mass 3.3 10^8 M _ , with an average outflow velocity of 100 km s ^ 1 walter2002 .
The morphology, dynamics and molecular masses of M82 are admittedly not explained by our simplified model, and therefore we wish to point out various caveats in our calculations.

sources.summary: The paper uses M 82 parameters to test two superwind models, one CC85 and one steeper distribution, finding little difference from CC85 because of the galaxy's low mass.

quotes.quote: Model 7 corresponds to one of the sets of parameters found by Strickland Heckman (2009) for M 82 through an extensive observational and theoretical study. As in their work, model 7 assumes a CC85 model for the hydrodynamics. Model 8 also corresponds to M 82, but it assumes a steeper distribution ( =1/2 ) and a concentration parameter that was derived from a smaller radius also used by Strickland Heckman (2009) to model this starburst galaxy. As in model 4, given the low mass of the galaxy, we do not obtain significant differences between these two models with respect to the CC85 solution, although a steeper distribution can compromise the solution stability.

sources.summary: The paper describes M82 as a peculiar M81 Group galaxy interacting strongly with M81 and NGC3077, with tidal debris and extended stellar halo features traced in the survey.

quotes.quote: It contains more than 40 member galaxies, including the large Sb spiral galaxy M81, the peculiar galaxies M82 and NGC3077, 9 late-type galaxies, at least 20 low-luminosity early-type dwarfs and a variety of stellar debris features, some of which are tidal dwarf galaxy candidates.

While the binary sub-group structure and the total number of member galaxies are similar to those of the Local Group, the recent strong gravitational interactions amongst M81, M82 and NGC3077 provide a quite different environment to that found locally. These interactions were first revealed through studies of the emission line of neutral hydrogen (HI) which showed an extremely disturbed HI gas distribution with tidal bridges connecting the three galaxies e.g. 1975ApJ...195...23G, 1981MNRAS.195..327A, 1994Natur.372..530Y, 2008AJ....135.1983C, 2018ApJ...865...26D .

Early highlights from this survey include the discovery of very extended (and rather perturbed) stellar halos around M81, M82 and NGC3077 as well as an enormous tidal stream which links the three systems 2015ApJ...809L...1O .

sources.summary: The paper uses M82 as a local mild starburst benchmark, citing its simulated or observed fast wind and adopting its distance for gamma-ray and neutrino flux estimates.

quotes.quote: The B0 prototype is reminiscent of local mild SBGs such as M82 and NGC253. Observations and numerical simulations of M82 suggest a terminal (wind) velocity 2000 km s^ 1 Strickland-Heckmann2009,Elizabete_2013 , with a mass loss rate up to 3 M_ yr^ 1 . The B1 configuration represents a somewhat more powerful wind that can be expected in objects for which the nuclear activity and temperature is higher (such as LIRGs) than what is inferred for M82 and NGC253  see e.g., Bustard_Zweibel2017 .

The bottom panels of the same Figures show the corresponding spectra of gamma rays and neutrinos resulting from pp and p interactions computed for the cases of a strong shock ( s=4 ) and assuming that the source is located at a distance of 3.9 Mpc (appropriate for M82).

sources.summary: The paper uses M82 as an archetypal nearby starburst whose circum-nuclear ring, molecular torus, and toroidal star-forming molecular lobes motivate ring-driven wind simulations.

quotes.quote: For example, the circum-nuclear ring of the archetypal nearby starburst galaxy M82 is well studied. Nakai1987 discovered a double-lobed distribution of CO, which is interpreted as a molecular torus. Hughes1994 studied thermal reradiated emission from dust in sub mm wavelengths and found that the intensity profile can be modeled as an edge-on nuclear starburst torus. provided a multi-transition analysis of the molecular gas and showed the regions of most violent star formation are confined to the molecular lobes, and are arranged in a toroidal topology around the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper reports Dragonfly pathfinder imaging around M82, confirming low-surface-brightness H-alpha structures and discovering emission, a tidal streamer counterpart, and a large outer gas cloud.

quotes.quote: The deep, wide-field data confirmed previously discovered low surface brightness features in the field such as the M82 H cap , as well as several new features and extensions to already known features in the group. These included a secondary ridge of H emission between the H cap and the M82 galaxy, as well as emission filling the region between the M82 galaxy and the H cap. In addition, H emission from a tidal dwarf candidate and emission corresponding to the HI tidal streamer on the northeast side of the M82 disk were discovered (indicated by dashed box in Fig. fig:hashell ; Ref. pash21 ). The most striking result of this imaging campaign was the discovery of a colossal cloud of gas in the outskirts of the M82 galaxy with an extent of 0.8 deg (labelled in Fig. fig:hashell ), which is analyzed further in Ref. lokh22 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses M82 as a deep LeMMINGs target whose optical extent contains many SNR and H II regions and needs wide-field sub-arcsecond radio imaging.

quotes.quote: In all, six sources have been observed and published as part of these deep observations: M82, a local supernova factory MuxlowM82 ; IC 10, a complex irregular star forming galaxy Westcott2017 ; NGC 4151, a well-known Seyfert galaxy with radio jets Williams4151,Williams4151_2 ; NGC 5322, an elliptical galaxy with collimated large scale radio jets Dullo5322 ; M51, an interacting galaxy with two AGNs RampadarathM51 and NGC 6217, a star-forming galaxy with a putative X-ray jet Williams6217 .

In Figure fig:M82_smudge , we show an optical image of M82 which includes dozens of SNRs and H II regions in the central kiloparsec e.g., MuxlowM82 . However, M82 is 7-arcmin across, so a large part of M82 has not previously been investigated in the radio at sub-arcsecond resolution. Our preliminary 5 GHz `shallow' data were used to test widefield wsclean algorithms, and detected a new radio source 4' away from the central pointing, and on the edge of the optical extent of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3034 (M82) as an interacting starburst galaxy and finds its integrated attenuation curve is MW-like to SMC-steep with a MW-like 2175-Angstrom bump.

quotes.quote: NGC 3034 (M82) is a starburst galaxy with the burst of star formation thought to be due to interactions with NGC 3031. 2014MNRAS.440..150H found evidence for a MW-like 2175 \ bump using color-color diagrams rather than SED fitting. As M82 is a starburst galaxy, the existence of a bump is contrary to the canonical starburst law. We find the attenuation curve is at least as steep as the MW and possible steeper than the SMC ( between -0.11 and -0.64) with a MW-like bump ( E_b between 2.94 and 3.43).

sources.summary: The paper uses M82 as the best-studied weak starburst outflow case, noting observed atomic and molecular wind phases and consistency with retained molecular gas far from launch.

quotes.quote: Perhaps the best-studied example is M82, where both atomic Martini18a and molecular Leroy15b gas have been mapped from the base of the outflow where it joins the ISM up to distances of few kpc above the disc. Quantitative analysis of these data indicates that the atomic and molecular phases likely carry as much or more mass flux than the cool ionised phase Yuan23a .

Radiative versus collisional processes As noted in sec:intro , the bulk of the mass available to be launched into outflows in most galaxies with substantial star formation rates exists in molecular form in the disc, and spatially resolved observations in both the Milky Way centre and M82 indicate that near their launch point most of the mass in outflows is molecular, with the balance shifting toward atomic further from the galaxy.

We note that our finding that outflows is galaxies like the Milky Way should completely dissociate by the time they reach distances of a few r_0 , while those in weak starburst galaxies like M82 should retain a substantial molecular component to distances much larger than r_0 , is in fact consistent with what is observed in those galaxies (Milky Way: Di-Teodoro20a, Noon23a ; M82: Leroy15b, Yuan23a ).

sources.summary: The paper uses M82 to illustrate MIR star-formation diagnostics, contrasting UIB-rich disk emission with its HII-continuum nucleus and classifying the nucleus as starburst dominated.

quotes.quote: As an example of the ``typical'' integrated MIR emission of normal spiral galaxies, we present in Figure fig1 the spectrum of a region in the disk of M82, 45 arcsec away from the central starburst. The usual UIB features, fitted by lorentzian profiles, are prominent. In addition, we notice the presence of several faint UIBs at 5.3, 5.7, 12.0, 13.6 and 14.3 m ( , , ).
Applying this criterion to the nucleus of M82, we clearly see that it is classified as a starburst while the nucleus of NGC 1068 has a typical AGN signature.

sources.summary: The paper presents M 82 as a close, well-studied starburst galaxy whose nuclear starburst, likely triggered by M 81, provides a long-cited case for a top-heavy IMF.

quotes.quote: The starburst galaxy M 82 has been the prime example for a top heavy IMF for 20 years . As the closest starburst galaxy with a distance of only 3.2 Mpc, no other starburst galaxy has been observed in such detail. The appearance of this galaxy is dominated by its nuclear starburst, which has probably been induced by a close encounter with M 81 about 100 Myr ago. The basic properties of the nuclear starburst are : Bolometric luminosity > 5.0 10^ 10 L_ , absolute K magnitude < -22.5 , ionising radiation L_ Lyc > 10^ 54 photons/s , total mass in the starburst < 2.5 10^8 M_ , CO index > 0.21 and supernova rate 0.1 yr^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper uses M82 to validate its PDR analysis, deriving G0 consistent with earlier work and finding a significant low-density ionized-gas contribution to non-PDR [C ii] emission.

quotes.quote: For M82 we derive G_ 0 = 10^ 3.4 , while Kaufman et al. ( ) estimated G_ 0 = 10^ 3.5 by taking account of several observed line intensities, suggesting that the present method provides a reasonable estimate of G_ 0 .

Petuchowski et al. ( ) reported a large [ N ii ] 122 m to 205 m line ratio in the central 850pc of M82 compared to the average ratio of the Milky Way (Wright et al. ), indicating that a fair fraction of the [ N ii ] line emission comes from the ionized gas of n_ e = 150-180 cm ^ 3 in M82. The ratio of [ N ii ] 122 m to [ C ii ] 158 m of non-PDR origin for M82 is about 0.3 in the present analysis, suggesting that there may be a significant contribution to the [ CII ] emission from the low density diffuse ionized gas in the outer part ( > 850pc) of the galaxy. The observed intensity is compatible with the interpretation that the non-PDR component of [ C ii ] 158 m comes from the ionized gas that emits [ N ii ] 122 m.

sources.summary: The paper identifies M82 as a nearby starburst with exceptional internal star-formation-efficiency variations, underestimated H-alpha-derived SFRs, and both low- and high-efficiency regions.

quotes.quote: M82, NGC520 and NGC6503, show exceptional internal SFE variations larger than a factor of 30. In particular, the nearby starburst M82 show a variation of about 2.6 order of magnitude.

Fig. 11 shows two edge-on spirals, NGC3079 and NGC1055 (see Figs. 2 and 3) and two well known starburst galaxies, NGC2146 and M82, in which SFRs deduced by the emission are underestimated by more than a factor of about 10.
In some cases, e.g. M82, starburst galaxies host both regions characterized by SFE lower and higher than the mean of the sample.

sources.summary: The paper reports a fossil-starburst cluster population in M82, with revised ages and masses from full SED fitting and a peak linked to the last tidal encounter with M81.

quotes.quote: In a recent study focusing on the fossil starburst site in the nearby, ``prototypical'' starburst galaxy M82 we found a large population of ( 110 ) evolved compact star clusters (de Grijs et al. 2001), whose properties appear to be consistent with them being evolved (and therefore faded) counterparts of the young star clusters detected in the galaxy's active core (O'Connell et al. 1995). Based on broad-band optical colours only and on comparison with stellar evolutionary synthesis models, we estimated ages for these clusters from 30 Myr to over 10 Gyr, with a peak near 650 Myr. We have since obtained new age and mass estimates for these star clusters, based on improved fitting methods to the full spectral energy distribution, including the F110W and F160W fluxes (de Grijs et al. 2002a). Our new age estimates confirm the peak in the age histogram attributed to the last tidal encounter with M81, but at slightly older ages than previously published, ( t_ peak / yr ) = 9.04 .

sources.summary: The paper treats M82 as one of two nearby metal-rich starburst galaxies proposed to supply the observed extreme-energy cosmic-ray flux and anisotropy.

quotes.quote: The starburst galaxies M82 and NGC253 have been proposed as the primary sources of cosmic rays with energies above 10^ 18.7 eV.

There are two candidate sources within the GZK sphere; namely, the nearby ( d 3 Mpc) metally-rich starburst galaxies M82 ( l = 141^ , b = 41^ ) and NGC253 ( l =89^ , b = -88^ ) . Phenomenological considerations based on analytical estimates of the diffusion coefficient and approximations to the photodisintegration losses and angular deflections suggest that the power of these starbursts is enough to provide all CRs observed on Earth above 10^ 18.7 eV . This analytical study is consistent with Monte Carlo simulations .

sources.summary: The paper treats M 82 as a prototypical nearby starburst whose nuclear supernova remnants imply heavy extinction and motivate near-infrared supernova searches.

quotes.quote: In the nuclear regions ( 600pc diameter) of the starburst galaxy M 82, large hydrogen column densities indicate extinctions [ 12 ] of A_ V = 30 ( 16) (Fig.1 right) towards the group of young supernova remnants (SNRs) observed at radio wavelengths.

We used our K-band template light curves for linearly declining "ordinary" CCSNe and "slowly-declining" CCSNe [ 12 ] , the amount and distribution of extinction found for the SNRs of M 82 (Fig.1 right) [ 12 ] , and the CCSN rates calculated from the far-IR luminosities of the targets according to r_ SN = 2.7 10 ^ 12 L_ FIR /L _ yr ^ 1 [ 12 ] . In Fig.3(left) the expected number of SN detections is plotted as a function of the intrinsic fraction of slowly-declining events. The results are plotted for a fixed extinction of A_ V = 10 towards the nuclear SNe, and for an extinction distribution similar to the SNRs of M 82. For one likely SN detection from the data collected so far, and an extinction of A_ V = 10 we obtain an upper limit ( > 90 confidence) of 30 for the slow-decliner fraction. However, assuming an M 82-like extinction distribution yields a most probable slow-decliner fraction of 10 .

sources.summary: The paper uses M82 as an archetypal nearby starburst and superwind laboratory, highlighting its compact starburst, high supernova rate, intense star-formation surface density, and radio supernova remnants.

quotes.quote: The classic starburst M82 is a particularly convenient laboratory for investigating starbursts and superwinds, given that it is one of the closest powerful starburst galaxies (D=3.6 Mpc), and that all current SF occurs within a 400 pc radius of the nucleus. Its FIR luminosity, dominated by the starburst activity, indicates a SN rate of 0.1 per year. In terms of SF rate per unit area M82 lies close to the upper limit found in starbursts at both low and high redshift meurer97 . Radio observations show 40 compact, but spatially resolved, SNR remnants muxlow94 , that outline the starburst region as seen at other wavelengths.

sources.summary: The paper uses M82 as a nearby starburst benchmark, citing its high supernova rate and cosmic-ray energy-density estimates derived from minimum-energy and synchrotron arguments.

quotes.quote: In nearby starburst galaxies, like M82 and NGC253, the supernova rate is estimated to be at least 0.1-0.3 yr ^ 1 (Rieke et al. 1980), comparable to the massive star formation rate (SFR), which is at least 0.1 M _ yr ^ 1 (Ulvestad et al. 1997, Forbes et al. 1993).

V olk et al. (1989) have calculated the CR energy density of M82 from minimum energy considerations, assuming a proton/electron ratio of 100 as it is locally observed. They obtain a value _ M82 80 eV cm ^ 3 . Suchkov et al. (1993) estimate _ M82 170 eV cm ^ 3 from the synchrotron flux, and an upper limit of 10^4 eV cm ^ 3 from the observed density of molecular clouds.

sources.summary: The paper treats M82 as the best local analog for diffuse red galaxies, noting its dusty irregular starburst nature, red color, weak B-band concentration, and likely inclination-independent reddening.

quotes.quote: The most plausible local red-sequence analog to DIFRGs is perhaps the dusty, irregular starburst galaxy M82, which has U-B=0.28 and whose light is not strongly concentrated in the B band (although it is more concentrated in K images). M82 itself is highly inclined but is likely to be reddened even if not viewed edge-on. Other than M82, there do not seem to be common local red-sequence analogs to distant DIFRGs, which is consistent with their apparent disappearance at low redshift in our sample, as noted in Section sec-morphology .
Based on an examination of red galaxies in a local sample drawn from the RC3, there is no common type of local red galaxy with morphology similar to the DIFRGs; M82 may be the best analog.

sources.summary: The paper treats M82 as a nearby starburst-galaxy example whose GRB and supernova activity could supply UHECR power from a local extragalactic source population.

quotes.quote: In the framework of our model, such extragalactic sources could be connected with starburst galaxies in the local group, such as M82 and NGC 253, both at distances 3.5 Mpc . Taking into account that the supernova rate in these galaxies is about 0.3 -- 1 per year, and that the estimated GRB rate in our Galaxy is about (0.3 -- 1) of the supernova rate, the mean GRB rate in the starburst galaxies is estimated as one per 300 - 1000 yrs. If the total energy of CRs accelerated by a typical GRB is indeed about 10^ 52 ergs , as for our local Galactic GRB, the characteristic injection power of UHECRs from starburst galaxies averaged over the timescale of 10^8 yr can be (1-3) 10^ 42 ergs s^ 1 . In Fig. \ 5 we show the UHECR fluxes expected from a single continuous source (which is valid for a GRB model because of the large number of GRBs from these starburst galaxies within the last 100 Myrs) at a distance from us.

sources.summary: The paper uses M82 as a nearby starburst example where compact super star clusters, spectral synthesis, and debated top-heavy mass functions motivate resolved observations with MOMSI.

quotes.quote: M82 is the nearest starburst galaxy, with considerable ongoing star formation. Much of this is seen to be occurring in young, very compact star clusters, usually referred to as super star clusters (SSCs). With the aim of understanding significant star-formation in the early Universe, spectral synthesis techniques have been applied to SSCs in M82 to understand their mass functions, and the feedback of ionizing photons into the local intercluster medium. A `top-heavy' mass function was found for an SSC in M82 , leading to suggestions of an abnormal initial mass function (IMF) therein.

sources.summary: The paper treats M 82 as a prototypical circumnuclear starburst whose compact star-forming disk drives a radio halo and high-energy outflows extending above the disk.

quotes.quote: The radio data of NGC 1808 were published by Saikia et al. (1990), the data of M 82 (=NGC 3034) were obtained by Reuter et al. (1992).

In these galaxies, the vast difference between r_ 25 and r_ SF (see entries for NGC 1808 and M 82 in Table tab:cuts ) illustrates clearly that r_ 25 is not a suitable tracer of currently ongoing SF activity.

A direct comparison of the values of r_ SF and r_ halo (Table par:data ) suggests that for circumnuclear starbursts (NGC 1808, M 82) r_ halo is slightly larger than r_ SF . This is caused by the fact that the starburst-driven outflow cones widen beyond the disk planes in the arche-typical hour-glass shape (e.g. Veilleux et al. 2005 and references therein).

sources.summary: The paper reports optical spectra of M82's dusty halo, interpreting its continuum as scattered disk light and identifying a broad red ERE band outside the Milky Way.

quotes.quote: Low-dispersion spectra have been obtained from 4000 to 9600 along the axis and across the dusty halo of the galaxy M82. The spectra of the halo are explained in terms of scattering of the galactic light by dust grains: when divided by the spectrum of the galaxy, they reveal a broad emission band, similar to, although wider than the bump of Extended Red Emission (ERE) commonly observed in galactic reflection nebulae. The feature is particularly well marked in the filamentary parts of the halo. This is the first detection of ERE outside of our Galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper reports silicate absorption in M 82, a large background-galaxy contribution to its infrared dust emission, and a dust-to-gas ratio as high as 0.1.

quotes.quote: A deep feature at 10 \ indicates strong absorption by silicates, particularly for in NGC 253, IC 342, M 82, NGC 5236, and NGC 5253. For NGC 5236 and NGC 7552 there is also evidence of PAH emission in the mid-IR.
The IR _d contribution from the starburst clouds with different velocity is generally high, except for M82 which shows a large relative contribution from the background galaxy.
More particularly, d/g as high as 0.1 were found in M 82, and d/g 0.04 in the starburst clouds of most of the LIRGs.

sources.summary: The paper monitors M82 in X-rays with RXTE, confirms a 62-day periodic signal, and notes the PCA flux includes all X-ray sources within the nearby starburst galaxy.

quotes.quote: In order to test this interpretation of the 62 day periodicity, we obtained additional monitoring of M82 in 2006/2007.

We obtained 187 observations of M82 using the Proportional Counter Array (PCA) on the Rossi X-Ray Timing Explorer (RXTE) covering MJD 53825 to 54223 with observations approximately every other day under RXTE program 92098.
We note that, due to the large angular acceptance of the PCA, the measured flux includes contributions from all X-ray sources within M82.

sources.summary: The paper treats M 82 as a dusty nearby starburst hosting massive clusters whose near-infrared ages trace its recent extended star-formation episode.

quotes.quote: Like other starburst galaxies, M 82 hosts compact, massive ( >5 10^5 M _ ) young star clusters that are interesting both in their own right and as benchmarks for population synthesis models.
In this paper, we analyse extended spectra of five massive clusters in the nearby starburst galaxy M82 , using a population synthesis tool with a highly improved near-IR spectroscopic extension.
The observed IR-bright clusters are part of the most recent episode of extended star formation in M 82.

sources.summary: The paper treats M 82 as a starburst galaxy with a hot bipolar outflow, using it to show that PAHs can be rapidly destroyed by electron collisions in the wind.

quotes.quote: Fig. timeEvolution_fig shows the fractional C-atom loss, due to electron and ion (H + He + C) collisions, for two widely different objects: the Orion Nebula (M 42) and the M 82 galaxy (cf. Table object_tab ). The famous Orion Nebula is an H ii region with high density ( n_ H = 10 ^ 4 cm ^ 3 ) and low temperature ( T = 7000 K) gas, while M 82 is a starburst galaxy, which shows outside the galactic plane, a spectacular bipolar outflow of hot and tenuous gas ( n_ H = 0.013 cm ^ 3 , T = 5.8 10 ^ 6 K). In M 82, PAHs are completely destroyed by electrons, even for the larger PAH, before electronic and nuclear contributions start to be relevant.

sources.summary: The paper cites M82 as a starburst galaxy with diffuse hard X-ray emission, complex ionized gas at the superwind roots, and a low [N 2]/H ratio that rises away from its disk.

quotes.quote: Similar diffuse hard X-ray emission has been detected from the starburst galaxy M82 Strickland:2007 .

Recently, showed that ionized gas in the starburst core of M82 is dynamically complex with many overlapping expanding structures located at different radii, with compressed, cool, photo-ionized gas at the roots of the superwind outflow.
[ N 2 ] /H has been estimated 1.5 in NGC 253, less than 1.0 in M82 where the ratio trends to increase far from the disk.

sources.summary: The paper uses M82 as a gamma-ray-detected starburst example, estimating its gamma-ray ionization rate, minimum GRDR temperature, and cosmic-ray transport by winds.

quotes.quote: The GeV and TeV gamma-ray observations of M82 and NGC 253 Acero09,Acciari09,Abdo10-Starburst indicate that roughly f_ 20 - 40 of their CR power is lost to pionic emission Lacki11-Obs , much higher than in the Milky Way where the ratio is only a few percent e.g., Strong10 , implying that pionic losses may be important for CRs in starbursts Lacki10-FRC1 .

The brightest starburst in the gamma-ray sky in terms of observed flux is M82, with a GeV-TeV gamma-ray luminosity of 2 10^ 40 \ Abdo10-Starburst and a starburst radius of 250 pc e.g., Goetz90,Williams10 .
For the values of _ derived for M82 in section sec:Estimates , I calculate T_k^ min to be 0.5 K if n_H = 10^4 \ ^ 3 and 0.02 K if n_H = 10^6 \ ^ 3 .

sources.summary: The paper compares M 82 with NGC 253 as another gamma-ray detected starburst galaxy, noting similar HE/VHE spectral shapes and a starburst probably triggered by interaction with M 81.

quotes.quote: The form of the ray spectrum of NGC 253 can be compared with another starburst galaxy, M 82 in the Northern Hemisphere, detected by the Fermi LAT Fermi:NGC253M82 and VERITAS VERITAS:M82 collaborations, respectively. If one looks at the corresponding HE and VHE ray spectra of M 82, the overall shape looks rather similar to the one presented in this paper for NGC 253. Even though the starburst in M 82 is in all probability triggered by the interaction with the companion galaxy M 81, the spectral similarity is consistent with the assumption that the CR sources in both galaxies produce similar energetic particle spectra and may therefore be of the same nature.

sources.summary: The paper links M82 to a recent merger, discusses M82 X-1 as a possible accreted dwarf-core black hole, and says a stalled satellite could explain its luminosity.

quotes.quote: In the noteworthy case of M82, which is likely to have undergone a recent merger, it has been suggested that the ULX M82 X-1 may be a low-luminosity active galactic nuclei powered by a intermediate-mass black hole (IMBH) from the core of a dwarf galaxy that M82 cannibalized King .
In the case of M82 X-1, the observed X-ray luminosity of 10^ 41 erg/s could be explained by the presence of a Sagittarius A*-sized satellite black hole spiraling through M82 under dynamical friction.

We have shown that this result is consistent with existing observations of ultraluminous X-ray sources, such as those found in M87 and, notably, M82 X-1, although the nature of these sources is not yet known conclusively and will require further study.

sources.summary: The review describes M82 as a nearby TeV-emitting starburst galaxy confirmed in gamma rays, with high compact star formation that can energize cosmic rays.

quotes.quote: M82 and NGC 253 are TeV gamma-ray emitting starburst galaxies (Acciari et al. 2009, Acero et al. 2009), both at a distance of 3 Mpc (see Fig. fig:starburst ). confirmed the gamma-ray emission in those objects and increased the sample of starburst galaxies by two more objects (NGC 4945, NGC 1068) although those are composite starburst/Seyfert 2 systems, which makes it challenging to disentangle the pure starburst component (Lenain et al. 2010). Their star formation rate (in a compact region) is larger than that of the Milky Way.

sources.summary: The paper reports an NH3 search at M 82's southwestern molecular lobe and finds a kinetic temperature similar to previous measurements.

quotes.quote: M 82SW: In addition to the central position in M 82 (a non-detection), we searched for NH _3 emission at a position offset of ( 12 , 4 ) arcsec from the nominal M 82 position known to be a source of NH _3 emission identified as the ``southwestern molecular lobe'' Weiss2001 . The _b 40^ and v 16 \ measurements of the (1,1), (2,2), (3,3), and (4,4) transitions by resulted in a kinetic temperature of 60 K based on a rotation diagram analysis of their (1,1), (2,2), and (3,3) spectra (they did not detect the (4,4) transition). The LVG model fit to our NH _3 (1,1) and (2,2) spectra result in a similar kinetic temperature (Figure fig:M82SWTkFit ).

sources.summary: The paper says M82 has unusually small and asymmetric scale heights and, together with NGC 4631, shows tidal-interaction signatures; M82 may have lost its outer HI disk.

quotes.quote: Recent determination of the scale heights in M82 and NGC 4631 also yielded different values: the scale heights in M82 are by a factor of 3 smaller than the mean values mentioned above. The scale heights in the north are larger than those south of the disk adebahr+13 .

Both galaxies show -different to the first five galaxies in Tab. tableone strong signs of tidal interaction like HI tails and bridges yun+93,rand94 where M82 might even have lost its outer HI disk by tidal disruption.

sources.summary: The paper uses M82 as a real galactic-outflow example, describing its outflows as powered by multiple super-star-clusters and its superbubbles as overlapping cluster-driven bubbles.

quotes.quote: Magnificent galactic outflows, such as M82, are powered by multiple super-star-clusters and the problem of understanding coalescing SBs is important. Star clusters more massive than 10^5 (and hence with >1000 SNe) are rare (e.g., see pro10 ); therefore, the superbubbles in M82 and in our N_ OB =10^5 SB model should be considered as giant bubbles driven by 100s of overlapping superbubbles due to individual star clusters. Indeed, 100s of star clusters have been observed in the central few 100 pc of M82 ( oco95 ).

sources.summary: The paper treats M 82 as a starburst galaxy with low CH/CO, cold and warm molecular-gas components, and CH arising in translucent gas similarly to NGC 253.

quotes.quote: In the star formation dominated galaxies, M 82, NGC 253 and Arp 220, the CH/CO abundance ratio is low ( 10^ 5 ), implying that the CH is primarily arising in diffuse and translucent gas where the chemistry is driven by UV radiation as found in the Milky Way ISM.

In the cases of the starburst galaxies Arp 220 and M 82, the low-J lines (up to J = 3-2) trace cold molecular gas ( : 15 -- 50 K), which dominates the mass of the molecular gas, whereas the mid- to high-J lines trace warm molecular gas ( : 450 -- 1300 K), which dominates the luminosity of the molecular gas.
Both M82 and NGC 253 have low CH/CO ratios, suggesting that the CH arises in translucent regions in these galaxies also and that the translucent regions contain about 10 of the molecular gas mass.

sources.summary: The paper treats M82 as a compact, luminous nearby starburst target whose outflow and distant cap show metal enrichment consistent with core-collapse supernova ejecta.

quotes.quote: Similar results were obtained with Suzaku on M82, finding via detailed abundance measurements that starburst activity enriches the M82 outflow through SNcc metal ejection Konami2011 . Suzaku studies of farther-out regions in M82, including the M82 Cap located 11 ^ (11.6 kpc) away from the nucleus have demonstrated metals consistent with Type II supernovae even at these great distances tsuru07 .
M82 is more compact and more luminous.

sources.summary: The paper treats M 82 as a nearby starburst outflow reference, using its mass loading factor and predicted iron-rich ejecta for star-formation-driven bubble models.

quotes.quote: We follow the descriptions in str09 , which discussed the outflow in the nearby starburst galaxy M 82.
For the nearby starburst galaxy M 82, the mass loading factor is in the range of 1.5 2.5 str09 .
In the nearby starburst galaxy M 82, its outflow is predicted to have X_ Fe, ejecta 5X_ Fe, see e.g. str09 , although the assumed IMF and yields are different.

sources.summary: The paper identifies M 82 as a nearby edge-on starburst, first detected in TeV emission, with high supernova and gamma-ray activity driven by interactions with M 81.

quotes.quote: M 82 The nearly edge-on, starburst galaxy M 82 (NGC 3034), located 3.6 Mpc away, was the first starburst detected in TeV emission, and perhaps the first direct detection of an extragalactic source of hadronic gamma-ray emission . It is the prototypical small starburst galaxy with an estimated supernova rate R_ SN 0.1--0.3 yr ^ 1 , a gamma-ray luminosity L (> GeV ) 2 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 and a photon number power-law index of = 2.21 0.06 over the 100 MeV -- 100 GeV energy bands . Interactions with neighboring galaxies, prominently the larger spiral M 81, have spurred star-forming activity, particularly in the central regions .

sources.summary: The paper describes M82 as a far-infrared starburst disk, observed at central and offset CO-peak positions, with narrow water-ion absorption in all three spectra.

quotes.quote: M82 is a starburst disk with \ = 5.3 10 \ and an SED that peaks in the far-infrared.
We have observed three positions in M82: the center and 15 '' offsets to the NE and SW (at PA = 72 ^ ), which correspond to peaks in the CO emission.
The spectra toward the three positions in M82 show narrow absorption in the \ and \ lines.

sources.summary: The paper treats M82 as the first extragalactic low-frequency CRRL detection, noting its nuclear starburst association and model comparison implying higher density, stronger radiation field, or blended components.

quotes.quote: Recently, morabito2014 discovered extragalactic CRRLs associated with the nucleus of the nearby starburst galaxy, M82.

Furthermore, following the first detection of low-frequency CRRLs in an extragalactic source (M82; morabito2014 ) we will also use LOFAR to perform the first flux limited survey of CRRLs in extragalactic sources.

In Figure fig_widthdiff we can see that the RRLs from Cas A and Cyg A fall in a region of the diagram corresponding to densities lower than about 0.1 cm^ 3 and the detection in M82 corresponds to either higher densities, to a much stronger radiation field or to the blending of multiple broad components.

sources.summary: The paper discusses M82 as SN 2014J's active star-forming host, emphasizing its low and complex reddening law and how extinction in M82 affects distance fitting.

quotes.quote: This was motivated by the fact that many studies (see below) found R_V < 2 in M82, quite different from the Milky Way value of R_V = 3.1 .
Thus, even though the host of SN 2014J, M82, is an extremely active star-forming galaxy, such a low value for the extinction law is not unprecedented.

This could be due to either an issue with our photometry (which is unlikely given that the other data do not show such a high deviation), or the complexity of the reddening law in M82 that may not be fully modeled by a single R_V foley14 .

sources.summary: The paper uses M82 as a nearby star-formation benchmark, adopting a 44 solar-mass-per-year SFR, noting H luminosity underestimates that SFR, and scaling transient-rate sensitivity to M82's distance.

quotes.quote: There is approximately 45.8 M _ yr ^ 1 within 3.6 Mpc, mostly from M82, which has a SFR of 44 M _ yr ^ 1 (Kennicutt de los Reyes, 2020 in prep ). This SFR is calculated using the sum of H luminosities from all galaxies in Kennicuttetal2008 with declinations greater than 30 , excepting M82, whose H luminosity severely underestimates the SFR. For a transient that has an all-sky rate of 1 sky^ 1 yr^ 1 at the distance of M82, the volumetric rate of fast transients above 5 10^ 33 erg s ^ 1 Hz ^ 1 is approximately 4.3 10^5 Gpc ^ 3 yr ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper models M82 as a nearby starburst galaxy whose Fermi-LAT gamma-ray flux can be reproduced by CRP transport models and whose neutrino flux may be detectable by future telescopes.

quotes.quote: Adopting different models for the transport of CRPs, we estimate the ray and neutrino emissions due to pp collisions from nearby SBGs such as M82, NGC253, and Arp220.
The Fermi-LAT ray observations, especially those of M82 and NGC253, are reproduced regardless of the diffusion model.
We suggest that M82 and NGC253 might be detectable as point sources of high-energy neutrinos in the upcoming KM3NET and IceCube-Gen2, when optimistic models are applied.

sources.summary: The paper discusses M82 as the starburst member of the nearby M81/M82 candidate source pair, with AGN activity, a massive black hole, and relevance to a Telescope Array hotspot.

quotes.quote: The galaxy M81 is a giant spiral, with a central black hole mass of 7 10^7 M_ , being a Seyfert I, although with weak activity at present. It interacted with the M82 galaxy some few hundred million years ago, what probably induced the strong starburst activity of this last, and the two galaxies are deemed to eventually merge into a single one in future interactions (the smaller galaxy NGC 3077 contributes to actually form a trio). M82 has some AGN activity, with a central black hole of 3 10^7 M_ . Note that while Cen A is almost at the center of the hot-spot hinted by Auger observations above 40 EeV , the M81/M82 complex is not far from the location of the Telescope Array reported hot-spot above about 57 EeV .

sources.summary: The review describes M82’s central region as a giant PDR and a highly inhomogeneous nuclear medium requiring multiple gas components in velocity-resolved observations.

quotes.quote: Interestingly, Goicoechea2013 point out similarities between the FIR spectrum of the hot gas around SgrA* and that of the starburst galaxy M82 Kamenetzky2012 , where shocks and turbulent heating are also found to be necessary to reproduce the high- J CO emission. Turning now to nearby galaxies, the presence of several ISM components is systematically established in galaxy centers. The central 650 pc of M82 are described as a `giant PDR' by , with a very high HCO abundance at the outer edge of the molecular torus. However, velocity-resolved observations with \ HIFI of the nuclear region of M82 reveal a highly inhomogeneous medium, with the presence of multiple components within a beam, which can be modelled with a low density component (70 ), a high density-low G_0 component (29 ), and high density-high G_0 component covering only 1 of the beam area Loenen2010 .

sources.summary: The paper cites M 82 as an interacting starburst whose H I velocity field deviates from circular motion, with filaments linking it to M 81 and NGC 3077.

quotes.quote: For example, the early H i observations of M 82 revealed that its velocity field showed strong deviations from circular motions, suggesting possible interactions with M81. Subsequent H i observations show filamentary structures connecting M 81, M 82 and NGC 3077, forming what is called the M 81-triplet 1994Natur.372..530Y, 2018ApJ...865...26D, 2019MNRAS.486..504S . The interaction of M 82 with its neighbour is thought to have triggered its starburst activity 2006ApJ...649..172M .

sources.summary: The paper uses M 82 as a benchmark starburst-wind case, citing its ionized outflow radius, hard X-ray constraints, and model parameters for starburst-driven warm outflows.

quotes.quote: It is also consistent with the radius at which the outflow of ionized gas is observed to begin in M 82 Shopbell98 .
Observations of the hard X-ray emission in the M 82 starburst favor the choice 1 Strickland09 .
The case considered is based on M 82, with \ = 150 km s ^ 1 , SFR = 20 M _ year ^ 1 , and a starburst radius of 300 pc.

sources.summary: The paper finds M82 follows the closest starburst sources under gamma-scaled UHECR luminosity, is enhanced by the Prim field, and helps dictate the gamma-scaled dipole.

quotes.quote: If the L_ CR scales with L_ , Cen A remains the main source followed by VirA and the closest SBGs (M82 and NGC 253).
The enhancement happens to M82, NGC 4945, NGC 2903, NGC 2146, and For A.
When the UHECR luminosity scales with the gamma luminosity, the dipole is dictated by Cen A, M82, and NGC4945.

sources.summary: The paper uses M82 as a typical starburst-galaxy exemplar, fitting its SED-derived parameters and saying its low-temperature, grey-body source environment agrees with viable starburst UHECR-source ranges.

quotes.quote: The relevant parameters of typical and extreme SBGs (exemplified by M82 and Arp 220) are determined in Appendix app:SBGs by fitting their SEDs. Both have a similar temperature, at the low end of the fit range. Arp 220 has n_0 1 so Fig. fig:srcs_constraints_CR applies directly, showing that extreme SBGs like Arp 220 cannot be major sources of UHECRs. However M82 has n_0 10^ 2 10^ 3 , sliding the posterior distribution 2-3 units to the right and down, for good agreement with the B and L range estimated for SBGs.

sources.summary: The paper models M82 as a nearby starburst gamma-ray source, using its halo benchmark and predicted spectral dips to constrain dark-matter cosmic-ray scattering.

quotes.quote: Here we show that the gamma-ray data from two nearby starburst galaxies, M82 and NGC 253, do not exhibit such a distortion, allowing us to bound the DM-CR cross section at the level of 10^ 34 cm ^2 for DM with 10 keV masses, as shown in Fig. fig:constraints .
As benchmark cases in the following analysis we use c_ 200 = 7 for both sources, M_ 200 = 10^ 12 M _ for M82 and M_ 200 = 3 10^ 11 M _ for NGC 253 .

The right panel of Fig. fig:M82_timescale_fluxes shows the resulting gamma-ray spectrum, evidencing the dips corresponding to different masses m_ due to elastic DM-CR scattering at an energy E^ _ dip 0.1 E^p_ dip - since gamma-rays carry on average 10 of the parent CR energy -- and the higher-energy power-law behavior of the gamma-rays from inelastic DM-CR scattering.

sources.summary: The review identifies M82 as the best-studied starburst-galaxy charge-exchange case, where XMM-Newton and Suzaku spectra need an added charge-exchange component to fit oxygen lines.

quotes.quote: There have been claims for the detection of charge exchange X-ray in several starburst galaxies. M82 is the best-studied object in this category. The X-ray spectroscopic works using XMM-Newton and Suzaku data showed that the thermal model is insufficient in particular for the O VII and O VIII lines, while adding an additional charge exchange component could fix most of the problem tsuru2007,analli2008, obs21, obs22, obs33, obs55 . A recent study using the RGS spectrum is plotted in Figure fig:m82 .

sources.summary: The paper maps M 82's mid-infrared emission, finding a smooth southwest-peaked morphology, extended PAH and 5.5 m disk-like emission, and more compact asymmetric 15 m and [ Ar ii ] emission.

quotes.quote: M 82 The continuum and emission feature maps in M 82 show a globally smooth spatial distribution, centered and peaking roughly 5 \ southwest of the nucleus. The PAH and 5.5 m continuum emission are the most extended and symmetric, with disk-like isophotes elongated along the galactic plane ( P.A. 70^ ). In contrast, the 15 m continuum and [ Ar ii ] 6.99 m line emission have more compact distributions which are more asymmetric relative to the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper contrasts M82 with bound-superbubble cases, calling it a true supergalactic-wind example with high mechanical input and strong local-Universe evidence for that phase.

quotes.quote: However, note that this is not the case for M82, where with an energy input rate of 3 10 ^ 42 and an ISM mass around 10 ^9 M _ , lies more than an order of magnitude above the limit prescribed by Silich Tenorio-Tagle (2001) and is thus a true example of a supergalactic wind.

Most galaxies in the local universe, including those with a low metal abundance, however, do not present as in M82, a clear evidence of having evolved into such a phase. Superbubbles, bound by a large-scale expanding supershell, are powered by the thermalized ejected matter and thus present a very even temperature distribution. On the other hand, supergalactic winds, given their stratification in density and temperature aught to present a stratified X-ray emission. - The metallicity within the interior of superbubbles changes rapidly with time. It exceeds Z_ , particularly within the first 10 - 20 Myr of the evolution. - The development of supergalactic winds depends drastically on the distribution of ISM both in the host galaxy disk and in the halo. Apart from M82, there is no strong evidence of a supergalactic wind in the Local Universe.

sources.summary: The atlas describes NGC 3034 as M81’s nearest companion and a dwarf starburst whose tidal interaction with M81 and gas infall drive violent star formation seen in the radio continuum.

quotes.quote: As M 81's nearest companion, this prototypical dwarf starburst galaxy has seen countless studies, from radio wavelengths through gamma ray energies. Similar to NGC 2146 (see above), although on a smaller scale, its violent star formation activity is caused by tidal interaction (in this case with M 81) and the resulting gas infall. Our radio continuum image delineates the central starburst, with some possible filaments emerging from the disturbed disk (although spoke-like calibration artifacts are also present), which were previously reported by Reuter et al. ( ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies M82 as a nearby starburst galaxy whose enhanced supernova rate and inner starburst winds improve prospects for GeV and TeV gamma-ray detection.

quotes.quote: The enhanced supernova rate in starburst galaxies such as M82 and NGC 253 at 3 Mpc improve the prospects that they could be detectable with GLAST and ground-based Cherenkov telescopes . In the inner starburst regions of these sources, interaction of cosmic rays with the strong stellar winds would produce GeV and TeV radiation . These processes will also generate neutrinos, though it is unlikely that they will be detected with IceCube or a Northern Hemisphere km-scale neutrino telescope, as these detectors have a sensitivity comparable to EGRET in terms of fluence A fuller discussion of neutrino sources will be given in my Madison proceedings for TeV/Particle Astrophysics II. .

sources.summary: The paper treats M 82 as a starburst galaxy whose observed infrared line ratios motivate multi-cloud shock/photoionization models with low and high density-velocity clouds.

quotes.quote: However, previous investigations on the physical conditions in the ISM of NGC 253 , NGC 3256 , and M 82 found relatively low electronic densities on the basis of the [ ] 33 m/18 m and [ ] 88 m/52 m line ratios, and low velocities derived from the FWHM of the IR line profiles. Multi-cloud models Many different conditions coexist in the ISM of single galaxies. Therefore, we performed multi-cloud model calculations to reproduce all the observed IR line ratios available in the literature for the starburst galaxies NGC 253, NGC 3256, and M 82.

gmw_00000020.name: NGC 253
gmw_00000020.overview: NGC 253 is a nearby barred spiral starburst galaxy [arXiv:1105.6336, arXiv:2404.04791] located in the Sculptor group [arXiv:1711.00805, arXiv:1510.03487]. The galaxy features a starburst-driven bipolar molecular outflow [arXiv:2404.04791] and has been detected in GeV and TeV gamma-ray emission [arXiv:1507.05711, arXiv:1202.0260].

sources.summary: The paper presents VLBI and VLA observations of NGC 253, finding its nuclear water maser and compact-source limits favor star formation and a starburst-dominated nucleus rather than an active nucleus.

quotes.quote: We present high-resolution spectral line and continuum VLBI and VLA observations of the nuclear region of NGC 253 at 22 GHz. While the water vapor masers in this region were detected on arcsecond and milliarcsecond scales, we could not detect any compact continuum emission with a 5 upper limit of 1 mJy. The observations reveal that the water maser emission is not related to a possible low-luminosity active galactic nucleus but is almost certainly associated with star-formation activity. Not detecting any compact continuum source on milliarcsecond scales also questions the presence of a -- previously assumed -- active nucleus in NGC 253.

One of the nearest water masers outside the local group that can be used for proper motion studies is located in NGC 253, a prominent spiral galaxy in the nearby Sculptor group of galaxies. It has been known for more than 20 years and is associated with the galaxy's mildly starbursting nuclear environment . NGC 253 is commonly placed at a distance of 2.5 Mpc, but some studies favor smaller ( 2.3 Mpc: DavidgePritchet1990 ) or larger (3.5 Mpc: RekolaRicherMcCall2005 ; 3.3 Mpc: MouhcineFergusonRich2005 ) distances. Throughout the paper we adopt the recent tip of the red giant branch distance estimate of 3.3 Mpc (1 '' = 16 pc or 1 mas = 3300 AU).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 253 as a nearby Sc starburst with cold dust, substantial free-free and synchrotron emission, radio recombination-line support, and low residual AME.

quotes.quote: NGC 253 NGC 253 ( 00^ h 47^ m 33.1^ s , -25^ 17^ m 18^ s J2000.0), also G097.18-87.96, is an Sc galaxy at a distance of 2.6 0.7 Mpc 1988PucheI with a starburst in its nucleus. Its optical dimensions are 27.5 6.8 arcmin 1991deVaucouleurs and at 857 GHz its dimensions are 6.1 5.0 arcmin (partially resolved).

We find that the best fit value for the free-free EM is NGC253:freefree cm ^ 6 pc, with a synchrotron spectral index of = NGC253:alphasync , which is steep compared to lower frequencies. Without free-free, the synchrotron index has an index above 1.5 GHz of = 0.77 0.11 . The H102 RRL in NGC 253 was the second extragalactic RRL to be detected, and was observed by 1977Seaquist at 6.1 GHz with a flux density of 20 3 mJy and width of 309 65 km s ^ 1 . At 6.1 GHz, our model predicts 1.27 Jy of free-free emission out of a total of 1.89 Jy, which agrees with the low T_ e ( < 3800 K) found by 1977Seaquist .

For NGC 253, the expected 30 GHz AME based on the 100 m flux density would be 0.5 Jy. The weighted mean between 23 and 94 GHz of the residual is NGC253:residuals Jy, giving a 3 upper limit of NGC253:residuals:3sig Jy.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 253's nuclear starburst with VLA polarimetry, finding filamentary radio emission in its outflow cone and helical magnetic fields that can collimate the outflow.

quotes.quote: We study the magnetic field structure in the central part of the nuclear starburst galaxy NGC 253 with spatial resolutions between 40 and 150 pc to detect any filamentary emission associated with the nuclear outflow. As the nuclear region is much brighter than the rest of the disc we can distinguish this emission from that of the disc. We used radio polarimetric observations with the VLA. New observations at 3 cm with 7 5 resolution were combined with archive data at 20 and 6 cm. We created a map of the rotation measure distribution between 6 and 3 cm and compared it with a synthetic polarization map.

In the SE part of the nuclear outflow cone the magnetic field is pointing away from the disc in form of a helix, with an azimuthal component increasing up to at least 1200 pc height, where it is about equal to the total component. The ordered magnetic field in the disc is anisotropic within a radius of 2.2 kpc. At larger radii, the large-scale field is regular and of even parity. The magnetic filaments indicate an interaction of the nuclear outflow with the interstellar medium. The magnetic field is able to collimate the outflow, which can explain the observed small opening angle of 26 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 253 as the comparison starburst, linking its nuclear molecular clouds to large-scale shocks and finding different chemistry, including higher CH2NH, NS, SiO, and HOCO+ abundances.

quotes.quote: We compare the chemical composition of the two starburst galaxies M82 and NGC 253. This comparison enables us to establish the chemical differences between the products of the strong photon-dominated regions (PDRs) driving the heating in M 82, and the large-scale shocks that influence the properties of the molecular clouds in the nucleus of NGC 253. Overall, both sources have different chemical compositions.

Some key molecules highlight the different physical processes dominating both central regions. Examples include CH _3 CCH, -C _3 H _2 , or CO ^+ , the abundances of which are clearly higher in M 82 than in NGC 253, pointing at photodissociating regions. On the other hand, species such as CH _2 NH, NS, SiO, and HOCO ^+ have abundances of up to one order of magnitude higher in NGC 253 than in M 82.

The galaxies M 82 and NGC 253 share several characteristics that have ensured that they are among the most studied of extragalactic objects. Apart from their luminosities ( L_ IR ^ NGC 253 =2.1 10^ 10 L_ , Vaucou91 ; L_ IR ^ M 82 =3 10^ 10 L_ , Rice88,Telesco88 ), they lie at a similar distance (D _ NGC 253 =3.9 Mpc, D _ M 82 = 3.6 Mpc, Freedman94 ), so their morphology, physical processes, and chemistry can be studied in detail with the same spatial resolution.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 253 as a nearby starburst detected in radio, GeV and TeV emission, with a compact central molecular zone, outflow, and high-density molecular complexes.

quotes.quote: The presence of high-energy electrons in the archetypal starburst galaxies NGC 253 and M 82 was identified through low-frequency radio-synchrotron emission measurements in the late 1970's (e.g. and early 1980's (e.g. ), respectively. VHE ray emission from these two objects has only recently been reported by and , respectively. Both galaxies have also been detected in the HE domain using data obtained in observations with the LAT instrument onboard the Fermi satellite ( ).

As a very nearby starburst with well-established radio, GeV and TeV emission, NGC 253 is well suited to such a comparison. The synchrotron emission from starburst galaxies is in general correlated to their far-infrared (FIR) luminosity and hence star-formation rate ( ). The radio emission of NGC 253 is coincident with the 0.5 kpc central molecular zone (CMZ), which has a star-formation rate exceeding that of the entire MW. Fig. fig2 provides a multi-frequency view of NGC 253 on the scale of the galaxy as a whole and close to the nucleus. The nucleus exhibits bright infra-red and radio emission, and an outflow is apparent in thermal X-rays. In a recent CO study found striking morphological similarities between the CMZ of NGC 253 and that of the MW (see Fig. fig2 ). The physical size of the CMZ as a whole, and that of the prominent CO peaks ( 20-50 pc), are remarkably similar in the two systems. Although the molecular mass estimate is afflicted by a considerable error in the CO-to-H _2 conversion ratio, the Giant Molecular Cloud (GMC) complexes in NGC 253 have significantly higher CO (2-1) intensities, suggesting order of magnitude higher densities in NGC 253 and a factor 10 higher total mass.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 253 as a chemically well-studied starburst comparison galaxy whose young nuclear starburst and large-scale shocks differ from M 82 and NGC 1068.

quotes.quote: NGC 253 and M 82 were also targets of unbiased molecular line surveys in the mm range, with 25 and 18 molecular species detected in their respective nuclear regions . On the other hand, NGC 253 is claimed to be in an early stage of the starburst evolution , with younger stellar populations in its nucleus ( 6 Myr ) where the PDRs, although present, do not drive the heating of molecular clouds. Instead, the nucleus of NGC 253 is dominated by large-scale shocks between molecular complexes. A detailed comparison of the M 82 and NGC 253 chemistries done by showed that the ISM in both galaxies have different composition, and that molecular abundances can be used to distinguish the physical processes that dominate galaxy nuclei. Figure Abundances shows the molecular abundances in the central regions of M 82, NGC 253, and NGC 1068.

The molecular ratios were calculated with respect to C ^ 34 S because, apart from being optically thinner than the main sulfur isotopologue, it does not seem to be significantly affected by the type of nuclear activity in galaxies . Furthermore, C ^ 34 S has similar column densities in the three galaxies (few times 10^ 14 cm ^ 3 , ). We note that while M 82 and NGC 253 are located at approximately the same distance, 3 Mpc , NGC 1068 lies nearly five times further, at 14.4 Mpc .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 253 as a nearby southern starburst spiral with measured HE/VHE gamma-ray emission, elevated star formation, and cosmic-ray transport from the starburst nucleus.

quotes.quote: The spiral galaxy \ is the closest object in the southern sky that belongs to the class of starburst galaxies. Its distance is measured as (2.6-3.9) Mpc using different distance estimation techniques NGC253:Davidge91,NGC253:Kara03,NGC253:Rekola05 . The reference distance is d = 2.6 Mpc NGC253:Davidge91 since this value is used most widely in the literature to determine the properties of .

The VHE ray data can be described by a power law in energy with differential photon index =2.14 0.18_ stat 0.30_ sys and differential flux normalisation at 1 TeV of F_ 0 = (9.6 1.5_ stat (+5.7,-2.9)_ sys ) 10^ 14 TeV^ 1 cm^ 2 s^ 1 . A power-law fit to the differential HE ray spectrum reveals a photon index of =2.24 0.14_ stat 0.03_ sys and an integral flux between 200 MeV and 200 GeV of F(0.2-200 GeV ) = (4.9 1.0_ stat 0.3_ sys ) 10^ 9 cm^ 2 s^ 1 . No evidence for a spectral break or turnover is found over the dynamic range of both the LAT instrument and the \ experiment: a combined fit of a power law to the HE and VHE ray data results in a differential photon index =2.34 0.03 with a p-value of 30 .

Compared to the Milky Way galaxy, \ exhibits an increased overall star formation rate (SFR), with the SFR in the starburst nucleus being comparable to that in the entire remaining disk of the galaxy. A SN rate _ SN of this nucleus can be determined from the far infrared (FIR) observations, since the FIR luminosity can be assumed to be directly proportional to _ SN VanBuren94 .

sources.summary: The paper studies near-infrared H2 excitation across the starburst galaxy NGC 253, finding UV fluorescence dominates over shocks and connecting the emission to PAH-tracing B-star radiation.

quotes.quote: Because of its large angular size and proximity to the Milky Way, NGC 253, an archetypal starburst galaxy, provides an excellent laboratory to study the intricacies of this intense episode of star formation. We aim to characterize the excitation mechanisms driving the emission in NGC 253. Specifically we aim to distinguish between shock excitation and UV excitation as the dominant driving mechanism, using Br , H _2 and [ FeII ] as diagnostic emission line tracers.

Using the 1-0 S(1)/2-1 S(1) line ratio as well as several other H _2 line ratios and the morphological comparison between H _2 and Br and [ FeII ] , we find that excitation from UV photons is the dominant excitation mechanisms throughout NGC 253. We employ a diagnostic energy level diagram to quantitatively differentiate between mechanisms. We compare the observed energy level diagrams to PDR and shock models and find that in most regions and over the galaxy as a whole, fluorescent excitation is the dominant mechanism exciting the H _2 gas.

We find that UV radiation is the dominant excitation mechanism for the H _2 emission. The H _2 emission does not correlate well with Br but closely traces the PAH emission, showing that not only is H _2 fluorescently excited, but it is predominately excited by slightly lower mass stars than O stars which excite Br , such as B stars.

sources.summary: NGC 253 is treated as a nearby barred spiral starburst whose central molecular gas is observed with Herschel SPIRE; shocks, turbulence, and mechanical heating are needed, while cosmic rays play at most a minor role.

quotes.quote: NGC 253 is a nearby, D _L =2.5 Mpc ( , 12 pc/''), edge-on barred spiral galaxy 1985ApJ...289..129S . The central kiloparsec of NGC 253 is considered an archetypal starburst nucleus ( L_ IR 2 10^ 10 L _ ), which is heavily obscured at optical wavelengths by dust lanes 1996ApJ...458..537P . However, in the far-infrared and submillimeter wavelength regimes, NGC 253 exhibits extremely bright molecular line transitions 1991A&ARv...3...47H , originating from large molecular clouds in the nuclear region 1995A&A...302..343I,1996A&A...305..421M,1997A&A...325..923H, 2003ApJ...586..891B,2009ApJ...706.1323M .

2006ApJS..164..450M finds evidence that large scale, low velocity shocks drive the chemistry and heating of NGC 253. In support of this, diffuse SiO emission has been detected throughout the nuclear region 2000A&A...355..499G . In addition, 2005ApJ...620..210M have measured an over-abundance of OCS, which is caused by low velocity shocks injecting these molecules into the gas phase. Mechanical heating can also be introduced into the ISM through supernova remnants 2008A&A...488L...5L or strong jets and we test its efficiency at heating molecular gas using the mPDR and mCDR models. Strong molecular outflows have been observed by 2013Natur.499..450B , and they estimate an outflow rate of 9 M _ yr ^ 1 , implying the outflows are a strong source of turbulence in the galactic nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper spatially resolves NGC 253's hard X-ray emission, finding it concentrated near the galactic center, dominated by ULX-like nuclear sources, and unsuitable for a dominant inverse-Compton gamma-ray explanation.

quotes.quote: The combined \ dataset, comprised of three 165 ks observations, allows spatial characterization of the hard X-ray emission in the galaxy NGC 253 for the first time. As a follow up to our initial study of its nuclear region, we present the first results concerning the full galaxy from simultaneous , , and VLBA monitoring of the local starburst galaxy NGC 253. Above 10 keV, nearly all the emission is concentrated within 100 \ of the galactic center, produced almost exclusively by three nuclear sources, an off-nuclear ultraluminous X-ray source (ULX), and a pulsar candidate that we identify for the first time in these observations. We detect 21 distinct sources in energy bands up to 25 keV, mostly consisting of intermediate state black hole X-ray binaries. The global X-ray emission of the galaxy -- dominated by the off-nuclear ULX and nuclear sources, which are also likely ULXs -- falls steeply (photon index 3 ) above 10 keV, consistent with other observed ULXs, and no significant excess above the background is detected at E > 40 keV.

We report upper limits on diffuse inverse Compton emission for a range of spatial models. For the most extended morphologies considered, these hard X-ray constraints disfavor a dominant inverse Compton component to explain the ray emission detected with \ and H.E.S.S. If NGC 253 is typical of starburst galaxies at higher redshift, their contribution to the E > 10 keV cosmic X-ray background is < 1 .

sources.summary: The GHOSTS analysis treats NGC 0253 as one of the nearest sample galaxies, measuring median RGB halo colours per HST field and finding a rather flat minor-axis stellar-halo colour profile.

quotes.quote: For the three nearest galaxies (NGC 0253, NGC 3031, and NGC 4945) we obtain a median colour measurement per HST field. These galaxies are at a distance of 4 Mpc and the size of their HST fields extends over roughly the same linear distance, covering 3.5 and 2.8 kpc on a side of the ACS and WFC3 field of view (FoV), respectively.

We assume that the stars observed along the minor axis fields located at galactocentric distances R> 5 kpc for the edge-on galaxies (NGC 0253, NGC 0891, NGC 4565, NGC 4945) and R > 10 kpc for the highly inclined galaxies (NGC 3031, NGC 7814) represent halo stellar populations. Figure fig:colprof_minmaj shows the minor axis stellar halo colour profile of each galaxy. In order to give a rough quantitative estimate of the magnitude of colour variation with radius, we fit a linear colour gradient to the data. Such a function has no particular physical relevance or motivation, and a variety of radial profile shapes are predicted by models e.g. Cooper10, Font11, Tissera14 . Other parameterizations are possible, but additional complexity seems unwarranted given the number of data points and their uncertainties.

Half of the galaxies (NGC 4565, NGC 0891, and NGC 7814) show fits consistent with stellar halo colour gradients whereas the remaining three galaxies (NGC 0253, NGC 3031, and NGC 4945) have rather flat colour profile.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 253 has a controversial AGN interpretation, with X-ray sources near the nucleus but no compact subarcsecond MIR source confirming AGN dominance.

quotes.quote: NGC 253 The presence of an AGN in the nearby starburst galaxy NGC 253 has been controversially discussed throughout the literature. In X-rays, an obscured point source, X-1, was detected close to the nucleus with weaver_chandra_2002 . While it does not coincide with the compact non-thermal radio source it is within the range of possible location for the dynamical centre of NGC 253 muller-sanchez_stellar_2010 . Interestingly, during the deep / monitoring in 2012, X-1 was not detected but a similarly bright source 1 arcsec away lehmer_nustar_2013 . This new source however, has a different X-ray spectrum and its location is incompatible with X-1.

In addition, the high energy emission detected by does not match expectations from an AGN as source. Combining all results, it is not clear whether X-1 is an X-ray binary or obscured AGN. Since no compact MIR source was detected at subarcsecond scales at the centre of NGC 253, its AGN nature is still unclear.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 253 over a specified radial range, notes its small bar and separately fitted disk-halo magnetic fields, and finds good model agreement.

quotes.quote: NGC 253 is included, however, with the radial range 1.4 - 6.7 Heesen+09b . Group II consists of M81, NGC 253, NGC 1566, NGC 6946, and IC 342. M81 has no bar whereas the others are SAB galaxies but with a small bar. In each case, the bar does not extend to the midpoint of the inner radial range considered. The bar radii are about 2.2 for NGC 253 Sorai+00 , 1.7 for NGC 1566 Combes+14 , 1.8 for NGC 6946 and references therein Fathi+07 , and 1.5 for IC 342 Crosthwaite+00 .

For M51 and NGC 253, disk and halo magnetic field components were fitted separately Fletcher+11,Heesen+09b , while for M31, such a separation was considered but found to be unnecessary Fletcher+04 . In Group II, however, radially binned field strengths are available for only one galaxy, NGC 253. The dynamo model typically overestimates the field strength, but only by a factor of about 2 on average, with the notable exceptions of M31 where the field strength is underestimated by a factor of about five, and NGC 253 where the theoretical estimate is in reasonable agreement with the observed value. For the galaxies M31, M51, NGC 253, NGC 1566, NGC 6946, and IC 342, our model does a fairly reasonable job of reproducing magnetic pitch angles.

sources.summary: The model fits NGC 253 gamma-ray data well over GeV and TeV energies, constraining its cosmic-ray parameters and implying near proton-calorimeter behavior.

quotes.quote: We now apply our model to five individual starbursts NGC 253, M82, NGC 4945, NGC 1068, and the Circinus galaxy, as well as the ULIRG Arp 220. The input parameters and best-fit results are listed in Table tab:parameter and Table tab:result . For each galaxy we adopt an observed star-formation rate (SFR), and then calculate the pionic flux E_ ^2dN_ /dE_ dAdt for each point in ( _ cr ,s) space.

From Table tab:result , we can see that the pionic gamma-ray luminosity calculated from our model agrees well with the phenomenological Fermi fits for the starburst galaxies M82, NGC 253, NGC 4945, NGC 1068 the Circinus galaxy, and ULIRG Arp 220. The best-fit pionic gamma-ray spectra can be seen in Figs. fig:spec-NGC253 through fig:spec-stb_2 . In left panels, the solid lines is our model's calculated differential spectral energy distribution of the five starburst galaxies with the best-fit parameters s and _ cr .

For M82 and NGC 253, we see that our best fit to GeV and TeV data is quite good and fairly well constrained thanks to the relatively large energy range. For NGC 1068, NGC 4945, Circinus and Arp 220, only GeV data is available and even our simple model is poorly constrained.

sources.summary: The paper reports 36.4 GHz maser emission in NGC 253, describing the galaxy as a nearby inclined nuclear starburst whose maser emission likely traces shocks near the bar-CMZ interface.

quotes.quote: We report the detection of maser emission from the J=4-3 transition of at 36.4 GHz towards the nearby starburst galaxy NGC 253. This is the first detection of maser emission from this transition in either a Galactic or extragalactic source. The maser emission has a brightness temperature in excess of 2500 K and is offset from the center of the galaxy by approximately 18 (300 pc), but close to a previously reported class I methanol maser. Both the and methanol masers appear to arise near the interface between the galactic bar and the central molecular zone, where it is thought that molecular gas is being transported inwards, producing a region of extensive low-velocity shocks.

NGC 253 is the largest galaxy in the Sculptor group and is one of the closest galaxies with a nuclear starburst, at a distance of 3.4 Mpc Dalcanton+09 . The disk of the galaxy has a relatively high inclination angle to our line of sight 72--78 ^ ; Puche+91 and shows strong molecular emission from a wide range of species e.g. Martin+06 . It has been the object of detailed studies across the electromagnetic spectrum from radio through to ray e.g. Ulvestad+97,Sakamoto+11,Weiss+08,Iodice+14,Dale+09,Lehmer+13 . NGC 253 hosts both water masers associated with star formation within the nuclear ring Henkel+04,Hofner+06 , and OH maser emission associated with the nuclear outflow Turner+85 .

sources.summary: NGC 253 is a nearby edge-on starburst spiral with extended CO and CN emission, dense-gas outflow tracers, and central-to-disk CO/CN changes.

quotes.quote: This paper presents the results of an archival project using ALMA Cycle 0 data to survey the CO/CN ratio in 17 nearby galaxies. We identified a sample of 8 galaxies with distances < 200 Mpc for which both the CN N=1-0 and CO J=1-0 lines were detected. These galaxies range from nearby starbursts (M83, NGC 253) to ultra/luminous infrared galaxies (AM 1300-233, AM 2246-490, AM 2055-425, NGC 3256, VV 114) to an S0 galaxy with an unusual far-infrared excess (NGC 1377). We measure S/N-matched CO/CN line ratios as well as ratios calculated from the total flux detected in each line with ALMA.

NGC 253 is an edge-on spiral galaxy with a strong central starburst. It has a starburst-driven outflow in which dense gas tracers such as HCN and CN are detected walter2017 . The CO/HCN line ratio is 10 in the outflow and central starburst but falls to 30 in the disk walter2017 . Individual giant molecular clouds appear as peaks in dense gas tracers as well as in the CO/HCN ratio map at 35 pc resolution leroy2015 .

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes H.E.S.S. and Fermi-LAT gamma-ray measurements of NGC 253, updating its spectrum, position, cosmic-ray luminosity, and calorimetric interpretation.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: optical center of NGC 253 at RA = 0 h 47 m 33.1 s , Dec = -25 d 17' 18''

quotes.quote: Trabalhador S a o-carlense, 400 - CEP 13566-590, S a o Carlos, SP, Brazil H.E.S.S. collaboration, mailto:contact.hess@hess-experiment.eu contact.hess@hess-experiment.eu ; 1 Corresponding authors 2 Deceased NGC 253 is one of only two starburst galaxies found to emit rays from hundreds of MeV to multi-TeV energies. Accurate measurements of the very-high-energy (VHE) (E > 100 GeV) and high-energy (HE) (E > 60 MeV) spectra are crucial to study the underlying particle accelerators, probe the dominant emission mechanism(s) and to study cosmic-ray interaction and transport. The measurement of the VHE ray emission of NGC 253 published in 2012 by H.E.S.S. was limited by large systematic uncertainties. Here, the most up to date measurement of the ray spectrum of NGC 253 is investigated in both HE and VHE rays.

The updated source position is RA = 0 h 47 m 32.54 s 0 m 11.2 s , Dec = -25 d 17' 25.4'' 0' 10.3'' (J2000), which changed only marginally towards an even better agreement with the optical center of NGC 253 at RA = 0 h 47 m 33.1 s , Dec = -25 d 17' 18'' . With the PSF being understood better, a possible extension of the ray source is constrained to 1.4' at the 3 level compared to the previous limit of 2.4' . The new ray spectrum, extracted at the best-fit position, is well described by a single power law, depicted in Fig. fig:combinedFit . The spectral points are given in Table tab:spec_points .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 253 as the best comparison for NGC 4945, describing weak 44.1-GHz masers, barred starburst properties, comparable maser luminosity, and lower per-region flux density.

quotes.quote: Ellingsen+17b detected weak 44.1-GHz methanol maser emission associated with two of the 36.2-GHz sites in NGC 253. They suggest that the low intensity of the 44.1-GHz transition compared to the 36.2-GHz in NGC 253 is strong evidence that extragalactic class I methanol maser emission cannot be explained as being due a large number of Galactic-like star formation regions within a small volume, but is rather a new and different extragalactic masing phenomenon. However, this has so far only been verified in the case of NGC 253.

The best match up of host-galaxies from the available sample is between NGC 253 and NGC 4945. Similarly to NGC 4945, NGC 253 is a nearby barred spiral starburst galaxy, with recently detected class I methanol maser emission at 36.2-GHz Sakamoto+11, Ellingsen+14 . These two sources are similarly distant 3.4 Mpc for NGC 253 and 3.7 Mpc for NGC 4945; Dalcanton+09, Tully+13 , and have methanol maser emission of comparable luminosity.

Our follow up observations have allowed us to more accurately compare the integrated intensity of NGC 4945 to NGC 253, as both sources have been observed with the same array configuration (H168) and are at similar distances. We find that the single region of class I maser emission in NGC 4945 has an integrated flux-density 50 greater than either emission region in NGC 253 Ellingsen+14, Ellingsen+17b .

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 253's nuclear starburst and outflow with 22 and 36 GHz masers, finding dense star-forming material entrained in its hot ionized wind.

quotes.quote: In this paper we explore the nuclear starburst of the nearby galaxy NGC 253 using astrophysical \ and \ masers. NGC 253 lies at a distance of 3.5 Mpc measured from the planetary nebulae luminosity function Rekola2005 . It has a total star-formation rate of 4.2 \ of which 50 is concentrated in the nuclear starburst Leroy2015 . The nuclear region is 1 kpc in diameter and contains about 90 of the CO luminosity of the galaxy Young1995 . The intense star formation at the center drives a molecular outflow of 30 \ Bolatto2013 .

We have presented subarcsecond observations of the 22 GHz \ maser and 36 GHz \ maser of the nuclear starburst in NGC 253. We have interpreted the results through the models of outflows in Strickland2002 and compared to the formation of SSCs of Leroy2018 .
We have found the following: We have detected thirteen water masers in NGC 253 and provided evidence for entrainment of dense star-forming material in the hot ionized outflow of NGC 253.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC253 as a nearby starburst galaxy whose compact luminous nucleus softens its gamma-ray spectrum below 1 TeV and whose emission above 100 MeV is pion dominated.

quotes.quote: In this section we specialize our calculation to the case of the three nearby SBGs, namely NGC253, M82 (respectively with D_L 3.8 and D_L 3.9 as found by 2005MNRAS.361..330R,1999ApJ...526..599S ) and Arp220 located at D_L 77 Mpc , as inferred by 1538-4357-492-2-L107 . The latter belongs to the ULIRG class, characterized by very prominent IR luminosity, higher ISM density and magnetic field energy density and more intense star formation activity . Arp220 shows a rate of SN explosions which is more than one order of magnitude higher than typical SBGs . For the modelling of the emission from these SBGs we start by fitting the thermal emission, in the 0.1 meV - few eV range, assuming that the observed emission in this band is dominated by the SBN and then we tune the other parameters to fit the multiwavelength spectra, from radio to gamma rays. The parameters' values used for each source are listed in Table tab:input_fits .

NGC253: The nuclear region of NGC253 is very compact and luminous at optical wavelength. This causes a non-negligible absorption at energies of few hundred GeV, which in turn determines a softening of the gamma-ray spectrum already below 1 TeV. The spectrum above 100 MeV is totally dominated by the _0 component.

sources.summary: The paper maps nearby barred spiral NGC 253 with DESHIMA, finds no continuum emission, and detects CO(3-2) emission at 345.53 GHz with an integrated peak intensity estimate.

quotes.quote: NGC 253 The OTF spectral map of the nearby barred-spiral galaxy NGC 253, presented in Fig. fig:orion f,g, was taken on 6--7 November 2017. The map size is 4.2 ' by 4.2 ' . The total on-source time was 1.7 hours. The data were reduced in the same manner as the Orion KL data, except that we used the median value of all channels to subtract the foreground sky emission. No continuum emission was detected from NGC 253, in an analysis similar to that for Orion.
