A galactic wind in NGC 253 was found in H McCarthy:1987 . discussed the spatial structure in detail in X-ray. The wind reaches out to 9 kpc perpendicular to the disk.

VLBI and VLA observations of the nuclear region of NGC 253 at 22 GHz shows no detection of any compact continuum source on milliarcsecond scales, indicating no low-luminosity AGN in the central region of NGC 253. It seems that the starburst region is the most plausible explanation for the source powering the wind Brunthaler:2009 .

sources.summary: The paper observes NGC 253's nuclear starburst ISM, deriving a ^12C/^13C lower limit of 81, tentative extragalactic ^13C^18O detection, and oxygen and sulfur isotopic limits.

quotes.quote: El Golf 40, Piso 18, Las Condes, Santiago, Chile To derive carbon isotopic ratios from optically thin tracers in the central regions of the starburst galaxies M 82 and NGC 253. We present high sensitivity observations of CCH and two of its ^ 13 C isotopologues, C ^ 13 CH and ^ 13 CCH, as well as the optically thin emission from C ^ 18 O and ^ 13 C ^ 18 O. We assume the column density ratio between isotopologues is representative of the ^ 12 C/ ^ 13 C isotopic ratio. From CCH, lower limits to the ^ 12 C/ ^ 13 C isotopic ratio of 138 in M 82, and 81 in NGC 253, are derived.

Lower limits to the ^ 12 C/ ^ 13 C ratios from CO isotopologues support these. ^ 13 C ^ 18 O is tentatively detected in NGC 253, which is the first reported detection in the extragalactic ISM. Based on these limits, we infer ratios of ^ 16 O/ ^ 18 O >350 and >300 in M 82 and NGC 253, respectively, and ^ 32 S/ ^ 34 S >16 in NGC 253.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 253 as a barred nuclear starburst with shocks and possible weak AGN activity, detecting p-H3O+ whose line shape fills the JCMT beam.

quotes.quote: NGC 253 is also a nearby barred galaxy located in the Sculptor group with a compact nuclear starburst and a IR luminosity that appears to originate in regions of intense massive star formation within its central few hundred parsecs strickland04 . From their 2 mm spectral scan martin06 suggest that the chemistry of NGC 253 shows strong similarities to that of the Galactic Center molecular region, which is thought to be dominated by low-velocity shocks. High resolution SiO observations show bright emission resulting from large scale shocks as well as gas entrained in a nuclear outflow garcia-burillo00 . Note also that it is suggested that NGC 253 is a galaxy in which a strong starburst and a weak AGN coexist e.g. weaver02,muller-sanchez10 .
p- \ line emission was detected towards IC 342, NGC 4418, NGC 253, NGC 1068 and NGC 6240, while Arp 299 and IRAS 15250 were not detected.
For NGC 253 the double peaked line shape suggests that the \ emission is emerging from both dense-gas peaks in the center and is filling the JCMT beam.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 253 as a nearby prototypical starburst with dense gas around its nucleus, a young starburst state, and varying molecular-disk line ratios implying warm dense gas.

quotes.quote: A comparison between M 82 and NGC 253 shows interesting similarities and differences. Both galaxies are considered to be prototypical starburst galaxies, nearby and seen at high inclination, with high infrared luminosities, super-star clusters and a prominent galactic wind. In both galaxies, the dense molecular gas is concentrated around the starbursting nucleus. The fractional abundances of a number of molecules, though, differ strongly between the two galaxies, which is seen as evidence that the starburst in M 82 is in an evolved state while the starburst in NGC 253 is (kept) young.

The intensity ratios derived from the observed lines are remarkably similar in the two lobes of M 82, whereas the ratios vary significantly along the major axis of the molecular disk in NGC 253, indicating different physical properties of the dense molecular gas across the galactic core region.
A preliminary analysis of the line ratios derived for the different positions in NGC 253 yields kinetic temperatures of T_ kin 70 150 K and gas densities of n_ H2 5 25 10^3 cm ^ 3 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 253 as a starburst template, reporting central-spaxel OH data, total OH emission, a 300 km/s terminal OH outflow, and mass loss similar to its SFR.

quotes.quote: For NGC 253 we show the central spatial pixel only. The Mrk 231 spectrum is taken from Fischer et al. (2010) and is repeated here for completeness. In all cases we detect P-Cygni profiles typical of outflows, with blue shifted absorption and red shifted emission features, of more than 1000 km/s in some cases.

The terminal velocity we measure in the OH outflow of our starburst template NGC 253 is 300 km/s. IRAS 17208-0014, a starburst dominated ULIRG with little AGN contribution has only a slightly higher v _ max (370 km/s).
The starburst-dominated objects of our sample (NGC 253, IRAS 13120-5453, and IRAS 17208-0014) have values of M similar to their star formation rates.

sources.summary: The paper uses EVLA radio recombination-line data to study NGC 253, finding complex thermal-gas kinematics, dense ionized gas, strong ionizing flux, and an unbound nuclear region consistent with outflow.

quotes.quote: We present the results of EVLA observations of the RRL and radio continuum emission at 33 GHz from NGC 253, a nearby nuclear starburst galaxy. We detect the previously unobserved 58 and 59 RRLs and make simultaneous sensitive measurements of the continuum. We measure integrated line fluxes of 44.3 0.7  W m ^ 2 and 39.9 0.8  W m ^ 2 for the 58 and 59 lines, respectively.

The thermal gas in NGC 253 is kinematically complex with multiple velocity components. We constrain the density of the thermal gas to 1.4 - 4 10^4  cm ^ 3 and estimate an ionizing photon flux of 1 10^ 53  s ^ 1 . We use the RRL kinematics and the derived ionizing photon flux to show that the nuclear region of NGC 253 is not gravitationally bound, which is consistent with the outflow of gas inferred from the X-ray and \ measurements.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC253 as a nearby true starburst galaxy detected above GeV energies, with flat gamma-ray spectra implying hadronic cosmic-ray interactions near acceleration regions.

quotes.quote: The nearest starburst galaxies M82 and NGC253 were detected above GeV energies, tracing the interaction of hadronic cosmic rays with the gas in the galaxy.

While observations of our own Galaxy indicate that propagation steepens the cosmic ray spectrum before interactions take place, the two starburst galaxies with observed gamma-ray spectra, M82 and NGC253, reveal a quite flat spectral behavior of around E^ 2.3 . Thus, it is expected that a large fraction of the cosmic ray interactions happen in the vicinity of the sources.

From Fermi- and IACT-observations, the gamma-ray flux from M82, NGC253, the Milky Way and the star-forming region 30 Doradus in the LMC are known fermi_starbursts2010 . The measurements give a unique opportunity to draw conclusions about the primary cosmic ray flux. First of all, the gamma-ray spectra for M82 and NGC253 appear to be very flat, i.e. \ E^ 2.3 and flatter.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 253 as a prototypical edge-on starburst whose radio halo, central filaments, and strong magnetic fields trace a hot-gas outflow.

quotes.quote: The strong magnetic fields in young galaxies may serve as the link between star formation and accretion. t t t NGC 253 is a prototypical starburst galaxy, hosting a starburst nucleus (Brunthaler et al. ). Its high inclination of 78.5^ allows observing the extraplanar emission. The bright radio halo (Fig. fig:n253 ) is probably a result of a galactic wind with a speed of about 300 km/s (Heesen et al. ).

Radio observations of the inner starburst region with high resolution revealed four filaments with widths of less than 40 pc and lengths of at least 500 pc (Fig. fig:n253_center ). These are interpreted as the boundaries of the outflow cone of hot gas interacting with the halo gas (Heesen et al. ). The equipartition strength of the total field in the filaments is at least 40 G, that in the central region 160 G. t t t Faraday rotation measures (RM) between 3 cm and 6 cm wavelengths are consistent with an axisymmetric, even-parity, spiral magnetic field in the disk at radii of more than 2 kpc, while the field in the inner disk is anisotropic (Heesen et al. ).

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 253 as a prototypical starburst core, using its nonthermal emission, star-formation intensity, gas consumption time, and predicted magnetic field strength as benchmarks.

quotes.quote: Throughout this work, I compare my results to four prototypical starburst regions: the Galactic Center Central Molecular Zone (GCCMZ), the starburst cores of NGC 253 and M82, and the starburst nuclei of Arp 220. The conditions in these regions span those of z = 0 starburst regions. In Table table:BasicProperties , I list the basic properties that I use in computations for these regions. These regions are relatively well studied. Models of the nonthermal emission from each has constrained the magnetic field strength and CR energy densities, and all but Arp 220 have gamma-ray detections. All have star-formation surface densities above the ``Heckman threshold" of 0.1 \ \ ^ 1 \ ^ 2 .f., Heckman90 .
I predict magnetic field strengths of 70 \ in the Galactic Center starburst, 300 \ in M82 and NGC 253, and 2 \ in Arp 220's nuclei.

sources.summary: The thesis reports high-resolution VLBI imaging of NGC 253, resolving compact radio sources, modeling absorbed spectra, and deriving supernova and star-formation-rate limits in its nuclear region.

quotes.quote: NGC 253 Wide-field, 2.3 GHz VLBI observations of the prominent starburst galaxy NGC 253, obtained with the LBA, have produced the highest angular resolution ( 15 mas) image of this source to date. The image revealed six sources, all of which corresponded to sources identified in higher frequency ( >5 GHz) VLA images. One of the sources, supernova remnant 5.48 43.3, is resolved into a shell-like structure approximately 90 mas (1.7 pc) in diameter and is estimated to have an age of 80(10^ 4 /v) yr, based on an assumed radial expansion velocity of v= 10,000 km s ^ 1 . From these data and data from the literature, the spectra of 20 compact radio sources in NGC 253 were modelled and found to be consistent with free-free absorbed power laws.

Based on the lack of detection of new sources over a period spanning 17 years, a supernova rate upper limit of 2.4 yr ^ 1 is determined for the inner 320 pc region of the galaxy at the 95 confidence level. Based on these limits, the star formation rate is estimated to be 3.4 (v/10^ 4 ) < SFR(M 5M_ )<59 M _ yr ^ 1 and is of the same order of magnitude as rates determined from integrated FIR and radio luminosities.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 253 as a nearby star-bursting spiral whose halo and satellite system are surveyed, with Scl-MM-Dw1 found projected 65 kpc away.

quotes.quote: In this work, we present the discovery of the faint dwarf galaxy Scl-MM-Dw1, the first result of a new survey to study faint substructure around NGC 253 (a star-bursting spiral galaxy in the Sculptor group) and NGC 5128 (the dominant elliptical galaxy in the Centaurus A group) in order to broaden our observational knowledge of faint substructure beyond the Local Group.

We have begun the Panoramic Imaging Survey of Centaurus and Sculptor (PISCeS), a resolved stellar survey of two of our nearest massive galaxy neighbors ---NGC 253 and NGC 5128---to search for archaeological remnants of their buildup via tidal streams and satellite dwarf galaxies down to M_V 8 mag. The most recent surveys in these galaxies for dwarf galaxy companions relied on photographic plates, and suffered from incompleteness fainter than M_ V 14 mag Cote97,Jerjen00,K02_cen,K04 . Our goal is to survey each galaxy out to =150 kpc to allow a direct comparison with the PAndAS survey in M31 McConnachie09 , and recent work around the Milky Way.

During the Fall 2012 observing season, we identified a clear dwarf galaxy candidate around NGC 253 using data taken with poor seeing. New, deeper observations were taken in Fall 2013, in much better seeing conditions (0 5--0 6; Figure fig:dwarf ). This new dwarf galaxy, which we dub Scl-MM-Dw1, is 65 kpc away from the center of NGC 253 in projection.

sources.summary: The paper reports an ATCA search toward NGC 253 that detected two offset 36.2 GHz methanol emission regions likely to be class I masers with luminosity exceeding the Milky Way central emission.

quotes.quote: We have used the Australia Telescope Compact Array (ATCA) to search for emission from the 4_ 1 3_ 0 E transition of methanol (36.2 GHz) towards the center of the nearby starburst galaxy NGC 253. Two regions of emission were detected, offset from the nucleus along the same position angle as the inner spiral arms. The emission is largely unresolved on a scale of 5 , has a full-width half maximum (FWHM) line width of < 30  , and an isotropic luminosity orders of magnitude larger than that observed in any Galactic star formation regions.

These characteristics suggest that the 36.2 GHz methanol emission is most likely a maser, although observations with higher angular and spectral resolution are required to confirm this. If it is a maser this represents the first detection of a class I methanol maser outside the Milky Way. The 36.2 GHz methanol emission in NGC 253 has more than an order of magnitude higher isotropic luminosity than the widespread emission recently detected towards the center of the Milky Way.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 253 as a bright spiral starburst whose nuclear molecular gas contains multiple sulfur-bearing species, with shock-driven chemistry similar to the Sgr B2 envelope.

quotes.quote: NGC 253 is also known as the Sculptor, or the Silver dollar galaxy. It is the bright, spiral, starburst galaxy where many molecular lines have been detected. Sulfur-bearing species H _2 S, CS, C _2 S, NS, SO, H _2 CS, OCS, and SO _2 were observed in the nuclear region of NGC 253 using the IRAM 30 m and the Swedish-ESO Submillimetre Telescope ( SEST ) Martin2005,Martin2006 .

Relative abundances of sulfur-bearing molecules in different environments suggest that the chemical processes in the nuclear region of NGC 253 are similar to those in the Sgr B2 envelope in the galactic center Martin2008 . Low-velocity large scale shocks were found responsible for the similar sulfur chemistry in these two regions. Both formaldehyde Gardner1974 and H _2 S Martin2005,Martin2006 , which react in laboratories under irradiation and form (CH _2 S) _n , were detected in NGC 253.

sources.summary: NGC 253 is described as a starburst nucleus shaped by both shocks and UV fields, with warm dense circumnuclear clumps and enhanced shock/grain chemistry relative to M 83.

quotes.quote: Regarding NGC 253, the observed circumnuclear region has five clumps of warm dense gas, but interferometric observations are needed to resolve them (e.g. Sakamoto11 ). With the 30 m telescope, the integrated spectrum of NGC 253 appears as two blended Gaussians.

As for NGC 253, groups of OB stars create photo-dissociated regions in the outskirts of the molecular clouds. This is confirmed by previous detections of PDR tracers, such as HOC ^+ , CO ^+ , HCO, and -C _3 H _2 Martin09 , which are easily formed by ion-molecule reactions in gas phase (e.g. Hollis83,Thaddeus85 ). However, the gas in NGC 253 is mainly characterised by the presence of many shock tracers and species formed in the icy mantles of dust grains. HNCO, CH _3 OH, and SO are a factor of two more abundant than in M 83.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 253 as an illustrative nearby starburst where X-ray and H observations are consistent with radiative cooling and thermal instability in a galactic wind.

quotes.quote: As a nearby observational example of a system that might showcase the onset of strong radiative cooling in a galactic wind, we consider the 0.3-0.6 kpc long conical limb-brightened frustum defined in along the minor axis to the south of NGC 253. Using the count rate in the energy range 0.3-1 keV (0.06 counts/s) or 0.3-2 keV (0.07 counts/s), temperature (0.5 keV), and volume reported by for this region (assuming a distance of D=2.6 Mpc), one derives a total radiated flux of 6-10 10^ 13 ergs cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 and a hot gas density of order 0.05-0.1 cm ^ 3 for gas with unity filling factor in CIE with Solar abundances. Comparing to the gas pressure and density derived from H observations by in the same region, argue that the true hot gas density is 0.1-0.3 cm ^ 3 .
These densities imply a cooling timescale of 3-9 Myr, whereas the advection time is of order Myr for gas at 500 km/s.

The NGC 253 observations therefore appear consistent with our models in which radiative cooling becomes important at larger scales. We thus propose that the H and X-ray emission from this region and beyond may be the result of the non-linear development of the thermal instability described in Section section:ti .

sources.summary: PISCeS maps Sculptor's resolved stellar halo to 150 kpc, treating it as a nearby spiral in a loose group with satellites, streams, and halo substructure shaped by accretion.

quotes.quote: In the wide-field Panoramic Imaging Survey of Centaurus and Sculptor (PISCeS), we investigate the resolved stellar halos of two nearby galaxies (the elliptical Centaurus A and the spiral Sculptor, D 3.7 Mpc) out to a projected galactocentric radius of 150 kpc with Magellan/Megacam. The survey has led to the discovery of 20 faint satellites to date, plus prominent streams and substructures in two environments that are substantially different from the Local Group, i.e. the Centaurus A group dominated by an elliptical and the loose Sculptor group of galaxies. These discoveries clearly attest to the importance of past and ongoing accretion processes in shaping the halos of these nearby galaxies, and provide the first census of their satellite systems down to an unprecedented M_V<-8 . The detailed characterization of the stellar content, shape and gradients in the extended halos of Sculptor, Centaurus A, and their dwarf satellites provides key constraints on theoretical models of galaxy formation and evolution.

The PISCeS survey targets two nearby galaxies ( D 3.7 Mpc): Centaurus A (Cen A, or NGC 5128), the closest elliptical to us and the dominant galaxy of a dense group of galaxies, and Sculptor (NGC 253), a spiral located in a much looser and elongated group. Sculptor has a mass comparable to our own Milky Way (MW; ), while Cen A is slightly more massive ( ), and due to their proximity they can be resolved into individual stars, thus allowing for a detailed comparison with the extant surveys of the MW, M31 and M81.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 253 as the likely host whose halo is being built up by Scl-MM-Dw2, a nearby diffuse satellite aligned toward and probably disrupted by it.

quotes.quote: Here we report the discovery of a faint, diffuse and highly elongated satellite galaxy located 50 kpc in projection from NGC 253, which we dub Scl-MM-Dw2, in accordance with our prior work in the Sculptor group. The data were taken as part of the Panoramic Imaging Survey of Centaurus and Sculptor PISCeS; Sand14,Crnojevic14 , which aims to study the faint satellites and stellar halo substructure of NGC 253 and NGC 5128 in resolved stellar light. Due to its high ellipticity and faint hints of other nearby substructure, Scl-MM-Dw2 is likely being disrupted, allowing for a direct view of the continuing buildup of NGC 253's halo.

We report the discovery of Scl-MM-Dw2, a dwarf galaxy with a luminosity of M _V=-12.1 located only 49' ( 50  kpc) in projection from NGC 253's center 3.6 r_h^ NGC253 ; RC3 . We use the TRGB method to find a distance modulus of m-M=27.47 0.21 which places this galaxy at 3.12 0.30  Mpc, consistent with that of NGC 253 within the uncertainty RadburnSmith11 . Thus, it is likely that Scl-MM-Dw2 is a satellite of NGC 253.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 253 as an interferometric example, comparing central-kiloparsec molecular lines with models and arguing different tracers sample different gas conditions.

quotes.quote: NGC 253 Again, as for the case of M82, here we simply perform an exercise to see how a priori modelling can help constrain some of the characteristics of the dense gas in another commonly studied galaxy. Meier2015 spatially resolved several molecular species in the central kiloparsec of NGC 253 (at a distance of 350 pc) with ALMA at a resolution of 50 pc. They identified 27 molecular lines in 10 individual locations. Meier2015 performed a detailed analysis highlighting the rich chemistry in the central zone of this galaxy. They reported the integrated line intensities, derived column densities, and LVG analyses.

The HCO ^+ (1-0) transition in the same nuclear region can be matched by Models 27, 39, and 54, differing in physical characteristics but all sharing the commonality that the cosmic ray ionization rate is higher than standard; more importantly the gas is at equilibrium, implying that the gas that HCO ^+ is tracing is not the same as that traced by HCN(1-0), which is another possible reason why their ratio is a poor indicator in this occasion.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 253 as a nearby barred spiral starburst where extragalactic methanol masers trace cool molecular gas and low-velocity shocks near the bar and central molecular zone.

quotes.quote: NGC 253 is one of the closest starburst galaxies to the Milky Way and as such it has been studied in detail across the electromagnetic spectrum. Recent observations have detected the first extragalactic class I methanol masers at 36 and 44 GHz and the first extragalactic HC _3 N (cyanoacetylene) masers in this source. Here we discuss the location of the masers with respect to key morphological features within NGC 253 and the association between the masers and the ongoing starburst.

NGC 253 is the largest galaxy in the Sculptor group, the closest group of galaxies beyond the local group. It is a barred spiral which we observe at a high inclination angle to our line-of-sight and has a star formation rate several times higher than that of the Milky Way e.g. Ott+05 , more than half of which is located in the central molecular zone (CMZ).
In NGC 253 the emission appears to trace regions where cool molecular gas is undergoing low-velocity shocks, such as at the interface between the galactic bar and the central molecular zone.

sources.summary: The paper detects 84.5 GHz class I methanol maser emission in NGC 253’s central region, compares it with other maser transitions, and notes its starburst molecular environment.

quotes.quote: We have investigated the central region of NGC 253 for the presence of 84.5 GHz ( 5_ 1 4_0 E) methanol emission using the Australia Telescope Compact Array. We present the second detection of 84.5 GHz class I methanol maser emission outside the Milky Way. This maser emission is offset from dynamical centre of NGC 253, in a region with previously detected emission from class I maser transitions (36.2 GHz 4_ 1 3_0 E and 44.1 GHz 7_ 0 6_1 A ^ + methanol lines) .

NGC 253 is a barred-spiral starburst galaxy in the nearby Sculptor group. Due to its proximity 3.4 Mpc; Dalcanton+09 and starburst nucleus, it has been extensively studied across a broad range of wavelengths, and displays emission from numerous molecular species Martin+06b, Meier+15, Ellingsen+17b . A star-formation rate of 1.7 yr ^ 1 has been determined for the central starburst region of NGC 253 Bendo+15 .

sources.summary: NGC253 is included through its starburst center, where molecular clouds occupy high-pressure environments and appear nearly virialized by self-gravity and internal motions.

quotes.quote: Our galaxy sample comprises eight local galaxies spanning a wide range of stellar mass and morphology. We include one dwarf irregular: the LMC, two low-mass spirals: M33 and NGC300, two massive spirals: the Milky Way and M51, one starburst galaxy center: NGC253, one green-valley galaxy: M31, and one lenticular galaxy: NGC4526. We compile global measurements of the stellar mass ( M_ ), atomic ( M_ atom ) and molecular gas mass ( M_ mol ), and star formation rate (SFR) from the literature or we derive them from our own data. Our sample covers about two orders of magnitude in various host galaxy properties: M_ = 2 10^9 - 10^ 11 , SFR = 0.03 - 4.2 , sSFR = 2 10^ 12 2 10^ 10 yr ^ 1 , and f_ gas = M_ gas / (M_ gas + M_ ) = 0.01 - 0.5 .

To first order, ``self-gravitating'' clouds have high surface densities ( _ gmc 10^2 10^4 ) and are found in high ambient pressure environments ( P_ ext 4 10^4 - 7 10^7 K cm ^ 3 ) as in the central and inner Milky Way, M51, NGC253, and NGC4526. Despite the high ambient pressure in their environments, these clouds appear nearly virialized considering only their self-gravity and internal motions.

sources.summary: The paper presents high-resolution Brackett-gamma kinematics of NGC 253's nucleus, finding complex broad components and identifying the infrared core as the likely source of its central galactic wind.

quotes.quote: We present Br emission line kinematics of the nuclear region of NGC 253, recently known to host a strong galactic wind that limits the global star formation of the galaxy. We obtained high-resolution long-slit spectroscopic data with PHOENIX at Gemini-South, positioning the slit on the nucleus Infrared Core (IRC), close to the nuclear disk major axis. The spatial resolution was 0.35 ( 6 pc) and the slit length was 14 ( 240 pc). The line profiles appear highly complex, with blue asymmetry up to 3.5 away of the IRC, and red asymmetries further away to NE. Several Gaussian components are necessary to fit the profile, nevertheless a narrow and a wide ones predominate.
The results indicate that the IRC would be the main source of the galactic winds originated in the central region of NGC 253.

sources.summary: The paper applies its PAH diagnostic to NGC 253 as a nearby starburst, assuming M82-like UV photon energy and finding predominantly cationic PAHs, less intense excitation, and an earlier starburst stage than M82.

quotes.quote: M82 and NGC 253 are the brightest prototypes of nearby starburst galaxies located at similar distances ( 3.5 Mpc; Dalcanton2009 ; Jacobs2009 ), with comparable IR luminosities but different chemical compositions Martin2009b .

For NGC 253, no SED modeling was available from the DustPedia archive, due to the absence of photometric coverage in the optical bands for this source. Therefore, we assumed a similar average UV photon energy to M82.

In terms of charge state, both M82 and NGC 253 are located in between the N25C75 and pure cationic tracks, indicating highly ionized environments where PAHs exist predominantly in their cationic state (Fig. fig:multigrid ). Specifically, NGC 253 resides closer to the N25C75 track while M82 is located closer to the track of pure cationic PAHs, suggesting less intense excitation mechanisms for NGC 253 compared to M82. Indeed, presented evidence of NGC 253 being at an earlier starburst evolutionary stage than M82.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 253 with the target starburst, describing its nearby edge-on starburst, young massive clusters, and similar ALMA-resolved cluster properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 253 is a nearly edge-on galaxy located nearby 3.5 0.2 Mpc ; Rekola2005 with similar properties as . It hosts a central starburst spanning 200 pc with young, massive clusters Leroy2018,Mills2020 .

Analyses of the cluster population of NGC 253 at 2 pc resolution with ALMA Leroy2018,Mills2020 revealed 18 sources as (proto-)super star clusters which are in the process of forming or close to a zero age main sequence ( 1 Myr) see also Rico-Villas2020 . Overall the properties of the clusters are strikingly similar as those in ; they have a median stellar mass of 1.4 10^ 5 \ and FWHM sizes of 2.5--4 pc. The star clusters of NGC 253 boast slightly higher ionizing photon luminosities and larger gas fractions M_ gas / M_ tot 0.5 , reflecting a slightly younger age. The slopes of the cluster mass function we derive are also consistent (see Section sec:cmf ), just offset by a factor of 3.5 in mass.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC253 as a local mild starburst benchmark, adopting its photon field, comparing inferred wind properties, and noting nearby starbursts could be resolved.

quotes.quote: The B0 prototype is reminiscent of local mild SBGs such as M82 and NGC253. We assume the photon field of NGC253  Galliano2008 as representative of the prototype B0. Similar terminal wind speed but higher mass loss rate are inferred for NGC253 Strickland_2002,Bolatto2013 . The B1 configuration represents a somewhat more powerful wind that can be expected in objects for which the nuclear activity and temperature is higher (such as LIRGs) than what is inferred for M82 and NGC253  see e.g., Bustard_Zweibel2017 .

In the VHE range, the nearest starbursts, M82 and NGC253, could already be resolved by current instruments. In fact, a bubble of size 50 kpc , at a distance of 3-4 Mpc , corresponds to an angular size 1^ , typically resolved by imaging air Cherenkov telescopes (IACTs) Veritas-sensitivity,MAGIC-sensitivity,HESS .

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 253 radio observations with a simulated star-forming analogue, finding flatter central spectra and explaining the flattening through dense central free-free absorption/emission effects.

quotes.quote: Thermal and non-thermal radio emission Figures fig: radio-spectra NGC 253 and M82 and fig: radio-spectrum NGC2146 show the radio spectra of the three star-forming galaxies NGC 253, M82 and NGC 2146. We compare observations by , 2013Adebahr_M82 and 2018Klein to radio spectra derived from our simulated galaxies that resemble the observed ones in terms of their SFRs and radio luminosities, respectively (see Table Table-Galaxies ). We show the observed and simulated total spectra (top panels) and the spectra from the central regions (middle panels).

Interestingly, we find that the central data both of NGC 253 and M82 show an even flatter radio spectrum in comparison to the total spectrum, with particularly strong free-free absorption at small frequencies. This is convincingly explained by the higher electron density in central regions of galaxies, as it is also manifested within our model of NGC 253.
We determine the contribution of the thermal free-free emission to the total luminosity at 1.4 GHz to 24.7, 6.3 and 5.7 per cent for NGC 253, M82 and NGC 2146, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper maps ionized gas in NGC 253’s nuclear starburst, finding embedded super star clusters that dominate recombination emission and likely contribute to the galaxy’s nuclear wind.

quotes.quote: We present new 3 mm observations of the ionized gas toward the nuclear starburst in the nearby (D 3.5 Mpc) galaxy NGC 253. With ALMA, we detect emission from the H40 and He40 lines in the central 200 pc of this galaxy on spatial scales of 4 pc. The recombination line emission primarily originates from a population of approximately a dozen embedded super star clusters in the early stages of formation. We suggest that these clusters contribute to the launching of the large-scale hot wind observed to emanate from the central starburst.

The barred spiral galaxy NGC 253 distance: 3.5 Mpc; Rekola05 is one of the nearest examples of a galaxy undergoing a nuclear starburst. Apart from this key difference, the stellar component of the nucleus of NGC 253 is in many ways an analog to the Milky Way's center.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 253's central proto-SSC 13 with ALMA, finding a hot, dusty super hot core whose velocity structure and temperature profile suggest cloud-cloud-collision-triggered star formation.

quotes.quote: Using high angular resolution ALMA observations ( 0.02 0.34 pc), we study the thermal structure and kinematics of the proto super star cluster 13 in the central region of NGC 253 through their continuum and vibrationally excited HC _3 N emission from J=24-23 and J=26-25 lines arising from vibrational states up to v_4=1 . We have carried 2D-LTE and non-local radiative transfer modelling of the radial profile of the HC _3 N and continuum emission in concentric rings of 0.1 pc width. From the 2D-LTE analysis, we found a Super Hot Core (SHC) of 1.5 pc with very high vibrational temperatures ( >500 K), and a jump in the radial velocity ( 21 km s ^ 1 ) in the SE-NW direction.

From the non-local models, we derive the HC _3 N column density, H _2 density and dust temperature ( T_ dust ) profiles. Our results show that the thermal structure of the SHC is dominated by the greenhouse effect due to the high dust opacity in the IR, leading to an overestimation of the LTE T_ dust and its derived luminosity. The kinematics and T_ dust profile of the SHC suggest that star formation was likely triggered by a cloud-cloud collision.

sources.summary: The paper updates NGC 253's placement in the SMBH-globular-cluster correlation, correcting prior globular-cluster counts and noting that the galaxy is classified as Sc with a pseudobulge.

quotes.quote: Until relatively recently, there were only 5 spiral galaxies with precise measurements of both N_ GC and M_ : the Milky Way (MW, Sbc), M 104 (Sa), M 81 (Sab), M 31 (Sb) and NGC 253 (Sc).
For completeness, we should mention that M 31 and M 81 have classical bulges, NGC 253 and the MW have pseudobulges, and M 104 has both a bulge and a pseudobulge korm13a .

We correct the position of NGC 253, whose number of GC had been read erroneously by harr14 . Indeed, olse04 report N_ GC = 21 , but with a completeness of 0.21, i.e., they really implied N_ GC 100 . They also give a specific frequency in the K band S_ N,K = N_ GC 10^ 0.4 M_K + 18 = 0.30 0.07 , or N_ GC = 92 21 . Previously, blec86 had found N_ GC = 80 40 for NGC 253.

sources.summary: The paper compares PDFchem to observations of NGC 253's central starburst region, finding its line ratios imply a high cosmic-ray ionization rate consistent with independent reports.

quotes.quote: Panel () shows the observed ratio for the central part in the NGC 253 galaxy Krie20 . This central part contains several regions of proto-super star clusters and they are thus best represented with the star-forming A_ V,obs PDF. NGC 253 is known to be a starburst galaxy of high star-forming activity Boll13b and of super-solar metallicity e.g. Z 2.19 Z _ Gall08 .

The shadowed region in panel () represents the mean and the 1 standard deviation of the Krie20 reported values. These lead to a high _ CR of approximately 10^ 14 s ^ 1 . Interestignly, such high values of cosmic-ray ionization rate have been independently reported e.g. Hold21 , confirming the PDFchem findings.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 253 as a nearby barred spiral and nuclear starburst, using ALCHEMI chemistry to reconstruct shocks in its central molecular zone and outflow environment.

quotes.quote: NGC 253 is a barred spiral galaxy that is almost edge-on with an inclination of 76^ McCormick+2013 . Being one of the nearest starburst systems D 3.5 0.2 Mpc, Rekola+2005 , NGC 253 is also one of the most studied starburst galaxies. The central molecular zone (CMZ) of NGC 253 spans about 300 100 pc across Sakamoto+2011 , and contains more than 10 well studied Giant Molecular Clouds (GMCs), observed in the continuum as well as in molecular emissions e.g.

NGC 253 is a prototype of nuclear starburst with an SFR of 2 M _ yr 1 coming from its central molecular zone CMZ, Leroy+2015,Bendo+2015 , which is half of its global SF activity. A large-scale outflow in NGC 253 has been revealed by multi-wavelength observations: in X-rays Strickland+2000,Strickland+2002 , H Westmoquette+2011 , molecular emission Turner1985,Bolatto+2013,Walter+2017,Krieger+2019 , and dust Levy+2022 .

The signature of shocks in NGC 253 has been suggested by the detection of HNCO and SiO GB+2000_sio_253,Meier+2015_hncosio_253 , the detection of Class I methanol masers Humire+2022 , and the enhanced fractional abundances of CO 2 Harada+2022 .

sources.summary: The review presents NGC 253 as a nearby starburst with a central molecular zone and outflow, where cosmic rays affect nuclear gas but do not drive the wind.

quotes.quote: NGC 253 has a lower star-formation rate, around 5 M _ yr ^ 1 . This is a residual burst, initiated by the collision with a gas-rich dwarf galaxy approximately 200 Myr ago . Although less active than M82, approximately 40 per-cent of NGC 253's star formation is concentrated within the central kpc and is associated with a dense CMZ of size approximately 0.8 kpc . This concentration of star formation drives an outflow, and may play a driving role in baryon recycling flows throughout the galaxy halo .

It follows that CRs are not dynamically important in driving the NGC 253 outflow . Despite this, CR feedback has been estimated to strongly impact the nuclear CMZ region of NGC 253 and potentially dominates the thermal balance of the gas in this area .

sources.summary: The paper finds prominent bright AGB stars in NGC 253 stellar halo, with inner-halo, shell, and major-axis AGB/RGB ratios implying relatively late halo star formation.

quotes.quote: For NGC 253 and NGC 891, a significant number of AGB stars are visible that are brighter than the TRGB; these are especially prominent in the CMD for NGC 253's minor axis. M81 shows a less apparent bright AGB star population. In addition, a prominent young ( < 200 Myr old) stellar population is detected in M81 bluewards of the RGB and AGB, representing stars formed in M81's group-wide Hi debris field Okamoto15,Okamoto2019 .

NGC 253 While the AGB/RGB ratio for the minor axis ( 1.16^ +0.04 _ 0.03 ) of the inner stellar halo is very well measured, the modest number of HST fields coupled with the small area covered by each HST field (at the distance of NGC 253, D=3.5 Mpc) limit the accuracy of our other measurements. NGC 253's shelf substructure Greggio14,Harmsen17 has an AGB/RGB ratio ( 1.04^ +0.11 _ 0.20 ) which appears somewhat higher than its inner stellar halo, although with uncertainties that overlap the inner stellar halo's value. NGC 253's major axis, comprising field 7, shows a very similar ratio of 1.01^ +0.08 _ 0.07 , also slightly higher than the inner stellar halo, but again with overlapping error bars.

sources.summary: The paper localizes GRB 180128A to NGC 253, identifies it as the galaxy’s second modern magnetar giant-flare association, and uses the galaxy distance for energetics.

quotes.quote: IPN localizes this burst with NGC 253, commonly known as the Sculptor Galaxy. This event is the second MGF in modern astronomy to be associated with this galaxy and the first time two bursts are associated with a single galaxy outside our own. Here, we detail the archival search criteria that uncovered this event and its spectral and temporal properties, which are consistent with expectations for a MGF.

The IPN refined the localization to 9.3 deg ^2 , which is centered on NGC 253, as shown in Figure fig:IPN . In further investigating this event as having a MGF origin, we perform spectral and temporal analyses for this burst.

Using the distance to NGC 253 of 3.7 Mpc at this distance, we neglect cosmological redshift leroy2019z , we find an E_ iso = (5.9 1.0) 10 ^ 44 erg from the fluence values of the COMPT fit to the spectrum over the BB duration of 155 s.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC253 with KAO PDR results, finding mostly consistent line fluxes but lower inferred densities; its high [O i] ratio is attributed to edge-on absorption effects.

quotes.quote: Carral et al. ( ) reported the results of observations of NGC253 and NGC3256 by the KAO and obtained G_ 0 and n based on the PDR models of Wolfire et al. ( ). While the line fluxes are in agreement with the present results within the errors except for [ O iii ] 88 m of NGC253, the present results indicate systematically low densities. The difference can be attributed to the relatively high gas temperature with the same G_ 0 and n in the models of Kaufman et al. because their models include the additional gas heating due to polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs).

However, a few galaxies clearly show higher ratios than the model. They are NGC4945 and NGC253, both of which have large inclination angles (for NGC4945 and NGC253, i 85 and 80 , respectively). NGC520, which shows the third largest ratio, has i=60 . The absorption of [ O i ] 63 m in the interstellar medium may affect the ratio in these galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper reports CANGAROO-II TeV observations of NGC 253, detecting broad gamma-ray emission from this nearby starburst spiral and motivating cosmic-ray studies.

quotes.quote: We report the TeV gamma-ray observations of the nearby normal spiral galaxy NGC 253. At a distance of 2.5 Mpc, NGC 253 is one of the nearest starburst galaxies. This relative closeness, coupled with the high star formation rate in the galaxy, make it a good candidate TeV gamma-ray source.

Observations were carried out in 2000 and 2001 with the CANGAROO-II 10 m imaging atmospheric Cerenkov telescope. TeV gamma-ray emission is detected at the 11 level with a flux of (7.8 2.5) 10^ 12 cm ^ 2 sec ^ 1 at energies > 0.5 TeV. The data indicate that the emission region is broader than the point spread function of our telescope.

sources.summary: The paper reports VLA and Effelsberg observations of water maser emission in NGC 253, finding multiple blueshifted components near the nucleus and interpreting them as disk, superwind, or star-formation related gas.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The phase center for the NGC 253 observations was ( , )_ 2000 = 00 ^ h 47 ^ m 33 1805, --25 ^ 17 \ 15 991.

quotes.quote: 22 GHz water vapor emission was observed toward the central region of the spiral starburst galaxy NGC 253. Monitoring observations with the 100-m telescope at Effelsberg and measurements with the BnC array of the VLA reveal three distinct velocity components, all of them blueshifted with respect to the systemic velocity. The main component arises from a region close to the dynamical center and is displaced by < 1 \ from the putative nuclear continuum source.

The bulk of the maser emission is spread over an area not larger than 70 50 mas ^ 2 . Its radial velocity may be explained by masing gas that is part of a nuclear accretion disk or of a counterrotating kinematical subsystem or by gas that is entrained by the nuclear superwind or by an expanding supernova shell. A weaker feature, located 5 \ to the northeast, is likely related to an optically obscured site of massive star formation.

sources.summary: The paper maps CO emission in NGC 253, connecting central molecular gas, density peaks, and star formation to bar-driven twin-peaks dynamics near the nucleus.

quotes.quote: We present 5 2 2 6 resolution interferometry of CO \ emission from the starburst galaxy NGC 253. The high spatial resolution of these new data, in combination with recent high resolution maps of , HCN and near-infrared emission, allow us for the first time to link unambiguously the gas properties in the central starburst of NGC 253 with its bar dynamics. We confirm that the star formation results from bar-driven gas flows as seen in ``twin peaks'' galaxies. Two distinct kinematic features are evident from the CO map and position-velocity diagram: a group of clouds rotating as a solid body about the kinematic center of the galaxy, and a more extended gas component associated with the near-infrared bar. We model the line intensities of CO, HCN and \ to infer the physical conditions of the gas in the nucleus of NGC 253.
In NGC 253, the coincidence and alignment of the density peaks, bar axes and possible ILR imply that NGC 253 is likely a twin-peaks galaxy, and the bar drives star formation around its nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper measures a TRGB distance for NGC 253 and describes its red halo RGB population, broad metallicity spread, starburst-driven outflow, and lack of blue main-sequence stars.

quotes.quote: NGC 253 is a well studied nearly edge-on Sc-type L _ * galaxy in the Sculptor group; it is an archetypal starburst galaxy, and is the closest edge-on infrared luminous galaxy (Radovich et al. 2001). Extended soft X-ray emission from a hot gaseous component was detected in the halo of NGC 253. This X-ray emission seems to be powered by a large-scale outflow, driven by the nuclear starburst activity (Pietsch et al. 2000; Strickland et al. 2002).

The left panel of Fig. ngc253 displays the color-magnitude diagram of the observed halo field. The stars in the halo are predominantly red; the main feature of the color-magnitude diagram is the RGB structure, which finishes in a well-defined TRGB. The red giant branch is prominent and wide, indicating a large spread in the metallicity distribution of the halo stellar population.

sources.summary: The paper reports high-resolution 2.3 GHz VLBI imaging of NGC 253, detecting compact nuclear radio sources, resolving one shell-like supernova remnant, and modeling source spectra as free-free absorbed emission in a clumpy ionized medium.

quotes.quote: Wide-field, very long baseline interferometry (VLBI) observations of the nearby starburst galaxy NGC 253, obtained with the Australian Long Baseline Array (LBA), have produced a 2.3 GHz image with a maximum angular resolution of 15 mas (0.3 pc). Six sources were detected, all corresponding to sources identified in higher frequency ( >5 GHz) VLA images. One of the sources, supernova remnant 5.48 43.3, is resolved into a shell-like structure approximately 90 mas (1.7 pc) in diameter.

From these data and data from the literature, the spectra of 20 compact radio sources in NGC 253 were modelled and found to be consistent with free-free absorbed power laws. Broadly, the free-free opacity is highest toward the nucleus but varies significantly throughout the nuclear region ( _0 1->20 ), implying that the overall structure of the ionised medium is clumpy. Of the 20 sources, nine have flat intrinsic spectra associated with thermal radio emission and the remaining 11 have steep intrinsic spectra, associated with synchrotron emission from supernova remnants.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 253 at 870 microns, finding a compact nuclear starburst, bar-associated dust, spiral-arm dust across the optical disk, diffuse trailing-edge emission, and no significant minor-axis dust continuum.

quotes.quote: The 870 \ distribution in the central region of NGC 253 is dominated by the compact, well studied nuclear starburst region (see Fig n253_image ). Fitting a two-dimensional Gaussian intensity distribution yields a deconvolved size (FWHM) of 30'' 16'' ( 370 200 pc at an assumed distance of 2.5 Mpc, Houghton \ ) and a total flux is 6.4 0.7 Jy. The dust surrounding the central starburst region closely follows the stellar bar visible at near infrared wavelengths (for an overlay of the 870 \ emission onto a NIR K band image see Fig. n253_image right). The dust distribution in this inner part of NGC 253 is consistent with previously published Submillimeter Common User Bolometer Array (SCUBA) maps (Alton \ ). The large scale dust distribution is dominated by two prominent spiral arms which originate near the stellar bar and are detected throughout the optical disk of NGC 253 out to a distance of 10' from the nucleus.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 253 as an edge-on spiral with a dumbbell-shaped radio halo, X-shaped vertical halo fields, and evidence for an antisymmetric poloidal field linked to outflow.

quotes.quote: The dumbbell-shaped halo around edge-on galaxies like NGC 253 ( and this volume) is the result of enhanced synchrotron and Inverse Compton losses in the inner region.

High-sensitivity observations of edge-on galaxies like NGC 891 ( ) and NGC 253 (Heesen et al., this volume) revealed vertical field components in the halo forming an X-shaped pattern (Krause, this volume). This is inconsistent with the predictions from standard dynamo models. The field is probably transported from the disk into the halo by an outflow emerging from the disk.

Vertical fields as predicted by dynamo models should generate large-scale RM patterns around edge-on galaxies, but so far indication for an antisymmetric poloidal field was found only in the halo of NGC 253 (Heesen et al., this volume).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 253 as a starburst test case, predicting slightly flatter-than-modeled radio spectra and gamma-ray fluxes lower than previous models.

quotes.quote: As an example of the power and limitations of our approach, we show the predicted synchrotron radio spectra of the starbursts in M82, NGC 253, and Arp 220 of our fiducial model in Figure fig:StarburstRadio .
The spectra of NGC 253 and Arp 220 are slightly flatter than predicted, which is probably due to free-free absorption.

We predict lower fluxes for NGC 253 than previous models by Domingo05 and Rephaeli09 , by a factor of 4 - 13 . In particular, we predict an integrated flux of F_ ( 100 \ ) 4 10^ 9 for NGC 253. This is substantially smaller than the predictions of 2.3 10^ 8 by Domingo05 and 1.8^ +1.5 _ 0.8 10^ 8 by Rephaeli09 .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 253 as a benchmark starburst whose millimeter line survey revealed many extragalactic species and shock-heated dense molecular gas feeding nuclear star formation.

quotes.quote: Among the two most extensively studied examples of starbursts we find NGC 253 and M 82. NGC 253 was recently the target of the first unbiased millimeter molecular line survey in the 2 mm atmospheric window with the IRAM 30m telescope Martin06b . This survey (shown in the upper spectrum of Fig. fig:comparison ) not only leaded to the detection of new extragalactic species such as SO _2 , NO and NS Martin03 , out of the more than a hundred of identified transitions, but it also represented the first step on the spectral classification of the molecular gas in the nuclei of galaxies. Such complete studies allowed the description, for the first time, of the chemistry of sulfur-bearing molecules in NGC 253 as well as the direct comparison with chemical templates within the galaxy such as that shown in Fig. fig:comparison . NGC 253 shows a molecular emission significantly comparable to that observed towards the GMCs we find in the Galactic Center region.

sources.summary: The paper detects PDR molecular tracers in NGC 253 and argues its nuclear ISM contains a substantial UV-driven photodissociation component despite shock-dominated chemistry.

quotes.quote: We report the first detection of PDR molecular tracers, namely HOC ^+ , and CO ^+ , and confirm the detection of the also PDR tracer HCO towards the starburst galaxy NGC 253, claimed to be mainly dominated by shock heating and in an earlier stage of evolution than M 82, the prototypical extragalactic PDR. The molecular abundance ratios we derive for these molecules are very similar to those found in M 82. This strongly supports the idea that these molecules are tracing the PDR component associated with the starburst in the nuclear region of NGC 253.

The presence of large abundances of PDR molecules in the ISM of NGC 253, which is dominated by shock chemistry, clearly illustrates the potential of chemical complexity studies to establish the evolutionary state of starbursts in galaxies. From this comparison we conclude that the molecular clouds in NGC 253 are more massive and with larger column densities than those in M 82, as expected from the evolutionary stage of the starbursts in both galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 253 as a nearby prototypical starburst whose GeV and TeV detections constrain cosmic rays and imply about 20% pionic losses.

quotes.quote: M82 and NGC 253 are nearby ( D 2.5 - 4.0 \ ), prototypical starburst galaxies, each having an intense star-forming region of about 200 \ radius in the center of a more quiescent disk galaxy.

Although ray emission from M82 and NGC 253 has been sought for several years with no success (at GeV, Cillis05 ; and at TeV, Aharonian05,Itoh07 ), the launch of Fermi and the advent of powerful VHE ray telescopes has led to recent detections of both starbursts at GeV energies (with Fermi ; Abdo10a ) and in VHE rays (M82 with VERITAS, Acciari09 ; NGC 253 with HESS, Acero09 ). These GeV and TeV detections constrain the cosmic ray (CR) population in these dense star-forming environments. In this paper, we discuss the implications of the ray detections of M82 and NGC 253.
We find that a fraction 0.4 and 0.2 of luminosity in CR protons is lost to pion production in M82 and NGC 253, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper reports GeV and TeV gamma-ray detections of NGC 253, enhanced gamma-ray luminosity, and H.E.S.S. localization toward its starbursting core.

quotes.quote: For the first time, steady GeV gamma-ray emission has been detected significantly by the LAT from sources positionally coincident with locations of the starburst galaxies M 82 and NGC 253 . Test statistic maps obtained with LAT for photons 200 MeV for regions around both galaxies are shown in Fig. fig:starbursts . Both starburst galaxies have also been detected at TeV energies by VERITAS and H.E.S.S. , and the emission is well explained by the interaction of CRs with local interstellar gas and radiation fields. M 82 and NGC 253, though having less gas than the Milky Way, have factors of 2-4 greater gamma-ray luminosity, suggesting a connection between active star formation and enhanced CR energy densities in star-forming galaxies. In particular, the H.E.S.S. observations of NGC 253 localise the gamma-ray emission towards the starbursting core of the galaxy, which is very much like the situation in the LMC where the gamma-ray emission is brightest towards 30 Doradus, a region that is considered as a ``mini-starburst'' .

sources.summary: The paper argues that the starburst galaxy’s GeV and TeV gamma-ray emission can include a major pulsar-wind-nebula contribution, comparing observed luminosity and supernova-rate efficiency.

quotes.quote: Recently, gamma-ray emission at TeV energies has been detected from the starburst galaxies NGC253 (Acero et al., 2009) [ 1 ] and M82 (Acciari et al., 2009 [ 2 ] . It has been claimed that pion production due to cosmic rays accelerated in supernova remnants interacting with the interstellar gas is responsible for the observed gamma rays. Here, we show that the gamma-ray pulsar wind nebulae left behind by the supernovae contribute to the TeV luminosity in a major way.

With the mean distances from the NED 3.8 Mpc for M82 and 3.1 Mpc for NGC253 the observed luminosities are L_ , M82 ( GeV )=2.2 10^ 40 \ erg \ s^ 1 and L_ , NGC253 ( GeV )=5.6 10^ 39 \ erg \ s^ 1 , implying an efficiency of 0.01 for R_ M82 =0.25  year ^ 1 and R_ NGC253 =0.07  year ^ 1 (see caption of Fig.2 for references). The values are lower than the values of 0.1 in the Ginzburg-Syrovatskii scenario for the origin of cosmic rays in the Milky Way galaxy [ 3 ] , perhaps due to adiabatic losses in the overpressured starburst region or the hotter interstellar medium compared to the Milky Way.

sources.summary: The RSR spectra show NGC 253 has the richest molecular spectrum, with nuclear starburst emission, shock-linked chemistry, and detections absent or weaker in M82.

quotes.quote: The richest spectrum is that for NGC 253, where 16 of the 33 spectral features listed in Table 2 were detected.

Comparison of M82 and NGC 253 The two galaxies with the strongest emission lines are M82 and NGC 253 which enable a comparison of emission from other molecular species, such as C _2 H, N _2 H ^+ , HNCO, CH _3 OH, HC _3 N, C _3 H _2 , SO and CH _3 C _2 H, that had only a limited number of detections in our galaxy sample as a whole. In both of these galaxies the emissions observed are likely dominated by molecular clouds associated with their nuclear starbursts. However there are differences, ala10 suggested that the starburst in M82 is more evolved and the chemistry is dominated by PDR regions, while the chemistry in the younger starburst, NGC 253, is dominated by shocks. Notable differences between these two galaxies are the detection of HNCO, CH _3 OH, N _2 H ^+ and HC _3 N in NGC 253 but not in M82, although these lines would have been readily detected if they had the same line strength relative to HCN as they have in NGC 253.

sources.summary: The paper applies the model to NGC 253, comparing previous H II/PDR treatments, observed neon gradients, assumed line-emission contributions, and CoStar-atmosphere results.

quotes.quote: To compare our results with abel2005 and the separated treatment of H II \ regions and PDRs car94 , we apply our results to NGC 253, which was also analyzed by abel2005 and car94 . M82 is observed by Herschel recently to obtain FIR continuum flux at 250, 350 and 500 \ rou2010 . We use our results for FIR continuum (Figure fig7 and fig8 ) to predict properties of dust emission in H II \ regions and PDRs, and compare it with observations. Application to NGC 253 and Comparison with abel2005 In this section, we compare our results with results of abel2005 and car94 , and discuss discrepancies. A significant gradient in [ Ne III ] 15.5 / [ Ne II ] 12.8 \ ratio is detected in NGC 253 dev2004 .

They assumed 30 of the [ C II ] 158 \ emission in NGC 253 originates in H II \ regions, and assumed no [ Si II ] 35 \ emission in the PDR modeling. They followed the model of WTH90 , using the [ C II ] 158 / [ O I ] 63 \ intensity ratio and the line to continuum ratio, ( I Si II 35 + I O I 63 + I C II 158 )/ I_ IR , to estimate G_0 and PDR density. Assuming that most [ Si II ] 35 \ emission comes from the H II \ region, car94 adopted ( I O I 63 + I C II 158 )/ I_ IR in stead of ( I Si II 35 + I O I 63 + I C II 158 )/ I_ IR used in WTH90 . G_0 increases when [ C II ] 158 / [ O I ] 63 \ intensity ratio and line to continuum ratio decrease (Figure 1 in WTH90 ). For WMBasic atmospheres, our models predict only 20 [ C II ] 158 \ arises from H II \ regions, and as much as 50 [ Si II ] 35 \ emission arises from PDR. For CoStar atmospheres, our models and suggest as much as 80 [ Si II ] 35 \ emission arises from PDR. Comparing with our predictions (Table tbl-1 ), car94 overestimated the contribution from H II \ regions to [ C II ] 158 and ignored the [ Si II ] 35 \ intensity from PDR, resulting in a lower [ C II ] 158 / [ O I ] 63 \ intensity ratio and a lower line to continuum ratio for PDR emission. Because of this underestimation of [ C II ] 158 / [ O I ] 63 \ ratio and line to continuum ratio, car94 derived a larger G_0 than predictions from our models and abel2005 . Application to M82 M82 is a nearby ( D=3.25 Mpc; tam68 ) starburst galaxy, well studied in FIR wavelength range (e.g., lor96, col99 ). In the core of M82, the active starburst region spans a diameter of 500 pc gri2001 . H II \ region diagnostics are observed at the center region of M82 bei2008 . [ Ne III ] 15.5 / [ Ne II ] 12.8 \ ratio is 0.15, and [ S IV ] 10.5 / [ S III ] 18.7 \ ratio is 0.036. Comparing observations with Figure fig3 and fig4 , we find that both ratios indicate U to be 2.5 for WMBasic atmospheres at T = 38000 K. Comparing Figure 22 of abel2005 with [ O III ] 52 / [ O III ] 88 \ ratio 1.24 col99 , we find density is to be 150 cm ^ 3 . Using this U and n (H), we derive other parameters with Figure fig5 , fig7 and fig8 in this work and Figure 16, 17, 27, 29 and 33 of abel2005 . Deduced parameters are summarized in Table tbl-2 . In M82, dust continuum emission is strong in the superwind region and the very extended emission indicates dust distribution in the halo of this galaxy eng2006 . rou2010 found that FIR flux ratios would then be a natural consequence of the dilution of the radiation field with distance from the emitting stars. The measured global flux densities are 457 2 Jy at 250 , 155 2 Jy at 350 , and 49.6 0.9 at 500 \ rou2010 . The center region of M82 has a complex structure, and the starburst emission from center region are 337, 111 and 35.4 Jy at 250, 350 and 500 , respectively rou2010 . After subtracting the starburst emission from global fluxes, the values of 250 /350 \ ratio and 250 /500 \ ratio are 2.7 and 8.5. Contributions of FIR continuum intensity from H II \ regions are similar for all three bands, at about 5 (Figure fig8 ). Compared with ratios of global emission and ratios of emission from wind and halo regions (Table tbl-2 ), the predicted FIR continuum ratios (Figure fig7 ) are more consistent with the wind and halo regions. The underestimation of 250 /350 \ ratio and 250 /500 \ ratio can be explained by the difference of dust size distribution between M82 and our assumption, since dust grains with different sizes emit FIR mission dominating FIR emission in different wavelength range. The predicted ratios of FIR continuum are more consistent with the FIR ratio for wind and halo regions suggests that size distribution of dust grains in these regions are closer to the model assumption than the center region. More detailed modeling exploration with Cloudy will be performed in the future to explain discrepancies between observations and models. The A05 model predicts diagnostic observables of an H II \ region and an associated PDR based on two external parameters: the ionization parameter U and initial total hydrogen density n (H) at the illuminated face, given the continuum shape and intensity of the ionization source, the chemical abundance of the gas, the condition of dust, and the geometry of the cloud. We explore the effect of different stellar atmospheres, discussing differences between calculation results for WMBasic and CoStar atmospheres. We presents the first set of plots of FIR ratios for Herschel bands, and contributions to these specific FIR emission from H II \ regions. [ Ne III ] 15.5 / [ Ne II ] 12.8 \ and [ S IV ] 10.5 / [ S III ] 18.7 \ ratios are sensitive to stellar atmospheres. With the same U , WMBasic atmospheres need a higher stellar temperature than CoStar atmospheres to produce the same [ Ne III ] 15.5 / [ Ne II ] 12.8 \ and [ S IV ] 10.5 / [ S III ] 18.7 \ ratios. We find that H II \ regions can dominate the 500 \ continuum emission when U>-2 and n (H) >3 cm ^ 3 , and dominate the 70 \ emission when U>-2 and n (H) <2.5 cm ^ 3 . PDR is the main origin of 110, 160, 250 and 350 \ continuum emission, although H II \ regions still contribute more than 20 emission at U>-1.5 . We apply our results to two galaxies. For NGC 253, our results for CoStar atmospheres is consistent with abel2005 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 253 as a starburst galaxy targeted by the first unbiased 2 mm line survey and chemically contrasted with M 82.

quotes.quote: The first unbiased spectral line survey was carried out with the IRAM 30m telescope towards the central region of the starburst galaxy NGC 253 and covered the 2 mm atmospheric window between 129 and 175 GHz Mart'in2006 . This scan resulted in the detection of 111 spectral features from 25 molecular species as well as three hydrogen recombination lines.

While a number of species, such as CH _3 OH, HNCO, NH _3 , SiO, NS , HOCO , and CH_2NH ,claimed to be formed in dust grains and injected into gas phase via shocks, were found to be enhanced in NGC 253, other molecules, such as CO ^+ , HCO, HOC ^+ , C_3H_2 , CH _3 CCH, and NH_2CN , mostly formed in the gas phase and likely enhanced by intense UV fields, are significantly more abundant towards M 82. In particular, line surveys allow the identification of particularly interesting and contrasted species such as the case of methanimine ( NH_2CN ) which shows rotational temperatures similar to those measured towards the Galactic hot core Sgr B2(M) in both M 82 and NGC 253, but shows an abundance enhancement towards the former. To illustrate this, the surveys towards M 82 and NGC 253 each required more than one hundred hours of telescope time at the IRAM 30m telescope.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 253 as a nearby gamma-ray starburst whose hard spectrum, TeV connection, and high flux imply enhanced cosmic-ray emission during rapid star formation.

quotes.quote: Two of the nearest starburst galaxies, M82 and NGC 253, have been detected in high-energy gamma rays by both space-based lat_2010_starburst and imaging air-Cherenkov telescopes acciari_2009_m82,acero_2009_ngc_253 . Although all galaxies are expected to produce CR-induced emission at some level, large numbers of SNRs together with dense interstellar gas (average number densities 500 cm ^ 3 ) and intense radiation fields led several authors to anticipate the central starbursts of M82 and NGC 253 as detectable gamma-ray sources e.g. paglione_1996_ngc_253,blom_1999_starburst,domingo-santamaria_2005_ngc_253,persic_2008_m82,de_cea_del_pozo_2009_m82,rephaeli_2010_ngc_253 . Indeed, the observed gamma-ray fluxes from M82 and NGC 253 imply enhanced CR energy densitities within galaxies undergoing rapid massive star-formation.

For M82 and NGC 253, the extrapolated power-law fits from the LAT energy range naturally connect with spectral measurements obtained by imaging air-Cherenkov telescopes at higher energies acciari_2009_m82,acero_2009_ngc_253,ohm_2011_ngc_253 . Whereas the power-law spectral indices of M82 and NGC 253 are 2.2--2.3 extending to TeV energies, the spectra of the LMC and SMC steepen above 2 GeV lat_2010_smc,lat_2010_lmc .

sources.summary: The paper models cosmic-ray perpendicular diffusion in nearby starburst galaxy NGC 253, using observed radio-continuum filaments, magnetic-field geometry, and tailored turbulence parameters.

quotes.quote: Radio continuum observations of the nearby starburst galaxy NGC 253 can be used to measure the distribution and transport of Cosmic Ray electrons see Heesen09a,Heesen11 . The current understanding is that Cosmic Ray electrons are accelerated in the disk by shock waves induced by supernova explosions Reynolds12 . The electrons are then transported away over their lifetime by either convection in a galactic wind or diffusion, where the two processes can be distinguished from their different typical transport length as a function of electron energy.

In the present paper we, therefore, revisit the problem of perpendicular transport of energetic particles in the nearby starbust galaxy NGC 253. We combine the approach of Shalchi10b with more realistic particle and turbulence parameters to compute the perpendicular diffusion coefficient more accurately.

sources.summary: The paper compares its GBT formaldehyde absorption profile for NGC 253 with prior single-dish and VLA work, finding extended structure and a density near 10^5 cm^-3.

quotes.quote: NGC 253: As noted in , the 4.8 and 14.5 GHz H _2 CO transitions have previously been detected in absorption Gardner1974,Baan1990 . Our GBT detection of the 1_ 10 1_ 11 transition (Figure fig:NGC253NGC660Maffei2IC342FormSpec ) is significantly different in shape from the line profile reported by Gardner1974 . Baan1997 have used the Very Large Array to image the 1_ 10 1_ 11 transition, measuring an absorption structure with FWHM size 39 12 . The peak integrated flux measured by Baan1997 of 1.72 Jy beam ^ 1 km s ^ 1 is about 60 of the integrated flux we measure, suggesting that some extended structure detected in our GBT observations is missing in these interferometric measurements. Previous CS, H _2 CO, NH _3 and high-J CO measurements Baan1990,Baan1997,Huettemeister1997,Ott2005,Guesten2006,Martin2006,Bayet2009 estimate spatial densities in the range 10^ 4-5 cm ^ 3 , which compares favorably to the 10^ 5.03 0.03 cm ^ 3 we measure (Table tab:lvg ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 253 as a starburst galaxy whose H.E.S.S. and Fermi LAT gamma-ray spectra trace non-thermal processes and cosmic-ray energy losses in its starburst nucleus.

quotes.quote: The ray observations performed with H.E.S.S. and Fermi LAT allow also to investigate the non-thermal processes in the starburst galaxy NGC 253. The ray source is compatible with the optical centre of NGC 253. The VHE ray data can be described by a power law in energy with =2.14 and differential flux normalization at 1 TeV of 9.6 10^ 14 TeV ^ 1 cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 . A power-law fit to the Fermi LAT spectrum reveals =2.24 and an integral flux between 200 MeV and 200 GeV of 4.9 10^ 9 cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 . No evidence for a spectral break or turnover is found over the dynamic range of both the LAT instrument and the H.E.S.S. experiment: a combined fit of a power law to the Fermi LAT and VHE ray data results in a =2.34 with a p-value of 30 .

sources.summary: The paper repeatedly pairs NGC 253 with M82 as a starburst whose gamma-ray observations imply significant pionic losses, cosmic-ray advection, equipartition behavior, and efficient proton stopping.

quotes.quote: The gamma-ray observations of M82 and NGC 253 indicate that CR protons lose perhaps 30 - 40 of their energy to pionic losses before escaping, implying much stronger losses than in the Milky Way ( Lacki11-Obs,Abramowski12,Ackermann12 ).
This is supported by the evidence for CR advection in M82 and NGC 253 ( Lacki11-Obs,Abramowski12 ).

When the CRs are removed by advection (as seems to be the case in M82 and NGC 253), the residence time for both the CRs and turbulence are also similar. In each case, the residence time is of order a sound-crossing time, with the CR lifetime perhaps being a few times longer than / , because the wind takes some time to accelerate to c_S and beyond. Then equipartition holds between magnetic fields and CRs, even if there is no direct coupling between the two. However, in strongly proton calorimetric galaxies, the residence time of CRs is instead set by pionic loss times that are much shorter than the sound-crossing time: the CR energy density becomes smaller than the magnetic energy density (compare with Lacki10-FRC1 ). Equipartition seems to work in M82 and NGC 253, as suggested by one-zone models ( Persic08,Rephaeli10 ) and gamma-ray observations ( Persic12,Abramowski12 ).

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 253 as a luminous inclined SBc galaxy with active star formation, a starburst nucleus, a massive central molecular zone, and a strong wind that shortens its gas-exhaustion time.

quotes.quote: NGC 253: NGC 253 is a luminous, M _B 22 SBc system with active star formation across the galaxy and especially in its starburst nucleus. We adopt D = 3.9 Mpc. The galaxy is highly inclined and the starburst CMZ also shows an inclined, disky structure (Sakamoto et al. 2011). Estimates for M _ mol range from 10 ^8  M _ from Sakamoto et al. to 3 10 ^8  M _ derived by Weiss et al. (2008) from a submillimeter dust mass determination.

The CMZ of NGC 253 supports a strong wind (e.g., Westmoquette et al. 2011), reducing the time scale to exhaust M _ mol in NGC 253 to 10 Myr, or roughly the age of the present starburst, in the absence of gas inflows.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 253 as a prototypical nuclear starburst comparison, describing its five molecular complexes, high surface densities, and lower molecular mass relative to Arp 193 structures.

quotes.quote: We can compare our computed surface densities (Table 3) with those in a prototypical nuclear starburst galaxy, NGC 253. Recent mapping by Sakamoto+11 at 20 pc resolution shows that the molecular emission from NGC 253 is concentrated in 5 molecular complexes, with typical surface densities 10^4 M _ pc ^ 2 and masses 10^7 M _ . By comparison, the molecular complexes in Arp 193 have a similar surface densities at our 90 pc resolution, although it is likely that clumping exists on smaller scales. Their masses, however, are an order of magnitude larger than those of the NGC 253 complexes, 10^8 M _ (Table arpclump ). In terms of the total molecular mass mapped, NGC 253 is also an order of magnitude lower ( 10^8 M _ ) than Arp 193 ( 10^9 M _ ).

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 253 as a nearby starburst whose hard X-ray emission is ULX-dominated, steepens above 6 keV, and shows an apparent hot-gas deficit likely affected by edge-on absorption.

quotes.quote: As a pilot program, we studied with \ and \ the nuclear region and galaxy-wide \ emission of the nearby starburst galaxy NGC 253 (Lehmer \ 2013; Wik \ 2014a). These studies showed that there was no evidence for powerful buried AGN activity in the nucleus, and that the galaxy-wide \ emission above 10 keV is dominated by a few ultraluminous \ sources (ULXs) with minority contributions from lower luminosity \ binaries that have \ colors similar to Galactic black hole binaries in intermediate accretion states. The galaxy-wide \ spectrum of NGC 253 steepens at energies above 6 keV, consistent with the spectra of other ULXs studied by \ (see, e.g., Bachetti \ 2013; Rana \ 2014; Walton \ 2013, 2014), signaling a possible dominance of super-Eddington accreting objects in the overall starburst galaxy spectra.

With the exception of NGC 253 ( orange curve ) the SFR-normalized gas components have similar normalizations around 1 keV, consistent with previous studies that have found a direct scaling of the hot gas emission with SFR (see, e.g., Mineo \ 2012b). Compared with the other three galaxies, NGC 253 is relatively edge-on ( i 80  deg), and therefore absorption is likely playing a role in the apparent deficit of hot gas emission in the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 253 radio and gamma-ray spectra, finding high magnetic field strengths, lower acceleration efficiency than M82, cosmic-ray energy density, and non-equipartition behavior.

quotes.quote: Starburst Galaxies: M82, NGC 253, Arp 220 Using the updated version of the YEGZ models, we have re-worked our analysis of the radio and ray spectra for M82 and NGC 253 and have also applied the models to the radio spectrum for the nuclei of Arp 220. Our previous results for M82 and NGC 253 are consistent with the newer models, where the best-fitting magnetic field strengths range from 200--250 G (NGC 253) and 250--350 G (M82) YoastHull13,YoastHull14 .

Though the observed ray spectra for M82 and NGC 253 are quite similar, we find that a slightly larger acceleration efficiency ( = 0.2 ; or equivalently a somewhat higher supernova rate) is required for M82 and a slightly smaller acceleration efficiency ( = 0.05 ) is necessary to fit NGC 253. Thus, for M82 U_ CR ranges from 420 to 620 eV cm ^ 3 and from 260 to 350 eV cm ^ 3 for NGC 253 (see Table 2, Figure 2).
Taking the ratio of U_ B to U_ CR from our models, M82 diverges from equipartition by a factor of 2.5 to 7 and NGC 253 by a factor of 2.9 to 5.9 (see Figure 2, Table 2).

sources.summary: The paper presents a Spitzer IRAC mid-infrared point-source catalog for NGC 253, reporting 9001 detected sources and common completeness limits corresponding to sources brighter than M_ 3.6 = -10.

quotes.quote: We present Spitzer IRAC mid-infrared point source catalogs for mosaics covering the fields of the nearby ( 4 Mpc) galaxies NGC 55, NGC 253, NGC 2366, NGC 4214, and NGC 5253. We detect a total of 20159 sources in these five fields. The individual galaxy point source breakdown is the following: NGC 55, 8746 sources; NGC 253, 9001 sources; NGC 2366, 505 sources; NGC 4214, 1185 sources; NGC 5253, 722 sources. The completeness limits of the full catalog vary with bandpass and were found to be m_ 3.6 =18.0 , m_ 4.5 =17.5 , m_ 5.8 =17.0 , and m_ 8.0 =16.5 mag. For all galaxies, this corresponds to detection of point sources brighter than M_ 3.6 =-10 .

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 253 as a gamma-ray bright star-forming galaxy with 7.00 significance after background modeling, and includes it among the four brightest systems used to derive the average spectrum.

quotes.quote: Our analysis confirms previous results indicating that the systems NGC 253, NGC 1068, NGC 3034 and NGC 4945 are ray bright SFGs. Utilizing the distribution of P _ bg to translate the likelihood function of each point source the statistical significance of the source (taking into account the possibility of background mismodeling), we can determine that the ray emission observed from each SFG is statistically significant at confidence levels of: 7.00 (NGC 253), 4.42 (NGC 1068), 8.06 (NGC 3034), and 7.10 (NGC 4945). Because the distribution P _ bg takes into account the possibility of background fluctuations, we note that all of these sources are highly significant.

Using the four brightest systems in our analysis (NGC 253, NGC 1068, NGC 3034 and NGC 4945), we calculate the average ray spectrum above 1 GeV to follow a power-law with a spectral index of = -2.32 0.06. These four systems are all individually best-fit with power-law spectra between -2.2 and -2.4.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 253 as a barred spiral with narrow 36.2 GHz methanol masers, likely tied to bar/CMZ gas, molecular-line behavior, shocks, and enhanced HNCO:SiO.

quotes.quote: The extragalactic 36.2 GHz emission observed from NGC 253 shares this property 10 km s ^ 1 ; Ellingsen+14 , however, the megamaser emission towards Arp 220 is significantly more broad Chen+15 .

The two regions of methanol maser emission in NGC 253 have recently been suggested as being associated with the inner interface of the galactic bar and CMZ Ellingsen+submitted . Like NGC 253, NGC 4945 is also classified as a barred spiral galaxy, however, in comparison to NGC253, the bar dynamics and molecular emission in NGC4945 are relatively poorly studied, due to a combination of a more edge-on orientation and a more southern declination.

Observations of 36.2-GHz methanol masers in NGC 253 and Arp 220 suggest that large-scale, low-velocity shocks are important, likely because they provide an efficient mechanism for releasing methanol from dust-grain mantles without dissociating it in the process. Once released into the gas phase, methanol is depleted relatively quickly through a combination of gas-phase chemistry and/or dissociation from shocks or external radiation fields. We suggest that the combination of cold molecular gas and low-velocity shocks (both on large-scales) are the key requirements for the presence of a luminous class I methanol maser emission, such as is observed in NGC 4945. Under this hypothesis, the absence of other sites of 36.2 GHz methanol masers in NGC 4945 is likely because other regions where there are low-velocity shocks do not also contain sufficient cold molecular gas. In NGC 253 the regions where the class I methanol maser emission is observed show an enhancement in HNCO:SiO ratio Meier+15, Ellingsen+submitted .

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC253 with M82, citing its helical central outflow field and noting similar star-forming nucleus properties despite lower star-formation rate.

quotes.quote: The magnetic field morphology is similar to the one in other nearby starburst galaxies (NGC1569, NGC253) with possible large-scale magnetic loops in the halo and a helical magnetic field inside the outflow cones.

2011A&A...535A..79H already detected a helical magnetic field in the innermost outflow region of the starbursting galaxy NGC253. One of the signs for such a configuration is the rapid switch of the sign of the RMs along this structure, which is also visible in our map (Fig. image_RM__ ).

A helical field morphology in the central starburst region of a galaxy was also observed for NGC 253 2011A&A...535A..79H .
