In Fig. \ 5 we show the UHECR fluxes expected from a single continuous source (which is valid for a GRB model because of the large number of GRBs from these starburst galaxies within the last 100 Myrs) at a distance from us.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 253's infrared SED with silicate absorption, AGN-like dust reradiation, and aperture-dependent optical/UV components requiring both AGN and starburst models.

quotes.quote: A deep feature at 10 \ indicates strong absorption by silicates, particularly for in NGC 253, IC 342, M 82, NGC 5236, and NGC 5253. For NGC 5236 and NGC 7552 there is also evidence of PAH emission in the mid-IR.

Fig. 5 shows that the IR reradiation by dust of NGC 253, IC 342, NGC 3256, NGC 3690, NGC 5236, and NGC 7552 can be also modelled by a unique model corresponding to a AGN with \ = 1000 , 0 \ = 1000 3, and a power-law flux with log \ = 11 (model m7) as was found in Arp 220 (Contini et al. 2004, Fig. A1).

Consider for instance NGC 253, besides the good agreement to the small aperture data by the AGN model in the optical range and by the low velocity model (m1) in the UV, we must account also for the data observed with large apertures which are explained by a starburst model (m4).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 253 as a starburst case where HNCO traces shock chemistry, but its HNCO abundance falls in the PDR-like range, implying UV affects part of its molecular gas.

quotes.quote: The results from this work point out that the relative abundance of HNCO seems to be one of the best discriminators between the chemistry driven by low velocity shocks found in SB like NGC253 and that driven by photodissociation due to the UV radiation from stellar clusters or AGNs.

Surprisingly, the early state starbursts in NGC 253, assumed to be mainly dominated by shocks Martin03,Martin05,Martin06b , shows abundances of HNCO within the range of the PDR region of the diagram, which suggest that a strong UV field is also affecting an important fraction of its molecular gas.

A similarly large variation in the HNCO abundance is observed with sources such as M 82 and NGC 253 showing abundances similar to the galactic PDRs while, on the other end, Maffei 2 appears to be more similar to the Galactic center GMCs.

sources.summary: The paper reports HCN(3-2) observations of NGC0253, treating it as the lone nearby mapped galaxy, computing its line luminosity with the mapped-galaxy formula, and sampling it along both axes.

quotes.quote: Our sample includes 30 galaxies and covers a broad range of IR luminosities: L_ IR 10^ 10.2 10^ 12.5 L_ . Central positions of all galaxies were observed in HCN (3-2). One nearby galaxy ( D < 7 Mpc) was mapped (NGC0253).

The integrated intensity of HCN (3-2) emission was calculated using velocity intervals based on the HCN (1-0) line profiles. We computed the HCN line luminosity using Equation 1 in gao04b for objects further than 7 Mpc away and using their Equation 4 for NGC0253, the nearby galaxy which we mapped. Our mapping strategy involved 5 pointings along the major axis and 3 along the minor axis, each separated by 15 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 253 as the nearest starburst with current VHE upper limits, expected gamma-ray detectability with better sensitivity, and detailed models testable by existing instruments.

quotes.quote: For the latter, we currently have only upper limits for the nearest starburst (NGC253, Aharonian et al. 2005c) and the nearest ultra-luminous infrared galaxy (Arp 220, the place in the universe with the highest supernova explosion rate known, Albert et al. 2007c). These galaxies are expected to appear in gamma-rays provided enough sensitivity is achieved. Detailed models have been constructed for NGC 253 which could be tested already with the current instruments (e.g., Domingo-Santamaria Torres 2005); others, like for instance the case of Arp 220 (Torres 2004) require yet further sensitivity.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 253 as the nearest 1LAC AGN and a starburst galaxy in the seven nearby gamma-ray loud AGNs associated with UHECR directions.

quotes.quote: We identify 7 `` ray loud'' AGNs which are associated with UHECRs within 17^ and are likely candidates for the production sites of UHECRs: Centaurus A, NGC 4945, ESO 323-G77, 4C+04.77, NGC 1218, RX J0008.0+1450 and NGC 253.

Only 9 AGNs remain, including the radio galaxies Centaurus A (the nearest radio galaxy) and M87, the Seyfert 2 NGC 4945, the Seyfert 1 ESO 323-G77 and the starburst galaxy NGC 253 (as previously noted by dermer10 ).

Within the distance _1 , 4 PAO events correlate with 3 AGNs: NGC 4945, Centaurus A and 4C+04.77; at _2 , 7 PAO events correlate with 4 AGNs: NGC 4945, Centaurus A, ESO 323-G77 and 4C+04.77; at _3 , 17 events are associated with 7 AGNs: NGC 4945, Centaurus A, ESO 323-G77, 4C+04.77, NGC 253, NGC 1218 and RXJ0008.0+1450.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 253 as a warped Sculptor-group galaxy with ionized gas beyond its H I disk, a possibly declining rotation curve, and outer-disk ionization linked to central young stars.

quotes.quote: To test this theory, they obtained very deep optical spectra for one of the brightest Sculptor group galaxies, NGC 253, and succeeded in detecting ionized gas (H and [ N ii ] ) beyond the H i disc. The rotation curve they derived also showed signs of a possible decline. However, their results led them to conclude that the existence of this extended ionized gas was not due to the ambient radiation field, but more likely due to hot young stars in the central regions of the galaxy ionizing the outer disc through a strong warp present in the galaxy's disc.

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 253 as the sample's brightest radio halo, with strong star formation and a cosmic-ray bulk velocity consistent with a galactic wind.

quotes.quote: We stress that our sample includes the brightest halo observed so far, NGC 253, with strong star formation, as well as one of the weakest halos, NGC 4565, with weak star formation. For NGC 253 Heesen et al. (2009) argued that the synchrotron lifetime (which is B_t ^ 2 ) mainly determines the vertical scale height of the synchrotron emission and estimated the cosmic ray bulk velocity in NGC 253 to 300 30 km/s. As this is similar to the escape velocity, it shows the presence of a galactic wind in this galaxy and implies that the galactic wind velocity is proportional to B_t ^2 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 253 as a Fermi and TeV-detected starburst whose propagation environment resembles M82, while noting much of its radio and infrared emission lies outside the core.

quotes.quote: EGRET only detected the Milky Way and the LMC Sreekumar92,Abdo10c , but Fermi has also detected the SMC Abdo10d , M31 Abdo10e , and the starburst galaxies M82 and NGC 253 Abdo10f , as well as the starburst-Seyferts NGC 4945, NGC 1068, and the Circinus Galaxy Abdo10g,Lenain10,Ackermann12,Hayashida13 . The TeV telescopes VERITAS (Very Energetic Radiation Imaging Telescope Array System) and HESS (High Energy Stereoscopic System) have also detected M82 and NGC 253 Acciari09,Acero09,Abramowski12 .

Models of NGC 253, the other TeV-detected starburst, generally find similar propagation environments as M82 Domingo05,Rephaeli09,Paglione12,YoastHull13 ; furthermore, a substantial fraction of its radio and infrared emission comes from outside the starburst core Melo02,Williams10 .

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 253 as a nearby TeV-emitting starburst galaxy confirmed in gamma rays, with high compact star formation that can energize cosmic rays.

quotes.quote: M82 and NGC 253 are TeV gamma-ray emitting starburst galaxies (Acciari et al. 2009, Acero et al. 2009), both at a distance of 3 Mpc (see Fig. fig:starburst ). confirmed the gamma-ray emission in those objects and increased the sample of starburst galaxies by two more objects (NGC 4945, NGC 1068) although those are composite starburst/Seyfert 2 systems, which makes it challenging to disentangle the pure starburst component (Lenain et al. 2010). Their star formation rate (in a compact region) is larger than that of the Milky Way.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 253's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 253 is an edge-on spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 3.2 0.6 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) with an active nucleus dominated by a powerful starburst engelbracht_nuclear_1998 , which according to the optical classification, is a starburst/LINER transition nucleus. Narrow-line emission tadhunter_optical_1993 , a compact non-thermal radio source, TH2 turner_1_1985 , and an obscured compact X-ray source, X-1 weaver_chandra_2002 , are detected in the nucleus, suggesting the presence of an obscured AGN (see also mohan_very_2002 ). On the other hand, no compact nuclear source could be detected with VLBI in radio brunthaler_evidence_2009 or with subarcsecond MIR imaging fernandez-ontiveros_nucleus_2009 .

sources.summary: The paper reports NH3 measurements toward NGC 253, characterizing its high-temperature starburst environment and two velocity components at 180 and 300 km/s.

quotes.quote: Our 30^ spatial resolution measurements of the para-NH _3 (1,1), (2,2), and (4,4) transitions and the ortho-NH _3 (3,3) and (6,6) transitions toward NGC 253 are in good agreement with many of the aspects of these previous studies.
The addition of the (5,5), (7,7), (8,8), and (9,9) transitions allows us to better characterize the high temperature environment in this starburst galaxy.
For both NH _3 and HC _3 N we measure velocity components at 180 and 300 km s ^ 1 , consistent with the NE and SW components noted previously.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 253 as a starburst nucleus whose observed water-ion spectra combine emission and absorption and show a P Cygni-type 971 GHz velocity profile.

quotes.quote: NGC 253 is a starburst nucleus with \ = 1.7-2.1 10 \ depending on the adopted distance (2.6--3.5 Mpc) and an SED that peaks in the optical.
The spectrum of NGC 253 shows a combination of emission and absorption for both lines, although the central velocities of \ and \ differ by 21--56 .
The \ velocity profiles are similar to those of \ and , with a double absorption toward NGC 4945 and a P Cygni-type line profile toward NGC 253.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC253 as a starburst HAGS prototype, adopting its gamma-ray luminosity and hard high-energy spectrum to benchmark diffuse neutrino models.

quotes.quote: The neutrino and ray spectrum from these objects is expected to be a power law, whose spectral index remains uncertain in the relevant energy range (above 50 GeV), since only a few members of the broad HAGS source class (NGC253, M82) have been detected up to very high energies.

We will therefore consider the spectral index as a free parameter in the present work, keeping in mind that the observation of NGC253 suggests an E^ 2.15 0.10 above 50 GeV, which is the energy region relevant for our purpose (see Fig. fig:ngc ).

sources.summary: The paper models NGC253 as a nearby starburst galaxy whose Fermi-LAT gamma-ray observations are reproduced under different diffusion models and whose neutrino flux may be detectable in optimistic cases.

quotes.quote: Adopting different models for the transport of CRPs, we estimate the ray and neutrino emissions due to pp collisions from nearby SBGs such as M82, NGC253, and Arp220.
The Fermi-LAT ray observations, especially those of M82 and NGC253, are reproduced regardless of the diffusion model.
We suggest that M82 and NGC253 might be detectable as point sources of high-energy neutrinos in the upcoming KM3NET and IceCube-Gen2, when optimistic models are applied.

sources.summary: The paper compares simulations to NGC 253 observations, saying its global CO/[C I] ratio matches high cosmic-ray, high-FUV, metal-rich models and aligns with its starburst environment and estimated metallicity.

quotes.quote: Recent observations by Krip16 in NGC 253, found a global ratio of the order of R^ CO10 _ CI10 2.5-10 , which agrees with our simulations for higher _ CR , higher G_0 and high metallicities, Z>1 Z _ for which both clouds match the observations. This is in line with the Krip16 suggestion that in NGC 253, which is a starburst galaxy therefore having a different and more energetic ISM environment than the local one, shocks and their associated radiation and/or an enhancement of cosmic-ray energy densities may explain such R^ CO10 _ CI10 values. It is, furthermore, in agreement with Gall08 who estimate a metallicity of 2.19 Z _ for NGC 253.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 253 as a nearby starburst comparison object with multi-temperature outflow gas and future gamma-ray observations to constrain wind models.

quotes.quote: in the nearby starburst galaxies Arp 220 e.g. Varenius2016AA, Barcos2018ApJ , M82 e.g. Shopbell1998ApJ, Bland1988Natur and NGC 253 e.g.
NGC 253 and M82 generally have several ionised gas components with different thermal temperatures, which is evident in the multi-temperature fits to their X-ray spectra.

Measuring the gamma-ray glow in external galaxies is one of the science themes of the Cherenkov Telescope Array (CTA) 2019sctabook , and future observations of nearby starburst galaxies, such as the identified CTA targets, NGC 253 and M82, will provide us with an empirical means to model the efficiency of energy and momentum deposition of CRs into galactic outflows.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 253 as the closest starburst source, normalizes modeled source fluxes to it, and finds it dominates SBG contributions under AstroR fields.

quotes.quote: NGC 253, the closest source, has its flux arbitrarily set to one.
NGC 253, the closest source, has its value arbitrarily set to one when the AstroR EGMF is used.
In this case, the closer sources have a more important contribution, being dominated by NGC 253 among the SBGs, and by Cen A among the AGNs.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 253 as a moderate starburst case, placing it near full calorimetry and estimating cosmic-ray ionization roughly ten times the Milky Way level.

quotes.quote: We find that weakly star-forming galaxies like the Milky Way and Andromeda sit at 10 of calorimetry, while starbursts such as NGC 253 and NGC 2146 sit near 100 calorimetry.
For moderate starbursts such as NGC 253 or M82, the enhancement compared to the Milky Way is roughly an order of magnitude, while for the most extreme starbursts such as Arp 220 it is at most 3 dex.

Ionisation rates in moderate starbursts such as NGC 253 or M82 are likely a factor of 10 above that in the Milky Way, while those in the most extreme starbursts such as Arp 220 can reach a few hundred times Milky Way values.

sources.summary: The paper lists NGC 253 as a nearby SBG source candidate and finds that removing it reduces the arrival-direction test statistic by about 4-5.

quotes.quote: The most important source candidates from the SBG catalog are NGC 4945, NGC 253, M83, and M82 (outside of the Observatory's exposure), which are all nearby at around 3-5 Mpc.

It is important, however, to note that the energy dependency of the test statistic is subject to large statistical fluctuations , so especially the less pronounced peak at =19.3 could just be a statistical fluctuation. The energy dependency of the arrival direction test statistic reveals another interesting finding, which is that the SBG model consistently describes the measured arrival directions better than the Centaurus A model in the energy range =19.4-19.6 . For these energies, the modeled arrival direction pdfs ( fig:ADs ) are very similar for both models in the region around Centaurus A / NGC 4945, but differ in the southern region around NGC 253. From tab:TS it can be concluded that 20 of the total arrival directions test statistic for the SBG model originates from the Centaurus A / NGC 4945 region. By removing NGC 253 from the catalog we explicitly checked that that source contributes around 4 to 5 to the arrival directions test statistic.

sources.summary: The paper finds 12 RSGs and one sgB[e] source in NGC 253, with metallicity consistent with M-type RSGs and stronger H II-region confusion than NGC 247.

quotes.quote: NGC 253 We identified 12 RSGs and one sgB [ e ] , NGC 253-739 Maravelias2023 .
The metallicity of NGC 253 (0.72 ) is consistent with finding mainly M-type RSGs.
The confusion by H ii regions was more extreme in NGC 253 (38 ), compared to NGC 247 (20 ), which is a similarly distant and inclined galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 253 as an OH megamaser comparison source, finding one ISO spectrum covering 53.3 microns and a peculiar broad blue absorption tail.

quotes.quote: For the purpose of comparison, the ISO LWS spectra of two OH megamasers, Arp 220 and NGC 253 are also processed.
Totally 3 ISO LWS spectra for Arp 220 and 1 spectrum for NGC 253 are found covering the 53.3 m region (see Table sptable ).
The spectral profile of NGC 253 is peculiar, for it shows a very broad absorption tail at the blue side of the line, but it is included in the ` GCmega ' group for the convenience of description.

sources.summary: The paper finds sulfur-bearing molecules in NGC 253's nucleus have abundances and low rotational temperatures consistent with large-scale low-velocity shocks driving its molecular chemistry.

quotes.quote: Our study of the sulfur chemistry towards the nuclear region of NGC 253 seems to indicate a chemistry similar to that of the envelope of SGR B2 where low velocity shocks are thought to be the main heating mechanism of the molecular material Flower,Pintado97,Pintado01 . The low rotational temperatures derived for NGC 253 are also in agreement with those derived by Cummins in the SGR B2 envelope. These results support the idea of large scale low velocity shocks driving the chemistry of the inner molecular material in the nucleus of NGC 253 which is consistent with the high rotational temperatures derived from NH _3 and the large observed SiO abundances Burillo00,Mauers03 .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 253 as a nearby starburst galaxy whose supernova rate, stellar winds, and cosmic-ray interactions make GeV and TeV emission potentially detectable.

quotes.quote: The enhanced supernova rate in starburst galaxies such as M82 and NGC 253 at 3 Mpc improve the prospects that they could be detectable with GLAST and ground-based Cherenkov telescopes . In the inner starburst regions of these sources, interaction of cosmic rays with the strong stellar winds would produce GeV and TeV radiation . These processes will also generate neutrinos, though it is unlikely that they will be detected with IceCube or a Northern Hemisphere km-scale neutrino telescope, as these detectors have a sensitivity comparable to EGRET in terms of fluence A fuller discussion of neutrino sources will be given in my Madison proceedings for TeV/Particle Astrophysics II. .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 253 as a starburst galaxy whose observed infrared line ratios are reproduced with multi-cloud calculations involving low and high density-velocity clouds.

quotes.quote: However, previous investigations on the physical conditions in the ISM of NGC 253 , NGC 3256 , and M 82 found relatively low electronic densities on the basis of the [ ] 33 m/18 m and [ ] 88 m/52 m line ratios, and low velocities derived from the FWHM of the IR line profiles. Multi-cloud models Many different conditions coexist in the ISM of single galaxies. Therefore, we performed multi-cloud model calculations to reproduce all the observed IR line ratios available in the literature for the starburst galaxies NGC 253, NGC 3256, and M 82.
